title: EHA Golfview Eldercare Mansion (Johor Jaya)
care_types: Nursing Home + Day Care + Assisted Living + Rehabilitation
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
EHA Golfview Eldercare Mansion is the Johor Jaya branch of EHA Eldercare Group, sited unusually within the grounds of Daiman 18 Golf Club at 85, Jalan Pesona, Taman Johor Jaya, 81100 Johor Bahru. Phone lines are 019-728 3697 and 019-772 3697, with email at ask.ehagroup@gmail.com and visiting hours 9am-6pm daily. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents.

Golfview's published profile emphasises landscaped grounds and lounge space inside the golf-club premises, on-site meal preparation tailored to individual dietary needs, and a 24/7 caregiving roster. Group-level services that apply across branches include long-term assisted living, respite stays of one to six months, day care, physiotherapy, dementia care, and skilled nursing procedures. The branch is associated with post-stroke rehabilitation in third-party listings, but the EHA website itself does not publish stroke-specific protocols, therapist staffing, or rehab equipment lists, so this should be verified on visit. Bed count and pricing are not posted publicly.

For families looking at Golfview, the practical checklist is the same regardless of the marketing: ask for the JKM licence on viewing, request a written fee schedule with all add-ons spelled out, and clarify medical capability item by item — PEG feeds, suction, oxygen concentrator, wound dressings, urinary catheter changes, and what is referred out. If post-stroke rehab is the reason for choosing this branch, ask how often a physiotherapist attends in person, who supervises home-programme exercises on non-therapist days, and what outcome measures (transfers, gait, ADL) the home tracks. Hospital Sultan Ismail is the nearest tertiary centre.
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, activities, dementia_care

title: Master Care Centre
longitude: Master Care Centre is a nursing home located in Johor Bahru, Johor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 21 reviews.
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 7 PM
editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre (MCC) is a multi-branch private nursing care group headquartered in Johor Bahru with over a decade of operation. The branch listed here — MCC@Yahya at Jalan Hassan Al-Attas, Bandar Johor Bahru — serves as the group's original base, focused on nursing care, post-surgery recovery, and stroke rehabilitation for elderly residents. MCC positions itself as a clinically oriented care provider rather than a simple old folks home, and its service mix reflects that: skilled nursing, physiotherapy-linked rehabilitation, long-term residential care, and memory care support for residents with dementia or Alzheimer's are all offered across the group's locations.

With more than ten years in the market and a Google rating of 4.8 from its reviewer base, MCC@Yahya carries a strong track record in elder care circles. The group runs three JB branches — Yahya (Bandar JB), Abad (Taman Abad), and Daya (Taman Daya) — sharing the same management team, clinical standards, and contact number (+60 11-1080 1805), which allows families to enquire about placement across all sites simultaneously. The clinical emphasis on post-surgical recovery and stroke rehab makes this branch particularly relevant for elders transitioning out of hospital.

Bed count and pricing are not published online, so prospective families will need to call or visit to assess current availability and monthly fees. The shared phone number across all three branches means enquiries are handled centrally — it is worth specifying which location you are enquiring about. Questions to ask include the nurse-to-resident ratio on night shifts, whether the facility can manage PEG feeding or oxygen dependency, and what the process is for residents whose care needs escalate after admission.

title: Rebina House Care Centre
visiting_hours: Monday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 8 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care (養身之家) has been running from Taman Kebun Teh in Johor Bahru since 2003, making it one of the older family-owned nursing homes in the city. The operation also runs Sunrise Homecare, a separate home-care service, so the same family has visibility into both residential and at-home elder care in JB. Communication is mostly in Mandarin and English.

Published service scope covers elderly nursing care, palliative and hospice care, with the family emphasising a homely rather than institutional setup. The website does not publish capacity, room configurations or detailed clinical capabilities (no specifics on dementia unit, doctor-visit cadence, or feeding-tube management). Google reviews are positive but limited in number — 4.8 stars across 17 reviews, consistent with a smaller home where most enquiries come by referral.

Families looking for a long-running, smaller-scale Mandarin-speaking home rather than a large purpose-built facility will find Rebina worth a visit. Continuity of ownership for more than two decades is a meaningful operational fact in a market where many private homes turn over within five years, and the parallel home-care arm means the family can sometimes step a relative down to at-home care if the residential placement no longer fits. Worth asking on the visit: licence number, current bed count, how many residents per caregiver on day and night shifts, what palliative actually means in practice (syringe driver capability, after-hours doctor access), how end-of-life symptom control is delivered overnight, and whether the home will accept a resident with advanced dementia or behavioural symptoms. Visit during a weekday afternoon to see staffing rather than the manager-led tour, and ask to speak to a current resident's family if any are visiting that day.
services: nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, activities, palliative

title: EHA Lakeview Eldercare Mansion (Permas Branch)
care_types: Nursing Home + Day Care + Assisted Living + Rehabilitation
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 12 AM; Sunday: 12 to 6 AM, 9 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
EHA Lakeview Eldercare Mansion is the Permas Jaya branch of EHA Eldercare Group, sitting lakeside in Taman Bayu Puteri on the eastern side of Johor Bahru. The branch has been promoted by EHA as a "Most Beautiful Elderly Care Mansion" — an internal label that aligns with reviewer comments about the lake-facing aspect and landscaped grounds. Rated 4.9 stars across 40 Google reviews, it sits alongside Golfview as one of the group's higher-reviewed Johor branches. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents — making it one of the more established private eldercare chains across Johor and the Klang Valley.

The group's website lists eight published service categories that apply across branches: medical support, nutrition and diet, rehabilitation by physiotherapy, social activities, caregiver training and support, skilled nurses, special nursing procedures, and a traditional Chinese medicine approach (acupuncture and herbal). Pricing is published on EHA's website (ehaeldercare.com.my/our-packages/) and is quoted as a starting point — actual fees vary with the resident's care needs. For the Johor Bahru and Klang Valley branches (which include this one), assisted senior living starts at RM 3,200/month for 24-hour residential care across able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents; respite stays of one to six months start at RM 120/day; and day care, available 11 hours a day across five days a week with flexible timing, starts at RM 1,500/month. The Kluang branch is priced lower (assisted from RM 2,300/month, respite from RM 80/day, day care from RM 1,300/month) — useful context if the family can travel and cost is a major factor. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Permas Jaya gives Lakeview good access to private hospitals and commercial services on the eastern JB corridor, which makes it a convenient choice for families visiting frequently from Pasir Gudang, Permas, or Tebrau. The lakeside setting and gardens are clearly part of the marketing pitch; on a viewing it's worth checking how often residents actually use the lake-facing communal space versus their rooms, asking how many caregivers are on the overnight roster, and confirming whether the published "starting from" rate matches the level of care your family member needs. Hospital Sultan Ismail is the nearest tertiary centre.
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, activities, dementia_care

title: EHA Parkview ElderCare (Perling)

editorial_summary:
EHA Parkview Eldercare is the Perling branch of EHA Eldercare Group, in western Johor Bahru on the Skudai corridor. With 85 Google reviews at a 4.9-star average, Parkview has the largest review base of any branch in the EHA network — a meaningful signal of sustained operating history rather than a newly-opened facility. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents — making it one of the more established private eldercare chains across Johor and the Klang Valley.

The group's website lists eight published service categories that apply across branches: medical support, nutrition and diet, rehabilitation by physiotherapy, social activities, caregiver training and support, skilled nurses, special nursing procedures, and a traditional Chinese medicine approach (acupuncture and herbal). Pricing is published on EHA's website (ehaeldercare.com.my/our-packages/) and is quoted as a starting point — actual fees vary with the resident's care needs. For the Johor Bahru and Klang Valley branches (which include this one), assisted senior living starts at RM 3,200/month for 24-hour residential care across able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents; respite stays of one to six months start at RM 120/day; and day care, available 11 hours a day across five days a week with flexible timing, starts at RM 1,500/month. The Kluang branch is priced lower (assisted from RM 2,300/month, respite from RM 80/day, day care from RM 1,300/month) — useful context if the family can travel and cost is a major factor. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Perling sits well-connected to the JB-Skudai highway and the wider Iskandar Puteri area, which suits families visiting from Bukit Indah, Tampoi, or further west. With Parkview's longer track record, the useful viewing questions are less about whether the facility runs and more about fit: ask how many residents are in each room, whether RN cover is on-site overnight (versus on-call), how often a physiotherapist attends in person, and what specific clinical procedures the home performs in-house versus refers out (PEG feeds, suction, oxygen, wound dressings, urinary catheter changes). Confirm that the "starting from" rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure. Hospital Sultanah Aminah is the nearest government tertiary centre; KPJ Puteri Specialist Hospital is the nearest private option.

title: Loving Mansion Care Centre
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Loving Mansion Care Centre occupies a cluster of shoplots on Jalan Kota 2/3 in Kawasan Perindustrial Kota Puteri, Masai, about 15 minutes from JB CIQ. Registered as CT0113053-M, the operator reports more than 20 years in elderly care, with day-to-day operations led by a manager named Alan who is repeatedly mentioned by name in family reviews. The home is bilingual in practice (English and Mandarin) and serves a predominantly Chinese-Malaysian resident base.

Services listed include assisted living, full nursing care, palliative care, dementia and memory care, and stroke rehabilitation. Two complementary therapies are offered on-site that are unusual for the segment: acupuncture and physiotherapy, presented as part of the routine care plan rather than paid add-ons. Residents receive 24-hour nursing coverage, home-cooked meals and a daily activities programme. Google reviews (5★ across 17 ratings) consistently flag the cleanliness of the premises and professionalism of the nursing team.

Loving Mansion is well-suited to families wanting nursing-grade care with built-in rehab and TCM-style therapy in one place — stroke recovery and chronic-pain elderly residents in particular benefit from the acupuncture/physio combination. The operator does not publish bed count, pricing, RN-to-resident ratio, or licence type on the website, so confirm capacity, monthly rates by room type, and whether acupuncture is delivered by a registered TCM practitioner before committing.
services: Geriatric/Orthopedic/Neurology Physio, 24/7 nursing

title: ECON Medicare Centre & Nursing Home - Taman Perling Branch
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed
editorial_summary:
ECON Medicare Centre & Nursing Home – Taman Perling Branch stands apart from other private nursing homes in Johor Bahru by virtue of its corporate pedigree and scale. Opened in April 2013, this purpose-built 199-bed, four-storey facility (57,000 sq ft) is operated by ECON Healthcare Group — the largest private nursing home operator in Singapore and Malaysia by revenue, with 36 years of eldercare experience. Located approximately 45 minutes from the Causeway, it is the primary cross-border option for Singaporean families seeking significantly lower-cost nursing home care without sacrificing institutional quality standards. In mid-2025, US private equity firm TPG completed an S$87.8 million acquisition and delisted ECON from SGX; institutional-grade governance continues under the ECON brand.

ECON Taman Perling's clinical offering is broad for a Malaysian nursing home: skilled nursing care, post-stroke rehabilitation, dementia and memory care, palliative care, Parkinson's and cancer care, physiotherapy, occupational therapy, and wound dressing are all confirmed. The group's distinctive 'East-meets-West' model integrates Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) including acupuncture alongside Western nursing protocols. The facility operates 24 hours, 7 days a week with Registered Nurses, physiotherapists, occupational therapists, and paramedics confirmed on staff. A group-wide Malaysia fee range of RM 1,950–6,600/month was disclosed in the 2021 IPO prospectus; room-type-specific pricing requires direct inquiry.

Visiting hours are 10am–7pm daily with a maximum of two visitors at a time; children under 12 are restricted from wards but may visit in the designated family area. The facility is not listed on AGECOPE or nursinghome.my directories; JKM licence details require direct verification. With 152 Google reviews averaging 4.5 stars — the highest review count in this dataset — families consistently praise nursing attentiveness, cleanliness, staff professionalism, and activity programming.
services: nursing_care, rehab, social_activities

title: Spring Valley HomeCare (Nursing Home)
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Spring Valley Homecare (J.B.) Sdn. Bhd. operates from Lot 3456, Jalan Lurah Utama, Kempas Baru — a stretch of Johor Bahru that has become a small cluster of private nursing homes. The company has Singapore links: the director, Mr Frankie Ker, runs the parent operation from Singapore, while local manager Ms Ada Teo handles day-to-day enquiries on +60 19-754 6410. The Mandarin signage (新山安老院) and trilingual website point to a primarily Chinese-speaking resident base.

The public site frames Spring Valley as an elderly care centre rather than a hospital-grade nursing facility. Specific capacity, staffing ratios, and clinical scope (dementia, palliative, tube care) are not published on the homepage. Languages used in the operation include Chinese, Malay and English. The Google rating sits at 4.2 across 38 reviews — solid for the Kempas cluster but a small sample.

For Mandarin-speaking families wanting a JB centre with cross-border Singapore management, this is a reasonable visit, especially where a relative is being moved south of the causeway for proximity. The cluster context matters too: Kempas Baru has several private homes within walking distance of each other, so it is worth visiting two or three on the same trip rather than committing on a single tour. Things to confirm on the visit: the JKM licence, whether a doctor visits on a schedule or on-call only, the night-shift nurse count, and what the published rate covers (diapers, feeds and physio sessions are commonly billed extra). Ask whether the home accepts residents on PEG feeds or with stage 3+ pressure ulcers — those answers tell you what the operation can and cannot manage. Also ask how the cross-border management arrangement plays out in practice: who is the on-the-ground decision-maker if a resident's condition changes overnight, and how quickly clinical decisions can be made without referring back to the Singapore office.
services: laundry, physio, activities

title: Woon Ho Family Care Centre
visiting_hours: Monday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 11 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
Woon Ho Family Care Centre operates from Lot 3468, Jalan Lurah 23 in Kempas Baru, on the same JB stretch as Spring Valley and a handful of other private homes. It markets itself across assisted living, skilled nursing, and rehab — covering both lower-acuity residents who need help with daily living and higher-acuity residents needing nursing oversight.

Published services include 24-hour nursing, four meals a day, daily recreational activities, and a stated physiotherapy programme. Doctor consultations are listed as twice-monthly and complimentary, with on-call doctor and ambulance escort available outside that schedule. Operating languages are English and Mandarin. Google reviews are strong but small in number — 5 stars across 19 reviews — typical for a home that grows by referral rather than online marketing.

A reasonable shortlist option for Mandarin-speaking families in southern JB who want a mid-tier home with built-in physio and a fixed doctor cadence. Worth probing on the visit: capacity and current occupancy, JKM licence number, the day and night nurse-to-resident ratio, what physiotherapy looks like in practice (frequency, who delivers it, and whether passive or active), and how the home handles a resident whose condition deteriorates beyond assisted-living level — is transfer to skilled nursing internal or do they discharge.
services: meals, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, transport, activities, dementia_care

title: FCC Family Care Centre Johor Bahru
care_types: Nursing Home + Day Care + Assisted Living + Rehabilitation
visiting_hours: Monday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 11 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 11 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
FCC Family Care Centre operates on Jalan Resam in Taman Bukit Tiram, Ulu Tiram, on the JB outskirts. It positions itself as Malaysia's first integrated senior living and wellness village, and was confirmed in 2020 as the first Malaysian retirement village to deploy SmartPeep — a Singapore-developed vision-AI monitoring system used to flag falls and unusual movement at night. The campus is split into named sub-homes including The CHS Home and a separate MemoryCare Home for dementia residents.

The operator runs three service tiers: stay-in residential care, day care with meals and basic medical support, and rehabilitation/physiotherapy. The site differentiates itself on environment rather than clinical intensity — an edible garden, farm animals, library, craft room and outdoor walking areas form the core of the daily programme, with the AI monitoring layered on top of conventional caregiver staffing. Reviews on Google (4.9★ across 137+ ratings) consistently mention the gardens, animals and friendly staff.

FCC suits families drawn to a low-stimulation, nature-based setting — early-stage dementia, recovery-phase residents, and seniors who would do poorly in a clinical ward are likely the best fit. Families considering it for higher-dependency cases (bedbound, advanced palliative, complex tube feeding) should ask specifically about night-shift RN cover, how SmartPeep alerts are triaged, and the split in capability between the general home and MemoryCare. Pricing, capacity and licence details are not on the public website.
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, transport, activities, dementia_care, palliative

title: Multicare Nursing Home Johor
pricing_display: From RM 1,800 (6-bed shared) to RM 3,500 (VIP single) — published on operator website
care_types: Nursing Home + Day Care + Assisted Living + Rehabilitation
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Multicare Nursing Home opened its Johor Bahru branch in August 2025 — the chain's first expansion outside the Klang Valley. The parent home in Petaling Jaya has roughly 32 years of operating history, which makes Multicare one of the longer-established private nursing groups in Malaysia. The JB site sits in the western Johor Bahru metro (Skudai/Iskandar Puteri area); the operator's published material describes a fully residential nursing facility rather than a light assisted-living setup, with day and night caregiver coverage and an on-call doctor for emergencies.

The chain's published service scope covers assisted living, elderly care, nursing care, stroke rehabilitation, post-operative care, palliative care, dementia care and respite care, with 24-hour registered nurses and caregivers, medication administration, physiotherapy, occupational therapy activities, and oxygen and ventilator support where required. Multicare publishes a full pricing tier list — uncommon among Malaysian nursing homes — at RM 3,500/month for a VIP single room, RM 3,000 for a single, RM 2,800 for 2-bed sharing, RM 2,500 for 3-bed, RM 2,300 for 4-bed, RM 2,000 for 5-bed and RM 1,800 for 6-bed. All tiers are described as AC rooms with TV and Wifi, an attached bathroom, meals delivered to the room and a personal caregiver included. Treat those as starting rates — the actual monthly fee will scale with dependency level, PEG/oxygen needs, wound care and any extras, and chain-wide rates are always worth re-confirming on the call for the specific branch.

Useful things to confirm before placing: the on-duty nurse-to-resident ratio for both day and night, broken out between RNs and caregivers; how many current residents are bed-bound or PEG-fed (those skew workload heavily); halal and dietary handling; language coverage among caregivers; the JKM licence number for the JB premises; and the exact street address and neighbourhood, since the operator hasn't yet published the JB branch address on its main site.
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, transport, activities, dementia_care, palliative

editorial_summary:
Anzaletta Living Care Centre is a licensed private nursing home at 9, Jalan Gangsa 1, Taman Kolam Air, Johor Bahru, operating for close to two decades. It serves residents of all religious backgrounds and communicates in English and Bahasa Malaysia, making it accessible to a broad range of families. The centre describes its approach as holistic elder care in a clean, spacious, home-like setting — a deliberate contrast to the more clinical atmosphere of larger institutional facilities — and flexible visiting hours are offered to help families stay closely involved.

Anzaletta is categorised as offering nursing home, assisted living, and rehabilitation services. Clinically, the centre provides skilled nursing for residents managing chronic conditions or recovering from surgery, weekly visits by a consulting doctor, daily personal hygiene assistance, and private transportation for medical appointments. Rehabilitation programmes are offered specifically for stroke patients and for those undergoing dialysis — both of which indicate a willingness to manage residents with moderate to high care needs. Staff are described as well-trained healthcare professionals with experience in elderly care practice.

Capacity and monthly fees are not published on the facility's website, so families will need to contact the team directly (016-681 1765 or 010-664 2170) to discuss availability and pricing. With roughly two decades in operation and a Google rating of 4.7, the centre has an established presence in the JB private care market. Useful questions to raise on a visit include nurse-to-resident ratios on overnight shifts, the level of dementia care available, whether PEG feeding can be managed on site, and the protocol when a resident's condition deteriorates and hospital transfer is required.
services: nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, transport, activities

title: Genesis Life Care Centre JB
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care JB is the Johor Bahru branch of the Genesis Life Care chain, which operates several centres around peninsular Malaysia. The JB centre opened in April 2025 and sits on Level 6 of Holiday Plaza Tower, Jalan Dato Sulaiman in Taman Century — a vertical, building-based footprint rather than a landed bungalow conversion. The operator advertises 120-plus beds at this address and a 20-plus-strong care team; both figures are operator claims and have not been independently verified.

The website lists a broad service mix — 24/7 nursing, dementia and memory care, post-stroke rehab, palliative and end-of-life care, post-operative recovery, respite, and a senior daycare option. The operator also states weekly on-site doctor consultations and a psychologist-led dementia programme; treat both as marketing claims pending verification at the visit. Packages are advertised from RM 2,500 per month with halal dining available. Google reviews stand at 4.4 across 14 entries, against a wider chain rating closer to 4.8.

A Level-6 mall-tower address running a stated 120-bed footprint is unusual for the JB segment — most homes of that size are in landed buildings or purpose-built blocks, not retail-tower floors. That alone makes an in-person walkthrough essential before any deposit. On the visit, ask which JKM licence number applies to this specific tower-floor address, count the beds and rooms yourself rather than relying on the brochure, and ask the day-shift charge nurse for the actual nurse-to-resident ratio across day and night. Get the visiting doctor's full name and the days they round, ask the same of the psychologist, and pin down what the RM 2,500 floor rate excludes — diapers, feeds, physio sessions, and consumables are commonly billed on top.
services: meals, nursing_care, physio, activities, dementia_care, palliative

parking: Free parking lot, Free street parking
editorial_summary:
Prestige Residence Care (Group) Sdn Bhd is a private nursing operator in Johor Bahru positioning itself for elderly and physically disabled residents. The website lists the basics — 24-hour nursing care, on-call doctor, ambulance arrangement, and physiotherapy — without publishing capacity, address detail, or pricing. Visiting hours are stated as daily 11am to 6pm.

Room configurations mentioned on the site include 2-bed and 4-bed setups, suggesting a small to mid-sized footprint rather than a large purpose-built complex. Specific clinical scope (dementia unit, palliative protocols, PEG/NG feeding, wound care, dialysis access) is not detailed publicly. Reviews on Google sit at 4.4 across 22 entries — a small sample but in line with comparable JB private homes.

For a family wanting a non-chain JB option with on-site nursing and physio, Prestige is worth a shortlist visit. The 2-bed and 4-bed configuration suits residents who want some shared living but not a dorm setup, and the absence of published pricing means there is room to negotiate based on dependency level rather than being locked into a chain rate card. Specific things to ask before signing: JKM licence number and registration status, the day and night nurse-to-resident ratio, the visiting doctor's name and schedule (call versus rounds), what physiotherapy actually involves (passive limb movement vs. supervised active sessions), how the home handles a resident whose acuity rises mid-stay, and whether the published room rate is inclusive or whether nappies, feeds and consumables are billed monthly on top. Ask also for two recent family references — that is the most useful sanity check available where the public review base is thin.
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, transport, activities, dementia_care

title: Evergreen ElderCare Centre EECMSIA
visiting_hours: Monday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM; Tuesday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM; Wednesday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM; Thursday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM; Friday: 9:30 AM to 8:30 PM; Saturday: 9:30 AM to 8:30 PM; Sunday: 9:30 AM to 8 PM
editorial_summary:
Evergreen ElderCare Centre has been operating from Taman Johor in JB since 2014 and is one of the few homes in the state that publicly anchors its dementia care in a Montessori-for-Dementia framework — a structured approach that uses meaningful daily activities to maintain function rather than relying on sedation or pure custodial care. The centre also runs a programme it calls Greening Dementia. Communication on the site is in English and Mandarin.

The model includes 24-hour nursing, respite stays, and short-term rehabilitation. The operator advertises daily on-site physician presence — verify what "daily" means in practice, because the term covers everything from a full-day staff doctor to a brief ward-round to an on-call arrangement labelled "daily". A genuine staff doctor at a small JB home is unusual and a real differentiator if it holds up; a daily ward-round is still meaningful; an on-call arrangement is closer to the JB norm. The operators describe an integrated approach mixing eastern and western medical practice. Specific capacity is not published. Google reviews sit at 4.8 across 16 entries.

For families dealing with moderate-to-advanced dementia who want more than custodial care — particularly Mandarin-speaking families in JB — Evergreen is a strong shortlist option. Visit during daytime hours and ask to observe a Montessori session in progress rather than just being shown the materials, then check the night-shift staffing, because structured-activity programmes only work if there are enough hands during the day to deliver them. Pin down the doctor question with specifics: name, hours on site, days per week, and whether they review charts or just see escalations. Also ask how the home handles resistive behaviours and whether antipsychotic use is openly discussed at family meetings.

title: EHA Sunview ElderCare (Kempas)
care_types: Nursing Home + Day Care + Assisted Living + Rehabilitation
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
EHA Sunview Eldercare is the Kempas branch of EHA Eldercare Group and the group's newest Johor Bahru opening. Sunview markets itself as Malaysia's first "theme-styled therapeutic villa" — a concept the group describes as borrowing from Taiwanese eldercare design that uses environmental theming to reduce cognitive disorientation and reinforce residents' sense of identity. Launch materials highlight fully automatic medical beds and on-site rehabilitation equipment. With a 5.0-star rating across 24 Google reviews, the early signal is favourable, though the review base is the thinnest in the group. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents — making it one of the more established private eldercare chains across Johor and the Klang Valley.

The group's website lists eight published service categories that apply across branches: medical support, nutrition and diet, rehabilitation by physiotherapy, social activities, caregiver training and support, skilled nurses, special nursing procedures, and a traditional Chinese medicine approach (acupuncture and herbal). Pricing is published on EHA's website (ehaeldercare.com.my/our-packages/) and is quoted as a starting point — actual fees vary with the resident's care needs. For the Johor Bahru and Klang Valley branches (which include this one), assisted senior living starts at RM 3,200/month for 24-hour residential care across able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents; respite stays of one to six months start at RM 120/day; and day care, available 11 hours a day across five days a week with flexible timing, starts at RM 1,500/month. The Kluang branch is priced lower (assisted from RM 2,300/month, respite from RM 80/day, day care from RM 1,300/month) — useful context if the family can travel and cost is a major factor. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Launch marketing also claims doctor, nurse, and physiotherapist availability around the clock, which is stronger than the group baseline and worth verifying against the current roster on a visit. Useful questions: how many residents are currently in the home, how many caregivers are on duty per shift, what specific clinical procedures are handled in-house, and whether the themed villa concept translates to anything operationally meaningful day-to-day. As a newer branch, Sunview suits families drawn to modern equipment and a fresh build; families with high-dependency relatives may want a longer operational track record before committing. Hospital Sultan Ismail and KPJ Pasir Gudang are the closest acute-care options.
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, activities, dementia_care

title: EHA Elder Care Home (Kluang)
care_types: Nursing Home + Day Care + Assisted Living + Rehabilitation
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
EHA Elder Care Home in Kluang is EHA Eldercare Group's only non-Johor-Bahru Johor branch, set in the Kluang–Batu Pahat–Yong Peng corridor. Reviewers specifically cite the cost advantage relative to JB, with one noting it is "more than 30% cheaper than Johor Bahru even though the service standard is higher than most nursing homes in JB." The 5.0-star rating across 33 reviews reflects consistent family satisfaction and a more hands-on, owner-operated feel than the JB corporate branches — multiple reviewers praise management by name. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents — making it one of the more established private eldercare chains across Johor and the Klang Valley.

The group's website lists eight published service categories that apply across branches: medical support, nutrition and diet, rehabilitation by physiotherapy, social activities, caregiver training and support, skilled nurses, special nursing procedures, and a traditional Chinese medicine approach (acupuncture and herbal). Pricing is published on EHA's website (ehaeldercare.com.my/our-packages/) and is quoted as a starting point — actual fees vary with the resident's care needs. Kluang is EHA's only value-tier branch and its only non-JB Johor location. Assisted senior living starts at RM 2,300/month — roughly 28% below the JB and Klang Valley group rate of RM 3,200/month — for 24-hour residential care across able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents. Respite stays of one to six months start at RM 80/day, and day care, available 11 hours a day across five days a week with flexible timing, starts at RM 1,300/month. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Kluang's trade-off is geography: KPJ Kluang Specialist Hospital is in-town and Hospital Enche' Besar Hajjah Khalsom (MSQH-accredited government) is also nearby, but the wider tertiary hospital cluster is in JB, roughly 100 km south. For medically stable residents who need personal care and supervision rather than complex acute support, this is not a barrier. The branch suits Kluang-based families keeping relatives near home, and JB families seeking a more affordable placement where geography is a secondary concern. On a visit, confirm how acute escalations are handled (which JB hospitals the home typically transfers to and how transfer is arranged), and check whether the "starting from" rate matches the actual care needs.

title: Gentle Hill Elder Care
visiting_hours: By appointment — 3 time slots daily
editorial_summary:
Gentle Hill Elder Care is a community nursing home in Kulai, Johor, distinguished from most facilities by two policies that speak directly to what families worry about most: a 2-week fully refundable trial admission, and structured appointment-only visiting in three daily time slots. The trial period is extraordinary — very few nursing homes in Malaysia offer it — and signals genuine confidence in their standard of care. Families can move a parent in, observe the experience firsthand, and receive a full refund if it is not the right fit.

The facility takes quality-of-life infrastructure seriously. Fall detection technology is deployed throughout, reducing response times and injury risk for residents. Construction and renovation used VOC-free materials, minimising chemical off-gassing in a population that often has respiratory sensitivities. The kitchen serves both halal and vegetarian menus, making it one of the more inclusively catered nursing homes in Kulai. Hospital Kulai is approximately five minutes away by car, providing solid emergency medical backup.

Gentle Hill is best suited for residents with low-to-moderate care needs — assisted daily living, companionship, supervised mobility — rather than high-dependency medical cases. Rooms are available in 2-bed and 6-bed configurations; no private singles have been confirmed. Pricing is not published online and requires direct enquiry. Families with a parent needing tracheostomy, PEG tube, or intensive dementia management should confirm clinical capabilities with the facility before proceeding.

title: Noble Care
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd. is one of the larger private nursing home chains in Malaysia, operating 16-plus centres across Klang Valley, Penang, Ipoh, Seremban and Johor Bahru. The JB branch this listing refers to is at 17 Jalan Tan Hack Kiat, off Jalan Tampoi, with enquiry lines on +60 16-278 6993 and +60 16-778 6795. The chain is registered as Noble Care (M) Sdn Bhd (registration 201001020128).

The published service scope is broad — respite care, palliative and end-of-life care, dementia care, stroke and cancer support, handicapped adult and child care, plus home nursing on the side. The chain also advertises specific clinical capabilities not always published by smaller homes: bedsore dressing, NG tube changes, colostomy bag changes, Foley's catheter care, tracheostomy care, and post-operative care, alongside physiotherapy. Read this list as advertised capability rather than confirmed delivery: which procedures are performed routinely on-site versus referred out varies by branch, and tracheostomy care in particular is a high-skill task that should not be assumed at the same level as bag and catheter changes (which most caregivers can be trained to do). At the Tampoi branch, ask explicitly which of these are done in-house, by whom, and how often. A separate luxury retirement living arm sits above the standard nursing footprint.

The chain footprint suits families who want operational scale, broader clinical scope on paper, and the option to move a relative between branches if circumstances change. Quality at the Tampoi branch should still be assessed on its own — chain-level marketing does not equal branch-level practice. On the visit, ask for the specific JKM licence for the Tampoi address, request a real-time look at the day and night nurse-to-resident ratio (not the brochure number), confirm the all-in monthly rate including consumables, and if tracheostomy or NG care is part of why you are looking here, ask to meet the named nurse who would actually perform it.

title: Green Acres Elderly Care Centre Managed By Green Acres Residence Care (Group) Sdn. Bhd.
visiting_hours: Monday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 11 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 11 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
Green Acres Elderly Care Centre, operated by Green Acres Residence Care (Group) Sdn. Bhd., has been running since 1998 and is one of the longer-established private nursing homes in Johor Bahru. The main branch sits at 50, Jalan Bukit Kempas 4/3 in Taman Bukit Kempas — a quiet residential neighbourhood about 20 minutes from the city centre via the EDL highway — with a secondary location at 2A-2B, Jalan Intan, Kim Teng Park. The bungalow-style setting is deliberately low-rise and domestic in feel, catering to aged and physically disabled residents who benefit from a calm, less clinical atmosphere.

The centre's stated care model rests on a multidisciplinary team: on-call doctors, a physiotherapist, registered nurses, and nursing aides, all supervised by a resident Home Manager. Physiotherapy and rehabilitation programmes are offered alongside standard nursing care, placing the facility in the nursing home plus rehabilitation category. The proximity to Hospital Permail (roughly 10 minutes away) is a meaningful safety net for residents who may need acute hospital backup. The group operates two JB addresses, suggesting the capacity to serve families from different parts of the city. Contact is via +60 11-5991 9916 or gacarecentre50@gmail.com.

Because the website does not publish bed counts, pricing, or shift-specific nurse ratios, families should go into a site visit with those questions prepared. It is also worth confirming which care conditions — dementia, PEG feeding, tracheostomy — the team is equipped to manage, and whether the rehabilitation programme follows a structured timetable or is delivered on request. Visiting hours are listed on the website as 11 am to 6 pm daily.
services: meals, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, transport

title: Lee Nursing
visiting_hours: 9am–12pm and 4pm–7pm daily
editorial_summary:
Lee Nursing Home (李护理疗养院) is a small, Chinese family-run facility in Taman Nesa, Skudai, operating since 2011. Run by the How family — Mr Richard How handles administration while Mdm Lee (a nurse) leads clinical care — it positions itself as a round-the-clock nursing home rather than a simple old-folk shelter. Services are meaningfully clinical: Ryle's tube feeding, tracheal suctioning, catheter changes, wound care, vital-sign monitoring, and blood-glucose checks are all offered, alongside meals, personal hygiene, laundry, and transport to clinic or hospital appointments. The same management has since opened a second site — Hibiscus Serenity Care Centre in Larkin, JB — suggesting growing operational confidence.

The facility's 3-star Google rating from just two reviews is a meaningful caution flag. Two reviews is too thin a sample to be statistically reliable, but a midpoint score indicates at least one reviewer had a poor or neutral experience. Neither the website (leenursinghome.com) nor any public directory publishes pricing; families must call or WhatsApp for a quote. No JKM licence number or Ministry of Health registration code has been publicly confirmed.

Address inconsistencies across directories (No. 7, 11, and 26 appear against different street names in Taman Nesa) warrant direct verification before visiting. Visiting hours are 9am–12pm and 4pm–7pm daily. Families considering Lee Nursing Home should visit in person, ask for the JKM or MOH licence, confirm the current address, and weigh the below-average Google score against the clinical depth the home claims — including the same management team's track record at Hibiscus Serenity Care Centre.

title: Lotus Ville Care Centre
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: Closed
editorial_summary:
Lotus Ville Care Centre Sdn Bhd is a private nursing home registered under Malaysia's Private Healthcare Facilities and Services Act (Act 586), operating from Taman Johor Jaya in Johor Bahru. Incorporated in April 2022, it is a relatively young facility that has earned membership in AGECOPE — the Association for Residential Aged Care Operators of Malaysia — signalling commitment to industry standards in a sector where many operators remain unaffiliated. Despite the .org.my domain, the company is registered as a private limited company (Sdn Bhd). AGECOPE confirms services include assisted living, palliative care, and dementia care, with staff described as trained in geriatrics, physiotherapy, and nursing care.

A Facebook presence notes a Dr Thurgadevi as a contact, suggesting at least periodic doctor involvement. The 4-star rating from 8 Google reviews reflects steady community satisfaction, reasonable for a facility less than three years old. Two addresses appear in aggregator data — 67, Jalan Anggerik 20, Taman Johor Jaya (the more frequently cited, likely main site) and No. 1, Jalan Camar 3, Taman Perling — which may indicate a second branch or a data discrepancy that families should verify directly before visiting.

No pricing has been published publicly — quote-on-request model consistent with the broader Malaysian nursing home market. No religious affiliation has been identified; the lotus branding appears to be aesthetic rather than Buddhist or denominational. Families should call +60 17-737 1233 to confirm the current address, pricing, RN night coverage, and halal meal availability before making a placement decision.

title: Yong An Old Folks Care Centre - Ulu Tiram, JB
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Yong An Old Folks Care Centre (荣安养老院) sits on Jalan Berlian 4 in Taman Gunung Emas, Ulu Tiram, on the eastern edge of Johor Bahru. The home has been operating since 2013 under registration JM0650487-K and runs primarily in Mandarin and English, reflecting a largely Chinese-speaking resident base. Its location is elevated, which the operator notes helps with airflow and odour control — a small but practical detail that matters in long-term residential care.

The core offering is full-board residential care: four home-cooked meals daily, twice-daily showers, daily linen changes, medication support, and basic monitoring of temperature and blood pressure roughly three times a week. Wound care, post-hospital recovery support, on-site doctor consultations and physiotherapy are listed, alongside structured activities and pet therapy sessions. Pricing is publicly stated — RM 2,400/month for ambulatory residents and RM 2,700/month for those needing diapers — which is unusually transparent for a Johor home in this segment.

Yong An reads as a mid-sized Chinese-Malaysian residential home suited to families wanting straightforward elderly care with predictable monthly costs and no language barrier in Mandarin. Diet appears flexible (pork is on the menu, which rules it out for halal-only families). The website does not publish bed count, registered nurse coverage at night, or whether the on-site doctor is salaried or visiting — useful things to confirm by phone before placement, especially for residents with active medical needs.
services: meals, nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, activities

title: AustinLoyal Care Centre
visiting_hours: Sat/Sun & public holidays 12:30pm–4:30pm; weekdays by appointment
editorial_summary:
AustinLoyal Care Centre operates from a corner lot at 1, Jalan Mutiara Emas 3/4 in Taman Mount Austin, a busy urban pocket of JB with hospitals, clinics, schools, and shops within a few minutes' drive. The home is run by AUSTINLOYAL SDN BHD (1142053-H) and soft-launched in March 2018, positioning itself as an "urban care retreat" — a modern, non-institutional alternative to traditional nursing homes, with a stated emphasis on rehabilitation and dignity rather than custodial care. The published service mix is broader than most JB homes its size: long-term residential care, short-term and respite stays, post-hospital rehabilitative care, adult social day care, and an outpatient physiotherapy clinic that runs weekdays 10am–6pm and is open to non-residents.

A quirk worth understanding before you read the Google rating: the aggregate score sits at 1.9 across 260+ reviews, but the negative reviews cluster around the 2018–2019 opening period, while recent reviews trend positive. The rating is a stale snapshot of the facility's growing pains, not its current state — which is why we encourage families to filter Google reviews by "newest" before forming a view, rather than reading the headline number. Visiting hours are weekends and public holidays 12:30–4:30pm; weekday visits are by appointment, which the operator frames as a resident-privacy policy.

What is not published is room configurations, monthly fees, capacity, JKM licence number, doctor visit frequency, and overnight nurse-to-resident ratio — so these belong on a tour or call. Useful questions for the enquiry: which care levels are accepted today (skilled nursing, dementia, palliative, tube-feeding via NG/PEG); the ratio of trained nurses to residents in the day and overnight; whether the visiting doctor is in-house or on-call and how often they round; whether physiotherapy is included or charged per session for residents; and a written, itemised quote covering room rate, diapers, medications, and supplements before any deposit.

title: Blissful Senior Care Centre
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Blissful Senior Care Centre is one of Kluang's licensed residential care options for older adults, operating in a town of 323,000 people in central Johor — roughly 100 km north of Johor Bahru and positioned as a more affordable alternative to the saturated urban eldercare market. The facility's emphasis on government licensing and certification, carried prominently in its name and description, signals a deliberate positioning against the unlicensed care homes that continue to operate across peninsular Malaysia's smaller towns. With two well-equipped hospitals in Kluang itself — the government Hospital Enche' Besar Hajjah Khalsom (MSQH-accredited) and the private KPJ Kluang Specialist Hospital (JCI and MSQH accredited) — residents requiring acute care have reliable access without lengthy transfers.

The 4.8-star rating drawn from 22 Google reviews is the facility's most telling public signal. For a care home in a secondary Malaysian town, 22 reviews represents genuine community engagement, and a 4.8 average at that sample size almost certainly reflects authentic positive family experiences. Malaysian eldercare review patterns consistently reward facilities where staff warmth, cleanliness, food quality, and responsive family communication converge — traits that small, owner-operated care homes in towns like Kluang sometimes deliver more consistently than larger corporatised urban facilities.

What limits this profile is entirely a data gap, not a performance concern. Blissful operates without any public web presence beyond its Google Business listing — no website, no indexed Facebook page, no published pricing. Pricing, clinical capabilities, room configurations, and staffing levels all require a direct call to the facility. Once pricing and clinical scope are confirmed, this facility has the hallmarks of a credible, community-trusted home in an underserved market.

editorial_summary:
JR Segamat Care Centre is the Johor branch of Johnson Residence, a Malaysian eldercare chain operating six locations: JR Pro (Kuala Lumpur), Kee Ann (Melaka), Turf View (Penang), JR Kuantan (Pahang), JR Segamat (Johor) and JR My Place (Ipoh, listed as forthcoming). The Segamat outlet is the chain's only Johor presence and serves a town and surrounding district where residential nursing options are limited compared to JB or the Klang Valley.

Group-wide, Johnson Residence positions itself on three pillars — affordable, professional, caring — and offers 24-hour care, personal hygiene support, laundry, customised meals, and a choice of private or shared rooms. Staff training is described as ongoing and explicitly includes vital-signs monitoring, body repositioning, hand hygiene and basic life support; the operator says it accommodates five distinct categories of residents but does not detail those categories publicly. Branch-level detail (capacity, RN coverage, pricing) for Segamat specifically is not on the website.

JR Segamat is a reasonable option for families based in central or northern Johor who need residential care close to home rather than commuting to JB. The chain network is a real advantage if a resident later needs to relocate to KL, Melaka or Penang under the same operator. Specific things to confirm by phone: bed count, monthly pricing for shared vs private rooms, language coverage among caregivers, whether a doctor visits routinely, and how Segamat handles palliative or end-of-life cases given its smaller-town setting.

title: Golden Age Care Centre
visiting_hours: 9:00am – 6:00pm daily
editorial_summary:
Golden Age Care Centre (GACC) is a multi-branch private nursing home chain headquartered in Muar, Johor — note that Muar is a distinct town roughly 90 km north-west of Johor Bahru, not part of the JB conurbation. The Muar branch, the chain's founding location (established 2004, incorporated 2014), sits at 28-10 Jalan Ria 2, Taman Ria — off Jalan Salleh in central Muar, the same arterial road as Hospital Pakar Sultanah Fatimah, placing it within easy reach of the town's main government specialist hospital. The chain also operates in Batu Pahat, Tangkak, and Melaka.

The Muar branch holds a 4-Star JKM rating — the highest tier achievable under the national licensing framework. Services span residential care, on-site nursing and physiotherapy, a dedicated Memory Care programme, occupational and speech therapy, nutritional dining with dietary accommodations, hospital transport, and 24/7 CCTV monitoring. Private and semi-private rooms are available; exact room pricing is not publicly disclosed. Visiting hours are 9am–6pm daily.

At 3.9 stars from 8 Google reviews, the Muar branch occupies a mid-range position — thin volume but a substantially better signal than the Batu Pahat branch (1.3★/3 reviews), which carries a poor reputation. Families choosing GACC should confirm they are placing a loved one specifically at the Muar location and ask directly about halal meal provision, language capabilities of care staff (Mandarin, Malay, English), and whether dedicated dementia-unit beds are currently available.

title: Cozzi Confinement Center (Yong Peng)
visiting_hours: Monday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 9:30 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
Cozzi Confinement Center at Yong Peng is a postnatal care branch of the Cozzi group, located at 2 Jalan Kota Impian 4 in Yong Peng township, Batu Pahat District. Yong Peng sits at the convergence of the North–South Expressway with routes to Muar and Batu Pahat town, giving the branch reasonable accessibility for families from across central Johor. Like all Cozzi branches, it operates exclusively as a Chinese-style confinement centre for new mothers and newborns — no elderly care, nursing home, or assisted living services are offered.

The Yong Peng branch follows the Cozzi group standard: 28-day residential packages (RM 17,800 Deluxe or RM 20,800 Premium) covering private air-conditioned accommodation with en-suite bathroom, 24-hour nursing and nanny coverage, five daily TCM-formulated meals, postnatal massage and body-wrap therapies, and structured educational activities for new parents. A TCM practitioner and dietician are involved in meal planning; medical emergencies rely on on-call arrangements. Babies and mothers share monitored rooms during the confinement period.

Families searching for elder care in Yong Peng or surrounding Batu Pahat districts should note that Cozzi does not serve that need at this branch. For new-mother families, the Yong Peng location is the newest addition to Cozzi's Batu Pahat cluster and is best contacted directly at +60 12-724 1013 to confirm room availability and any seasonal promotions, as package pricing and add-ons are updated periodically by the group.
services: meals, laundry, nursing_care, transport, activities

title: Haywood Senior Living Medini Johor Bahru
care_types: Independent, Assisted, Long-term, Short-term rehab, Day care, Palliative
visiting_hours: Monday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 10 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed
editorial_summary:
Haywood Senior Living Medini is the first hotel-concept senior living facility in southern Malaysia, embedded inside the Ramada by Wyndham Medini hotel in Iskandar Puteri. Residents live in remodelled 400 sqft hotel studio apartments — complete with private balconies, CCTV, in-room call bells, and elderly-adapted bathrooms with grab bars and wall-mounted bath chairs — while receiving 24/7 caregiver support, on-site nursing, and standby doctors. The model is built for dignity of space: no shared wards, no institutional corridors. It is operated by Mintygreen Healthcare Group (Emerald Care Center Sdn Bhd), a Malaysian eldercare brand established in 2017.

The location is a genuine differentiator. Gleneagles Hospital Medini Johor — the first tertiary private hospital in Iskandar Puteri — is three minutes away by car. For families whose primary concern is emergency medical access, no other nursing home reviewed in Johor Bahru can match this adjacency. The facility explicitly markets itself to JB-Singapore cross-border families: it sits 20 km from the Tuas Checkpoint, includes monthly resident outings to Singapore's Botanic Gardens, and positions the cost difference between Malaysian and Singaporean residential care as a key value proposition.

The amenity programme goes beyond standard nursing home offerings: a senior-friendly heated swimming pool with aqua aerobics sessions, a fully equipped gym, reflexology pathways, in-house physiotherapy, and TCM services (acupuncture and Tui Na) available as add-ons. Four meals are included daily, with a complimentary hotel breakfast buffet once per week. Pricing is by enquiry only — consistent with premium positioning and likely in the upper tier of the Malaysian market. Families should confirm the JKM licence number directly with the facility, as it was not publicly listed at time of research.

title: GOLDEN AGE CARE CENTRE TANGKAK
care_types: Nursing Home + Day Care + Assisted Living + Rehabilitation
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Golden Age Care Centre (GACC) Tangkak is the chain's northern Johor branch, set at 5 Jalan Teratai 2 in Taman Tangkak Jaya — a small-town setting in Muar District where most placements come from local kampung and town families rather than the cross-border commuter market that drives JB demand. GACC was founded in 2004, is licensed under the Akta Pusat Jagaan, and carries a 4-star JKM rating. The Tangkak branch operates alongside sister homes in Muar, Melaka and Batu Pahat; group-wide the chain reports more than 200 residents.

In a Tangkak catchment, the practical implication is that resident profiles skew towards lower-acuity stable elders and end-of-life care close to family, rather than the heavier post-discharge rehab cases more common at the chain's larger-town branches. The published trilingual operation (English, Mandarin and Malay) genuinely matters here — Tangkak's Chinese, Malay and Indian populations live more mixed than in metropolitan JB, and a family member visiting parents at the home shouldn't have to bring a translator. Branch-level features to confirm at Tangkak specifically: how many beds are on site (the chain figure is not the branch figure), whether the named physiotherapist is full-time at Tangkak or rotating from Muar, whether the speech-therapy and OT services advertised at chain level are actually delivered at Tangkak or only at the larger branches, and what local hospital arrangement applies — Hospital Tangkak is small, so escalation may mean a transfer to Muar or Segamat.

The Tangkak branch suits families wanting a licensed, multilingual chain home for an older relative who would otherwise be cared for at home in town or kampung — the JKM 4-star rating, the chain backing, and the in-town location are the genuine differentiators, particularly for families who want a parent to remain near siblings rather than be moved to JB. Pricing is not published; call ahead for a branch-specific quote that breaks out room rate, consumables and any therapy add-ons.
services: meals, nursing_care, physio, transport, activities, dementia_care

title: Summer Eldercare Johor Bahru
visiting_hours: Monday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 8 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Summer Eldercare is a small Johor Bahru eldercare provider with a public phone line at +60 17-220 3342 and a single-page Canva-hosted website that does not publish operational detail beyond the brand name. The facility appears on Google with a 5★ rating across a small number of reviews (around 5), placing it among newer or smaller operators in the JB market without significant directory or media coverage at the time of writing.

Because the website carries no service catalogue, no address detail beyond the JB area, and no description of clinical capability, almost nothing about scope can be verified from public sources. There is no published information on bed capacity, founding date, ownership, languages, religious orientation, halal status, registered-nurse coverage, or pricing. Likewise, whether the home accepts dementia, palliative or bedbound residents is not stated.

Summer Eldercare is best treated as a facility that requires direct phone contact before any decision — families considering placement should ask for a site visit, request the licence number under the Akta Pusat Jagaan, confirm the operator's experience and staff qualifications, and obtain pricing in writing. The strong but small Google rating is a positive signal but not yet enough on its own to characterise the home; until the operator publishes more detail, this listing should be considered minimally documented.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan Tangkak
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan Tangkak is a nursing home located in Tangkak, Johor.
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan Tangkak is a welfare-model residential care home operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor, a charitable association founded in 2009 to provide shelter and care for abandoned elderly persons and individuals without family support. The Tangkak branch — located in Parit Bunga, Ledang District, near the Tangkak town cluster — is one of four Nur Ehsan homes spread across Johor, with the others in Johor Bahru (Kempas Baru), Bukit Indah, and Simpang Renggam. Collectively the four branches house approximately 167 residents comprising senior citizens alongside adults with physical and mental disabilities.

The Tangkak facility was the site of a 2024 clinical community programme by UiTM medical students that provided a useful snapshot of residents' clinical profile: all 21 elderly residents showed signs of frailty, six were assessed as high-risk for dementia, and residents ranged widely in their independence with daily activities. The programme introduced structured physical exercise, cognitive training, cooking sessions, and music therapy to supplement the home's existing programme of morning exercise and recreational outings. Regular health monitoring and physiotherapy are part of the standard care offer, and the home operates with 24-hour security and CCTV. The UiTM engagement also noted that limited staffing constrains the range of leisure activities that can be run on a day-to-day basis.

Because Nur Ehsan operates as a charity with no fixed resident fees, it functions primarily as a safety-net placement for elderly persons who lack family or financial resources — not as a fee-for-service nursing home. Families considering this route should contact the Johor association directly at 010-764 1379 or 07-232 6415 to understand the intake process and whether the Tangkak branch has capacity. It is also worth asking about medical referral arrangements for residents who require hospitalisation, the staff-to-resident ratio at the Tangkak site specifically, and whether residents with moderate dementia can be supported given the frailty profile of the current resident group.

title: Pusat jagaan kebajikan Manna Sinar Cahaya
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Kebajikan Manna Sinar Cahaya sits in a distinct segment of Johor Bahru's elder-care landscape — the welfare home sector — that serves clients who cannot access or afford the city's growing private nursing home industry. The name itself signals its positioning: 'Manna' is a Biblical reference found almost exclusively in Christian charity branding in Malaysia, while 'Kebajikan' (welfare) signals a social-care rather than commercial orientation. Families considering this facility should understand they are not comparing it against private nursing homes charging RM 2,000–5,000 per month; they are comparing it against other welfare homes where care is donation-subsidised, JKM-referred, and focused on shelter and basic daily support rather than clinical nursing.

The facility has a minimal digital footprint — no website, no social media presence, and no listing in the major NGO or JKM directories accessible online — which is not unusual for small Christian welfare homes in Malaysia but does mean families must make direct contact and conduct in-person visits to assess quality. Its Google rating of 3.9 stars from just 7 reviews provides a weak signal; with so few reviewers, the score could reflect one or two dissatisfied family members rather than a systemic pattern.

For families seeking an affordable long-term placement for an elderly relative who is financially disadvantaged, ambulatory or minimally dependent, and comfortable in a Christian environment, this facility warrants investigation through JKM Johor Bahru's referral process. Families should request to see the facility's JKM registration certificate, visit in person, and ask specifically about the monthly fee, staff roster, and emergency medical arrangements before committing. For high-dependency care — ventilator, PEG, dementia unit, post-surgical recovery — this type of welfare home is not the right fit.

title: Master Nursing Care Centre @Daya
visiting_hours: Monday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: Closed
editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre @Daya (MCC@Daya) is one of three branches operated by Master Care Centre (慈康护理中心), a private nursing home group with over a decade of presence in Johor Bahru. The Daya branch sits at 58–62, Jalan Nipah 14, in Taman Daya — a mature residential suburb in JB's Tebrau zone, roughly 10–12 km north of the city centre, served by Causeway Link bus T11. The group's website (masternursingcare.com) was offline during research, but third-party directory data confirms the group's core services: personalised elderly care, post-surgery recovery, stroke rehabilitation, physiotherapy, dementia care, and palliative care.

The 5-star rating from only 4 reviews warrants caution. A perfect score on a very thin review base is statistically unreliable. MCC@Daya does not appear in the top-10 nursing home roundups circulating in JB, nor in the AGECOPE member directory. Contact for the Daya branch: Mr Kelvin (+60 11-1080 1805) or Ms Wawa (+60 11-1129 9791). The group also operates branches at HQ in Bandar JB and at Taman Abad — families should confirm which branch they are enquiring about.

Pricing is not published on any platform; families must call for a quote. No JKM licence number has surfaced in public directories. Halal meal policy is unconfirmed despite Taman Daya being a multi-faith neighbourhood. A site visit remains the recommended first step, with particular attention to staffing levels, overnight nursing cover, and the condition of shared facilities given the limited review base.

title: Hibiscus Serenity Care Centre (Larkin)
visiting_hours: Monday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 8 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
Hibiscus Serenity Care Centre is a newly established nursing home in Kampung Ungku Mohsin, Larkin — north of central JB and within easy reach of Larkin Sentral and Hospital Sultanah Aminah. While Hibiscus itself is new, the operating family has been running Lee Nursing Home since 2011, so the centre carries forward more than a decade of practical experience even though it has only a handful of Google reviews so far (5 stars across 7 entries).

The site describes nursing care for older adults with experienced nurses and caregivers, communicated in English and Mandarin. Specific capacity, room types, doctor visit frequency, and detailed clinical capabilities (PEG feeding, wound care complexity, dementia unit) are not yet published. Pricing is not listed online; the published enquiry line is +60 12-777 6673.

North-JB families wanting a smaller, family-run home with continuity to a longer-running operation should consider a visit. The proximity to Hospital Sultanah Aminah is a genuine practical advantage for residents likely to need acute escalation — transfers from Larkin to HSA are short, and the family can usually get to either site quickly. Because the centre is new in its own right, ask harder questions on the visit: what is the current resident count and intended capacity, how many caregivers are rostered per shift in practice (not on paper), who is the visiting doctor and how often they actually round, whether the home is JKM-licensed in its own name or only under the older Lee Nursing Home licence, and what the published rate covers versus what is billed extra each month. Worth asking also whether residents from Lee Nursing Home have transferred across — if they have, the staff continuity is a real signal of the operation's depth.
