title: Master Care Centre • Taman Abad
longitude: Master Care Centre • Taman Abad is a nursing home located in Johor Bahru, Johor. It holds a 4.7/5 rating on Google Maps from 6 reviews.
visiting_hours: Monday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 10 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre — Taman Abad (MCC@Abad) is the second branch of the Master Care Centre group, located at 5, Jalan Dato Sulaiman, Taman Abad, 80250 Johor Bahru. Taman Abad is a mature, centrally positioned neighbourhood in JB, making this branch well-placed for families living in the western and central parts of the city. Like the group's other locations, MCC@Abad focuses on nursing care, post-surgery recovery, long-term elderly residential care, and rehabilitation support, with a particular emphasis on stroke recovery and memory care for residents with dementia or Alzheimer's.

MCC@Abad shares the same clinical standards, management team, and contact number (+60 11-1080 1805) as the group's other two branches — MCC@Yahya in Bandar JB and MCC@Daya in Taman Daya. This multi-branch structure means families can discuss availability and care requirements across all sites with a single enquiry. The group's more than ten years of operation across JB supports a reputation for consistent, professionally managed elder care, and the Abad branch carries a 4.7-star Google rating. The care model is oriented toward residents with clinical needs rather than purely assisted-living requirements.

Bed count and fee schedules are not published online. When calling, it is advisable to specify the Taman Abad branch, as enquiries are handled centrally across all three locations. Key questions include current vacancy, monthly fees broken down by room type and care level, nurse availability on overnight shifts, whether the facility can manage residents dependent on oxygen or PEG feeding, and what the escalation pathway is if a resident's condition requires hospital-level intervention.

title: Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd (Co. 567396-U) is one of three facilities operated under the Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care group, a family-owned eldercare operator established in Johor Bahru in 2003. The group's founding mission was to address a critical shortage of reliable nursing care in the city, and it has since expanded to three locations clustered in and around Taman Kebun Teh. Sunrise Care Centre occupies No. 48 Jalan Keranji — directly adjacent to the original Rebina House Care Centre at Nos. 15 & 17 on the same road. The domain rebinasunrise.com serves all three entities under a shared group brand.

Clinically, the group punches above its residential positioning. Confirmed services include CAPD peritoneal dialysis, Ryle's tube feeding and replacement, phlegm suction, nebuliser therapy, balloon catheter management, post-operative wound care, acupuncture, physiotherapy, blood pressure and glucose monitoring, and oxygen supply — a notably comprehensive list for a private nursing home. The group also offers palliative and hospice care, indicating acceptance of higher-dependency residents. Individualised nursing care plans with hourly schedules are a documented care standard.

Pricing is not published on the group's website or third-party directories; families must call +60 7-333 7715 or email care@rebinasunrise.com for a quote. The facility has 3 Google reviews at 5 stars specifically for the Sunrise branch — the sister facility Rebina House has wider review coverage (4.8★). No JKM licence number is publicly visible. A home care arm (Rebina Sunrise Home Care Services Sdn Bhd) also operates from 112 Jalan Wijaya, Taman Abad.
services: nursing_care, physio, doctor_visit, activities, palliative

title: Alda Homes Eldercare
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 8 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 8 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 8 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 8 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 8 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 8 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 8 PM
editorial_summary:
Alda Homes Eldercare has been caring for elderly residents in the heart of Johor Bahru since 2016, operating from a converted residential property in Taman Abad — a central, accessible neighbourhood within easy reach of the city's hospitals and amenities. Positioning itself around personalised, home-like care rather than a clinical or institutional model, the facility suits lower-dependency residents who would thrive in a small-scale, family-run environment rather than a large purpose-built centre. Its active social media presence, featuring group exercise sessions and community events, suggests a genuine commitment to resident wellbeing beyond basic physical care.

The facility's key limitation for families conducting online research is a near-total absence of digital transparency. Its website domain is unreachable, its three Facebook pages create confusion about the canonical contact channel, and no pricing, staffing ratios, clinical capabilities, or JKM licence number have been disclosed publicly. Alda Homes does not appear in the AGECOPE Johor member directory, and has accumulated only 11 Google reviews in nearly a decade of operation.

For a low-to-moderate dependency resident who needs a warm, central JB placement rather than high-acuity nursing, Alda Homes is worth a site visit. Pricing is unconfirmed but expected to be broadly mid-market for JB central. Prospective families should verify the JKM licence on-site, confirm RN coverage for both day and night shifts, and establish a clear communication protocol with the operator before committing to placement.

title: AX Nursing Home PLT
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
AX Nursing Home PLT is a small, privately-owned nursing care facility in Johor Bahru, registered under Malaysia's Limited Liability Partnership (PLT) structure — a commercially oriented entity used by small-to-medium businesses. Its online presence is confined to a Facebook page (axnursinghome) and a Google Business listing, with no independent website, and it does not appear in any published 'top nursing homes in JB' roundup or major directory.

A Google rating of 3 stars from only 2 reviews signals a facility in an early or underpublicised stage: it is neither well-rated nor widely reviewed, and families researching online will find little to go on. No verified address, pricing, bed count, or clinical capability has been found in any public source. For-profit PLT registration indicates commercial intent; families should confirm whether nursing care or basic residential care is the primary offer.

Prospective families should call +60 11-5999 9905 directly and ask for the JKM licence number to confirm the home's regulatory standing. Given the below-average rating on very thin review data, a site visit and verification of current residents' families' experiences is strongly recommended before any placement decision.

title: Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd
editorial_summary:
Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd is a formally incorporated elder care operator in Taman Abad, Johor Bahru, Johor, at 112, Jalan Wijaya — one of the older residential neighbourhoods in JB, close to the city centre. The registered corporate name suggests formal legal incorporation, though no website is available for further detail.

The facility appears to offer both residential and day care services based on its registered care types. No phone number is currently published on Google Maps; families should visit directly or contact the JKM Johor office to obtain current details.

Two Google Maps reviews average 3.5 stars. One reviewer described the environment as clean and quiet; a second observed strict daily routines and noted that some staff could be short-tempered with residents. When visiting, observe staff interactions with residents first-hand and ask about staffing ratios, resident routines and the JKM licence number.

title: Pusat Perjagaan Orang Tua Ren Ai
editorial_summary:
Pusat Perjagaan Orang Tua Ren Ai operates in the Senai township within Kulai District, Johor — an area near Senai International Airport, midway between Kulai town and central Johor Bahru. The name 'Ren Ai' (仁爱, benevolence and love) and its Facebook handle RenAiSenai indicate a Chinese-community welfare orientation, likely Buddhist or non-denominational Chinese charitable in character, consistent with the tradition of self-help elderly care associations common to Johor's Chinese townships.

Online presence is extremely limited: the Facebook page confirms the Senai location, but the facility does not appear in any major Malaysian nursing home directories including AGECOPE, ielder.asia, nursinghome.my, or Homage. No address, bed count, pricing, or JKM licence number could be confirmed. This is a zero-review, minimal-web-presence facility — indicating either a new operation, a primarily word-of-mouth referral base within the Chinese community, or a very small facility that has not engaged with digital platforms.

Prospective families should call +60 7-599 1944 directly and verify JKM registration before committing to a visit. Confirm the exact address in Senai, the care model offered (nursing vs residential), language capabilities of staff (likely Mandarin/Cantonese), and current bed availability.

title: MK ELDERCARE CENTRE
visiting_hours: Monday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 10 AM to 6 PM

title: Fu Ka Care Center
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Tuesday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Wednesday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Thursday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Friday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Saturday: 9 AM to 7 PM; Sunday: 9 AM to 7 PM
editorial_summary:
Fu Ka Care Center, operating under the registered entity Fu Ka Nursing Care Center Sdn. Bhd. (reg. 1512468W), is a private nursing home established in May 2023 and situated at PTD 14521, Jalan Persiaran Kempas Baru, Kempas Baru, Johor Bahru. The facility is located in the Kempas Baru corridor — a growing residential district in south-central JB well-served by Kempas Medical Centre nearby. Being a newer entrant, the centre has not yet appeared in major Malaysian elder-care directories such as AGECOPE Johor or nursinghome.my, though it has established a presence on Facebook and Instagram at @fukacarecenter.

The facility promotes itself as a full-service nursing home offering 24-hour nursing care, medical supervision, physiotherapy, dementia care, and specialist care. Its 5-star rating across 24 Google reviews is a positive early signal for a facility incorporated in 2023. The name 'Fu Ka' (福家) translates from Chinese as 'blessed family,' suggesting a Chinese-community orientation, though care for all ethnicities is not excluded. Pricing information has not been publicly disclosed on any platform and must be confirmed by direct inquiry.

JKM licence details, halal certification status, nurse-to-resident ratios, and staffing structure remain unverified from open sources. Families considering this centre should call +60 19-381 4521 or email fukanursinghome14521@gmail.com for pricing and availability. A site visit is recommended before any placement commitment given the limited review history for a facility less than three years old.

title: Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Yeo JB
visiting_hours: Monday: 8 AM to 8 PM; Tuesday: 8 AM to 8 PM; Wednesday: 8 AM to 8 PM; Thursday: 8 AM to 8 PM; Friday: 8 AM to 8 PM; Saturday: 8 AM to 8 PM; Sunday: 8 AM to 8 PM
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Yeo JB is a family-operated care home in Johor Bahru, identifiable by the Facebook page at gracehome.yeo and associated with a proprietor surnamed Yeo. The 'Grace Home' Facebook brand suggests either a Christian-leaning ethos or simply a name chosen by the Yeo family. Two different addresses surface in public data — Stulang Darat (80300 JB) and Taman Bukit Kempas (81200 JB) — suggesting the facility may have relocated or that one source holds stale data.

The facility carries zero Google reviews and is absent from all major nursing home directories. No service details, pricing, bed count, or JKM registration status could be confirmed from available online sources. Family-operated care homes in JB can offer warm, personalised care in a home-like setting, but the complete absence of digital presence and verifiable regulatory information means families cannot conduct any meaningful remote assessment.

A phone call to +60 16-717 3127 and a site visit are the only reliable ways to verify the current address, services, and licensing status. Ask specifically which address is current, request sight of the JKM licence on arrival, and speak with the proprietor Mr/Ms Yeo about staffing model and care levels.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan
visiting_hours: Monday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Tuesday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Wednesday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Thursday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Friday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Saturday: 8 AM to 5 PM; Sunday: 8 AM to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan is a Muslim welfare home for the elderly operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor (PKNENJ), a registered NGO that has been active since November 2008. This is not a commercial nursing home — it is a faith-based welfare facility that accepts destitute, abandoned, and family-less elderly Muslims, as well as individuals with physical and mental disabilities and converts (muallaf). Residents are referred by JKM officers, hospital social work units (including Hospital Permai JB), the Johor Islamic Council (MAIJ), and the Johor Islamic Affairs Department (JAIJ). Families who can contribute are asked to pay a modest amount toward costs, but the facility is donation-driven and does not publish a standard fee schedule.

The Kempas Baru branch at Jalan Denai 1 serves primarily male elderly residents across five wards categorised by care dependency, with a stated capacity of 56. The wider organisation runs four branches across Johor (Ledang, Bukit Indah, and Simpang Renggam), housing roughly 167 residents in total. Physiotherapy, daily health monitoring by visiting medical professionals, social and recreational activities, and 24-hour CCTV security are documented. The facility has received donations from Yayasan JCorp and Boustead Properties, confirming legitimate NGO standing.

For families considering placement, this facility is most appropriate for low-income elderly Muslims without a family support network, or as a JKM-referred placement for individuals whose families cannot be traced. Families seeking fee-for-service care should contact the manager (Encik Saiful, 013-794 0350) to discuss individual arrangements. No JKM licence number is publicly listed; prospective referrers should request it directly before placement.

title: Agape Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Agape Care Centre sits quietly on Jalan Lurah 18 in Kempas Baru — a residential street that has become one of Johor Bahru's most recognisable care home clusters, home also to Woon Ho Family Care Centre and Spring Valley Homecare. At No. 62, this small facility operates as both a nursing home and a retirement home, catering to elderly who need daily assistance rather than intensive clinical intervention. With a 4.6-star Google rating from five reviewers, it carries an excellent word-of-mouth reputation within what appears to be a tight-knit referral circle.

The name 'Agape' — Greek for unconditional love, central to Christian theology — strongly suggests faith-inspired ownership or management, placing Agape Care Centre in the tradition of church-affiliated elderly care. For Christian residents or those whose families are embedded in a Christian community, this setting may offer something most commercial nursing homes do not: a genuine sense of belonging, pastoral companionship, and a value system that treats care as vocation. Whether this translates to organised chapel sessions, church volunteer visits, or informal devotional culture is something families should ask directly.

Agape Care Centre has no confirmed website, no publicly available pricing, no JKM licence number in any accessible registry, and no detailed clinical services list. This level of opacity is not unusual for a small referral-based care home in Malaysia, but it means families cannot evaluate this facility without a phone call and a site visit. Before placement, the JKM licence must be verified, clinical capabilities assessed against the resident's medical needs, and the nature of any religious programming clarified for non-Christian families.

editorial_summary:
Comfort Home Care Centre is a residential nursing home at 56, Jalan Kampung Tengah 1/2, Taman Kampung Tengah, 84000 Muar, Johor — a quiet residential neighbourhood in central Muar town. It is one of a small number of dedicated elder care facilities serving Muar, a town with limited nursing home supply relative to its aging population. Phone: +60 12-696 9403.

The facility has 11 photos on Google Maps, more than most comparable homes in the district, suggesting active day-to-day operations. Care details, capacity and pricing are not published online.

Five reviews average 5.0 stars. No detailed review text was available at the time of writing, so specific care observations cannot be confirmed. When calling, ask about current bed availability, care types accepted, JKM licence status and monthly fees.

title: Sincere Heart Care Centre 爱馨养老院
longitude: Sincere Heart Care Centre 爱馨养老院 is a nursing home located in Muar, Johor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 2 reviews.
visiting_hours: Monday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 10 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 10 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
Sincere Heart Care Centre (爱馨养老院) is a nursing home located in Muar, Johor, serving elderly residents in one of the town's smaller care facilities. The Chinese name — 爱馨养老院 — translates loosely as "loving fragrance eldercare home," reflecting a warmly oriented residential ethos that is common among community-scale homes in the Muar area. The facility appears to be part of a small cluster of care homes in this part of town, and notably shares its listed contact number (+60 12-696 9403) with the nearby Comfort Home Care Centre, which may point to a common operator managing more than one property.

Public information on Sincere Heart Care Centre is limited and most operational detail — bed count, daily schedule, medical staff coverage, care specialisations — is not available online. What is established from its Google Maps listing is its residential nursing home classification and its geographic position in northern Muar town. Families in Muar looking for local options will find that calling directly is the most efficient way to establish current availability, pricing, and the scope of nursing support on offer.

Practical questions to ask when you reach the home include whether the same management oversees both this facility and Comfort Home Care Centre (which can affect staffing), what the nurse-to-resident ratio looks like on evenings and weekends, whether a doctor reviews residents regularly or purely on an as-needed basis, and what care conditions the home is equipped — and licensed — to accept. An in-person visit will give the clearest sense of the environment before a placement decision is made.

title: Graceville Senior Care Centre 美好疗养中心
longitude: Graceville Senior Care Centre 美好疗养中心 is a nursing home located in Muar, Johor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 9 reviews.
visiting_hours: Monday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Tuesday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Wednesday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Thursday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Friday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Saturday: 8 AM to 6 PM; Sunday: 8 AM to 6 PM
editorial_summary:
Graceville Senior Care Centre (美好疗养中心) is a nursing home situated in the Muar area of Johor, serving the town and surrounding district. The dual English and Chinese name signals that the centre caters to the local Chinese-speaking community — Muar has a historically significant Chinese Malaysian population — though the facility's exact language and admission policies are best confirmed directly. Muar is a river town roughly 45 km from Batu Pahat and 90 km from Johor Bahru, and Graceville represents one of the more established residential care options in the district for families who want elderly relatives to remain in the area.

Graceville is listed on Google Maps as a nursing home and carries a 4.8-star rating. Reviewer impressions describe good food, attentive staff, and a comfortable environment, reflecting a facility that has built local word-of-mouth goodwill. Beyond the Google listing, the home keeps a low online profile and does not appear to operate a public website, so granular detail on capacity, clinical capabilities, staffing ratios, and pricing is not publicly available.

Families considering Graceville should reach out by phone (+60 12-611 9093) and arrange an in-person visit to assess whether the facility is the right fit. During the visit it is worth asking about the total number of residents currently housed, the qualifications of overnight caregiving staff, what care dependency levels the home accepts (including bedridden or dementia residents), monthly fees and what is included, and whether the facility holds a current JKM registration. A visit also provides the clearest picture of the physical environment and day-to-day atmosphere.

Its bilingual Chinese–English name and the rural township setting suggest a centre primarily serving the local Sungai Mati and surrounding agricultural community, where purpose-built nursing care options are scarce and most families have historically relied on informal home-based care. The contact number on record is +60 12-611 9093.

Graceville has a minimal online footprint — no website, no Facebook page, and no publicly available detail about bed count, staffing structure, or the specific care services offered. Its presence on Google Maps under the nursing home category is the primary public record. This is common for smaller community homes in semi-rural Johor districts, where word-of-mouth referral and direct phone contact remain the main routes to admission. The home's Chinese name (美好疗养中心, meaning "Beautiful Wellness Care Centre") implies a Chinese-managed operation, and the Sungai Mati demographic mix would support Mandarin and possibly Cantonese as working languages alongside Bahasa Malaysia.

Families in the Muar–Ledang corridor who are exploring placement options should contact Graceville directly to establish current availability, the range of dependency levels accepted, nursing staff qualifications, and what arrangements exist for residents who need acute medical attention — the nearest district hospital is Hospital Muar, approximately 20–25 km away. Because so little is publicly disclosed, an in-person visit before any commitment is strongly advised, allowing families to assess the physical environment, meet the care team, and ask about JKM licensing status.

editorial_summary:
WCC WoundCare and Nursing Centre is a nursing home in Taman Sungai Abong, Muar, with a specific specialisation in wound care that sets it apart from most community nursing homes in the area. Wound management — including chronic wound care for conditions such as diabetic foot ulcers, pressure injuries, and post-surgical wounds — requires trained clinical staff and appropriate dressing supplies, and a centre that names this as a core competency signals a clinical capability beyond basic nursing home care. The facility can be reached at +60 13-379 4428.

The centre keeps a low online profile, so details such as bed count, staffing establishment, therapy offerings, and pricing are not publicly available and are best gathered directly. What the listing does confirm is the combination of wound care and general nursing home services, making it a potentially strong option for elderly patients who have been discharged from hospital with an open wound, pressure sore, or post-amputation site that needs ongoing clinical management alongside their daily residential care.

For families or discharge planners assessing whether WCC is an appropriate placement, the key questions to ask upfront are: what wound care qualifications the nursing staff hold (e.g. wound care certification, stoma care training), whether a doctor visits regularly to review complex wounds or whether specialist input requires a hospital trip, what wound types and dressing protocols the centre routinely handles, and what the monthly rate covers versus what attracts additional charges. An in-person visit — with the opportunity to see the wound care setup and speak with the clinical lead — is strongly recommended before confirming a placement.

The group self-describes as a "Home for the aged and physically disabled," and its long operating history makes it one of the more established private nursing home providers in the Johor Bahru southern corridor. Green Acres advertises 24-hour nursing care, a doctor-on-call arrangement, physiotherapy, and ambulance service as its core clinical capabilities. The facility accepts residents with physical disability and age-related dependencies, and the care_types data records it as covering both nursing home and rehabilitation services — consistent with the physiotherapy provision. The group has a Facebook presence under "Green Acres Elderly Care Centre" with patient testimonials citing attentive staff, a clean environment, and good food. While specific bed counts are not published, the multi-site structure and 27-year operating history indicate meaningful capacity across the two JB locations. The phone on record for the Bukit Kempas site is +60 7-221 3393.

Families evaluating Green Acres should ask about the specific dependency level and mobility profile accepted at each site, the frequency and coverage model for doctor visits, and what the rehabilitation pathway looks like for post-stroke or post-surgical residents. Pricing is not published online, so a direct enquiry to enquiry@greenacreshome.com.my or the phone line is the right starting point. Given the multi-site operation, it is also useful to confirm which branch a prospective resident would be placed at and whether transfers between sites occur as needs change.

Ummi Naura Care Centre (UNCC), also styled Pusat Jagaan dan Pendidikan Warga Emas, is a Muslim-oriented residential care centre in Muar, Johor. It operates from No. 19, Jalan Murni 15, Taman Putera Murni, off Jalan Salleh, 84000 Muar, and is contactable on 013-979 6861 or umminauracarecentre@gmail.com. The centre began operations in 2019 and is registered with Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat. Its stated scope is 24-hour residential care for older adults including stroke survivors, bedridden and paralysed residents, abandoned elderly, cancer patients, persons with disabilities (OKU), and mental-health patients whose condition is controlled.

Day-to-day care is delivered by nursing staff described by the centre as experienced in handling dependent residents. The current admission policy as published by the home is male residents only, on either permanent or short-term placement. The centre sits in the Muslim social-care tradition — the name Ummi Naura and its activity calendar (e.g. Ramadhan infaq drives, donation campaigns through ToyyibPay and Sumbangan.com) signal a Muslim, Malay-medium environment, though formal halal certification status is not separately published.

Families considering UNCC should confirm bed availability for the specific dependency level needed (ambulant vs bedridden vs PEG-fed), ask for the JKM licence number in writing, and clarify what is included in the monthly fee versus charged extra (diapers, milk feeds, dressings, doctor visits, hospital escort). Because the centre takes mental-health and OKU cases alongside frail elderly, it is reasonable to ask how residents are grouped by ward and how behaviourally complex residents are managed. Visit before signing.

Cozzi Confinement Center Muar (娘家精致月子中心) is one of several branches operated by the Cozzi group across Johor and Kuala Lumpur, and sits at 1A, Taman Jaya Baru, 84000 Muar. As the Chinese-style name makes explicit, this is a postnatal confinement centre for new mothers rather than a nursing home for elderly residents — it provides care during the roughly 28-30 days following childbirth, a practice deeply rooted in Chinese postpartum tradition known as zuo yue zi. The Muar branch was established as part of the group's Johor expansion and serves families in Muar and surrounding Johor districts who prefer a structured confinement facility over home-based care.

Cozzi's group-wide model — which applies at the Muar location — centres on 24-hour professional nursing and nanny care for newborns, midwife-guided support for mothers, daily meal plans developed by Chinese medicine practitioners and dietitians (five meals per day plus beverages), postnatal massages, herbal baths, exercise programmes, and regular paediatrician visits. Rooms are air-conditioned with attached bathrooms and WiFi. Cozzi's two standard packages across its network are Deluxe (RM 17,800 for 28 days) and Premium (RM 20,800 for 28 days), though branch-specific pricing should be confirmed directly.

Families planning to use the Muar branch should confirm availability well in advance, as confinement centres frequently fill up months before due dates. Specific questions worth asking: whether a lactation consultant is on roster, what the neonatal escalation pathway is if the baby needs hospital review, and whether the room rate includes all meals and supplements or whether some items carry surcharges. The centre can be reached at +60 12-712 6705.

Golden Age Care Centre Batu Pahat is the chain's south-Johor branch, at Lot 7887 Jalan Bukit Pasir in Batu Pahat town — a larger-town setting than the Tangkak sister branch, with a different competitive landscape and a different resident profile. Batu Pahat is well served by KPJ Batu Pahat Specialist Hospital and Hospital Sultanah Nora Ismail, so the home sits in a corridor where post-discharge rehab placements are a more realistic part of the casemix. GACC was founded in 2004, is licensed under the Akta Pusat Jagaan and rated 4 stars by JKM, with sister branches in Tangkak, Muar and Melaka.

The Batu Pahat branch competes with several other private nursing homes in the wider south-Johor area, which means the questions that matter on a visit are sharper than at a small-town branch with no real alternatives. Ask which clinical capabilities differ between Batu Pahat and the chain's other sites — speech and occupational therapy depth, dementia-specific staff, and whether physiotherapy is delivered by a full-time on-site therapist or one rotating between branches. The chain advertises CCTV, a 24/7 call system, scheduled hospital transport, and operating languages of English, Mandarin and Malay; in Batu Pahat the trilingual capability matches the town's mixed Chinese-Malay demographic well. Confirm how the home's hospital transport works in practice with KPJ Batu Pahat versus the public Sultanah Nora Ismail — that pathway matters when a resident decompensates.

A reasonable shortlist option for south-Johor families looking for a JKM-rated chain home rather than a single-operator residence, particularly where rehab after stroke or surgery is part of the placement reason. Pricing is not published; call the Batu Pahat branch directly and ask whether the quoted rate is genuine to that branch or a chain default, what is bundled versus extra each month, current bed availability, and how end-of-life or escalation cases are handled internally before any hospital transfer.

Cozzi Confinement Center Sri Jaya (娘家精致月子中心) is the Batu Pahat branch of the Cozzi group, located at 1, Jalan Rotan Utama, Taman Sri Jaya, 83000 Batu Pahat. Like its Muar sibling, this is a postnatal confinement centre designed to care for mothers and newborns during the traditional one-month recovery period following childbirth — it is not an elderly nursing home. The Sri Jaya branch forms part of Cozzi's cluster of Batu Pahat locations, which also includes centres in Parit Besar, Tanah Merah, and Yong Peng, giving the group a strong footprint across this district of Johor.

The Sri Jaya centre follows Cozzi's group-wide care model: 24-hour nursing and nanny care for newborns, weekly visits from a Chinese medicine practitioner, postnatal massage and herbal bath programmes for mothers, a structured six-meal-per-day dietary plan incorporating tonic soups and confinement-appropriate nutrition, and regular paediatrician visits. Rooms are air-conditioned with attached bathrooms, nursing chairs, and baby cots, set within a facility designed for natural light and a calm environment. Operating hours for enquiries are listed as 09:30–18:00 daily, and the centre is contactable at +60 12-756 6705. Group-wide packages run approximately RM 17,800 (Deluxe) to RM 20,800 (Premium) for 28 days.

Families in Batu Pahat considering the Sri Jaya centre should book well in advance given how quickly confinement centres fill around due dates. It is worth confirming on contact whether the specific Sri Jaya branch pricing aligns with the group's standard packages, whether a lactation consultant is available on-site, and what the neonatal emergency escalation pathway looks like. As with the Muar branch, this facility is classified in the sheet as a nursing home but is operationally a postnatal confinement centre — families seeking long-term elderly care should look elsewhere.

editorial_summary:
Cozzi Confinement Center at Tanah Merah is one of several Batu Pahat–area branches operated by the Cozzi group, a multi-location postnatal care chain active across Johor since 2017. The Tanah Merah branch sits at 12 Jalan Matahari in the Tanah Merah township of Batu Pahat District, offering residential postpartum packages that blend traditional Chinese confinement practices with modern nursing support. The centre is Chinese-run and operates primarily in Mandarin and English, and it is positioned as a premium option for new mothers rather than a facility for elderly or medically complex residents.

Cozzi's standard offering is a 28-day confinement package (priced chain-wide at RM 17,800–RM 20,800 depending on tier) covering private accommodation, 24-hour nursing and nanny care for mother and newborn, five TCM-guided meals daily, postpartum massage and body-wrap treatments, and structured activities. The Tanah Merah branch shares this template with sibling sites in Yong Peng, Sri Jaya, Parit Besar, and Muar. Medical oversight is by on-call arrangement rather than on-site physicians, consistent with the confinement-centre model across Malaysia.

Families researching elder care should note that Cozzi does not offer nursing home, assisted living, or elderly day care services — the facility is exclusively for postpartum mothers and newborns. If your search led here while looking for an elder care bed in Batu Pahat, you will need to look at other providers in the district. For families seeking postnatal confinement care, the Tanah Merah branch is worth a direct call to confirm room availability and current package inclusions, as promotional add-ons change seasonally.

Pusat Jagaan Impian Syimah (Jalan Kemuncak) is a small, privately run care home in Kampung Nong Chik, Johor Bahru. A Sunway-reported event noted 61 elderly residents and 23 bedridden among the population served, indicating the centre takes high-dependency residents. The faith-based environment and consistent community goodwill suggest a genuinely caring ethos at the management level.

The 3.5-star Google Maps rating — below the JB average for licensed care homes — is a flag that warrants honest disclosure. The text of the 11 reviews driving that score could not be independently retrieved. What is known is that this is a small, home-based setup in a kampung residential address, relying heavily on donor support for basics such as diapers and cleaning supplies. These characteristics are consistent with care homes that sometimes attract criticism for under-resourcing, though no inspection violations or regulatory actions appeared in any source.

Families considering this centre should treat the below-average rating seriously and ask direct questions on-site: staff-to-resident ratio, nursing qualifications, how bedridden residents are managed overnight, and whether a current JKM licence is displayed. For families needing a fully staffed clinical environment, a higher-rated licensed home with documented RN coverage is a safer choice. Families seeking a small, Muslim-run community environment at a modest cost may find this worth a personal visit with due diligence.

title: Hang Nursing Home (Gelang Patah Branch)
editorial_summary:
Hang Nursing Home operates at least one branch in the Gelang Patah area of western Johor Bahru, near the Second Link causeway — a district that has expanded rapidly with Iskandar Malaysia development and carries a substantial Chinese-Malaysian residential population. The Facebook page at pusatjagaanhang confirms the facility exists; the 'branch' designation in available data implies at least one other location exists, though no other branch could be identified online. The contact number is +60 12-766 0927.

No address, services breakdown, bed count, pricing, or JKM licence number was retrievable from online sources. The name 'Hang' is most plausibly a Chinese family surname, and the operator is likely a small family business serving the local Gelang Patah community. Hang Nursing Home does not appear in AGECOPE, ielder.asia, nursinghome.my, or any directory article reviewed, and carries zero Google reviews.

Families should call ahead for directions, confirm which branch they are visiting, and request sight of the JKM licence certificate on arrival. Gelang Patah's rapid development means there may be limited competing care options in the immediate area, making this a local-community option worth investigating for families resident in the western JB corridor.

title: Green Garden Care Center Sdn Bhd
visiting_hours: Monday: 9 to 11 AM, 1 to 5 PM; Tuesday: 9 to 11 AM, 1 to 5 PM; Wednesday: 9 to 11 AM, 1 to 5 PM; Thursday: 9 to 11 AM, 1 to 5 PM; Friday: 9 to 11 AM, 1 to 5 PM; Saturday: 9 to 11 AM, 1 to 5 PM; Sunday: 9 to 11 AM, 1 to 5 PM
editorial_summary:
Green Garden Care Centre (绿园乐龄养生之家) sits in Pontian, near the Pulai Bridge and the Kukup area — a quieter, more rural stretch of Johor that suits families wanting their relative away from JB city traffic. The centre is run as Green Garden Care Centre Sdn. Bhd. and has 60 beds across double and triple rooms, which puts it in the mid-sized bracket for Johor.

Services are explicitly described: 24-hour nursing care, post-discharge rehabilitation with treadmills and step machines, respite care, and dedicated medical transport. The grounds include walking trails, foot massage paths and accessible bathrooms with flat floors — features built around mobility recovery rather than pure custodial care. There is a stated focus on stroke rehabilitation, plus Tai Chi and chess sessions during the week. Operating language appears to be Mandarin and English; visiting hours are 9am to 5pm.

A reasonable choice for families whose relative is recovering from a stroke or surgery and benefits from rehab equipment plus space to walk, particularly Mandarin-speaking families in southwest Johor. Things to verify: JKM licence, current admission ratios and prices for double versus triple rooms, doctor visit frequency (the site doesn't state a fixed schedule), and whether the rehab equipment is used in supervised sessions or simply available. Confirm transport coverage if the family is JB-based — Pontian is a 45-minute drive.

Pusat Jagaan Teduhan Zafrah (Warga Emas & OKU) LELAKI
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Teduhan Zafrah (Warga Emas & OKU) LELAKI is a nursing home located in Muar, Johor. It holds a 4.9/5 rating on Google Maps from 12 reviews.
visiting_hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Teduhan Zafrah is a private care home in Taman Desa Sepayung, Muar, specialising in care for elderly men (Warga Emas) and male persons with disabilities (OKU). Founded on 9 September 2013 and run by a husband-and-wife team — Pn. Nurfarah and En. Ariffin — the facility was built on a personal mission to care for seniors and disabled residents as one would care for family. The male-only admission policy (reflected in the full name, which includes "LELAKI") means the home is purpose-structured for that cohort, allowing a focused care environment.

Teduhan Zafrah operates under dual oversight by JKM and the Ministry of Health, and nursing staff are registered with Lembaga Juruawat Malaysia. The facility offers geriatric and palliative care alongside daily structured programmes: religious classes, group health exercises, physiotherapy and rehabilitation sessions, and vocational training activities. The holistic model addresses physical, mental, emotional, and social needs in a single setting. Pricing starts from RM1,300 per month for residential care, with a RM200 registration fee and day-care options from RM100 per day; final fees are set after an individual needs assessment. The home partly supplements operating costs through public donations and grants from Baitulmal and JKM.

Given the facility's community-driven funding model and modest resident numbers (recorded at more than ten), prospective families should confirm current capacity and vacancy at the time of enquiry. The primary contacts are Pn. Nurfarah (019-685 5649) and En. Ariffin (013-311 9462). Questions worth raising include the current number of residents, the scope of palliative care available for end-of-life situations, and the process for managing residents whose medical needs escalate beyond what the home can support.

title: Amitabha Foundation (Amitabha Malaysia)
visiting_hours: Monday: 8 AM to 4 PM; Tuesday: 8 AM to 4 PM; Wednesday: 8 AM to 4 PM; Thursday: 8 AM to 4 PM; Friday: 8 AM to 4 PM; Saturday: 8 AM to 4 PM; Sunday: Closed
editorial_summary:
Amitabha Foundation (Amitabha Malaysia) is a Buddhist-affiliated charity headquartered at Taman Tasek in Johor Bahru, established in June 1998 under the motto "Help the Needy and Relieve the Distressed." Its elder care arm is one of four programme pillars alongside a children's home, a haemodialysis centre, and a financial assistance scheme. The head office at 59B Jalan Glasiar doubles as the administrative hub for elder care operations, with the foundation explicitly stating that its elder homes accept chronically ill, abandoned, and destitute elderly persons — including those discharged from hospital with nowhere to go. All ethnic and religious backgrounds are welcomed, and the foundation emphasises that contributions are fully channelled to care rather than administration.

The foundation's elder care capacity is spread across two residential homes — one in Country Heights and one in Kuchai Lama, Kuala Lumpur — while the Johor Bahru base manages outreach, referrals, and day-care coordination. The facility is recorded as a day-care service in Johor Bahru, likely serving seniors in the Taman Tasek catchment area through structured daytime programmes that complement rather than replace residential care. The foundation also runs a haemodialysis clinic at the same Jalan Glasiar address, making it a genuinely multi-service welfare hub. Operational funding comes from recycling proceeds and corporate sponsors, keeping the charitable elder care model financially independent from residents' families.

Families considering Amitabha Malaysia for a day-care placement or residential referral should contact the head office directly at +60 7-213 2448 to understand current availability and the intake process for each service tier. Because the organisation operates on a welfare rather than commercial model, admission typically involves a social welfare assessment rather than a simple fee schedule. It is worth asking specifically which services are available at the Johor Bahru site versus the residential homes in KL, whether transport assistance exists for day-care attendees, and how medical needs above basic health monitoring are managed.
services: meals, activities
