The Tamil-language cultural orientation may be an asset for Indian families in Rawang seeking a culturally familiar environment — confirm the primary language of caregiving when you call +60 16-448 2421.

title: Love and Care Village
longitude: Love and Care Village is a nursing home located in Taman Ampang Utama, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 7 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Love and Care Village is a nursing home located in Taman Ampang Utama, a residential estate in Ampang on the Selangor–KL border. The area sits in a well-connected part of greater Ampang, close to Jalan Ampang and within reach of Ampang Puteri Specialist Hospital and Hospital Ampang. The facility is listed on Google Maps as a nursing home with a 5-star rating from seven reviews — a perfect score that, on a small sample, reflects early positive feedback but should be read cautiously rather than as a definitive quality signal.

Beyond the Maps listing, Love and Care Village has no publicly accessible website or Facebook page that surfaced in research. No capacity figures, care programme details, pricing, or staffing information have been independently published. The name suggests a residential, village-style model oriented toward warm community living rather than a purely clinical nursing approach, though this cannot be confirmed from available sources. Contact is through a mobile number (+60 12-353 9035), which is typical of smaller, owner-operated homes.

Families should call to determine whether the facility caters to ambulant elderly, nursing-dependent residents, or both; what medical cover (if any) is on site or on-call; and whether a JKM licence is held. The Ampang Utama location, while residential and relatively quiet, may also be of interest to families based in Ampang, Wangsa Maju, or Keramat who want proximity to KL without the congestion of the city centre. Given the thin public footprint, an in-person visit before committing is strongly advisable.

title: Ampang Old Folks Home
longitude: Ampang Old Folks Home is a retirement home located in Kampung Baru Ampang, Selangor. It holds a 4.5/5 rating on Google Maps from 4 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Ampang Old Folks Home is one of the older recorded care facilities in Kampung Baru Ampang, 68000 Ampang, Selangor — an address that has appeared in social welfare directories going back at least to the mid-2000s. The Wikimapia record and legacy directory listings confirm its physical presence in the area, and the Google listing gives a phone of 03-4291 8978 with a 4.5 rating from four reviews and a retirement home classification.

As with many long-standing community welfare homes, public documentation is sparse. No website or social media presence could be located. There is no verified data on bed count, staffing, care scope, pricing, language of operations, or JKM licensing status. The retirement home classification on Google suggests it may cater to more mobile or semi-independent residents rather than high-dependency nursing cases, but this is not confirmed.

Families should call to verify the home is still actively accepting residents — legacy listings sometimes persist after facilities have closed or significantly downsized. Ask about current capacity, the level of nursing care available for any medical needs your family member has, whether meals and laundry are included, and what the monthly fee structure looks like. A site visit is strongly recommended before committing.

title: Convee Elderly Care Center
longitude: Convee Elderly Care Center is a nursing home located in Taman Seraya, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 37 reviews.
care_types: Nursing home, Adult day care center, Home health care service
editorial_summary:
Convee Elderly Care Center is located at Jalan Bunga Tanjung 22, Taman Seraya, Ampang — a quiet, established residential neighbourhood in the Ampang-Kuala Lumpur fringe that the Google Maps listing attributes to Selangor. The centre holds a 4.8 rating from 37 reviews, an unusually high score that suggests consistently positive feedback from families using the service. Its listed care types span three distinct service lines: nursing home residential care, adult day care, and home health care — making it one of the few smaller operators in the area advertising all three under one roof.

The facility's website describes care packages ranging from active-senior day care through to 24-hour residential care for bedridden residents, with packages described as personalised to individual needs. Regular health checks by doctors and licensed medical practitioners are listed as part of the care programme. The centre is accessible by bus (route 23 is mentioned) and has external parking, with ramp access indicated — practical details that matter for families bringing in residents who use wheelchairs.

Detailed service specifications — bed count, pricing, staffing composition, JKM licence number — are not published publicly. The home health care offering is particularly worth probing: ask whether nurses are dispatched for wound care, IV administration, or purely companion-level assistance, as the scope varies widely between operators. Families who want day care only should clarify start and end times and whether transport is provided. The strong Google rating is encouraging but independent verification through a site visit remains the essential step before placing a family member.

title: Glory Living Care Home
longitude: Glory Living Care Home is an assisted living centre located in Taman Hillview, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 11 reviews.
care_types: Aged care, Assisted living facility, Retirement home
editorial_summary:
Glory Living Care Home is positioned in the Taman Sri Ukay / Taman Hillview area of Ampang — a hillside residential enclave off Jalan Ulu Kelang, about 15 minutes from the Ampang town centre. The facility describes itself as a retirement home and assisted living centre focused on seniors who value independence, friendship, and a healthy lifestyle, with an emphasis on a clean, hygienic, and home-like environment suited to those who need support but wish to remain socially engaged. Contact is via a mobile number (+60 10-962 8683), and the facility has a Facebook page but no independent website with detailed service listings.

Based on its Google listing category of aged care, assisted living, and retirement home, the facility appears to cater to relatively mobile or semi-independent residents rather than high-dependency nursing cases. The Sri Ukay hillside setting offers a quieter, greener environment compared to urban care homes, which some families find preferable for residents with mild cognitive decline or those in earlier stages of frailty. The 4.8 Google rating across 11 reviews is positive, though the small sample limits confidence. No bed count, pricing, or staffing details are publicly available.

With an online footprint this limited, families should visit in person before committing. Key questions: Is registered nursing staff present around the clock, or is this primarily caregiver-staffed? What conditions can the home manage — incontinence, tube feeding, wound care — versus what would trigger a transfer? The Ampang area has reasonable hospital access via Hospital Ampang and several private facilities along the Ampang-KL corridor, but confirm the home's emergency protocol and hospital relationships before placement.

title: Mona Elder Care | Nursing Home in Jalan Sri Kemuning Lembah Jaya Selatan Ampang
longitude: Mona Elder Care | Nursing Home in Jalan Sri Kemuning Lembah Jaya Selatan Ampang is a nursing home located in Kampung Lembah Jaya Selatan, Selangor. It holds a 4.7/5 rating on Google Maps from 15 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care's Ampang branch is situated at 2, Jalan Sri Kemuning, Lembah Jaya Selatan, 68000 Ampang — a residential pocket just south of the main Ampang town corridor, with reasonable access to Hospital Ampang and the broader Ampang-Cheras clinical network. This is one of at least four Mona Elder Care locations in the Klang Valley, sharing a common brand, clinical identity, and service menu with the Pandan Perdana branch. The group's 24/7 model and its emphasis on medically complex cases — stroke, coma, tube feeding, post-operative recovery, and tracheostomy care — position this as a nursing home that can take on cases hospitals discharge earlier than families might expect.

The services page of monaeldercare.com lists nine primary care types offered across the network: respite care, palliative care, home nursing, stroke and coma care, senior citizens care, handicapped care, physiotherapy, dementia care, and day care. Clinical procedures documented include bedsore dressing, NG tube changes, colostomy bag management, Foley catheter care, tracheostomy care, post-operative care, and cerebral palsy support. This is a more clinically detailed public disclosure than most similarly-sized Selangor nursing homes provide, which is useful for families assessing whether a specific care need can be met. The facility describes a structured 24-hour shift system across morning, afternoon, evening, and night shifts.

With a 4.7-star rating from 15 Google reviewers, this Ampang branch performs slightly better on volume than the Pandan Perdana location's identical 17-review base. As with all Mona Elder Care sites, no pricing, bed count, or staff ratio figures are publicly available — request these by contacting +60 16-616 4535. Language coverage by caregivers is not disclosed; given the Ampang location's mixed Chinese, Malay, and Indian demographic profile, ask directly about Mandarin, Cantonese, and Tamil capability. Confirm the JKM licence number and ask whether the Ampang site has its own registered licence or operates under a group licence.

title: Noble Care Malaysia Ampang
longitude: Noble Care Malaysia Ampang is a nursing home located in Taman Ampang Utama, Selangor. It holds a 4.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 8 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Malaysia Ampang is one of the network's Klang Valley branches, situated at 537 Jalan 5, Taman Ampang Utama, 68000 Ampang, Selangor. Ampang Utama is a long-established residential neighbourhood with good access to hospitals — Ampang Hospital is approximately 5 kilometres away, and Gleneagles Hospital Kuala Lumpur is reachable within 15 minutes — making this a relatively well-serviced location for a nursing home. The Noble Care brand operates more than 15 branches nationally and has over 16 years in the sector, positioning itself as a professional, multi-location aged care provider.

The Ampang branch offers the group's standard clinical menu: 24-hour nursing supervision, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and respite support, physiotherapy, and skilled nursing procedures including Foley's catheter care, tracheostomy management, nasogastric tube care, colostomy bag changing, bedsore dressing, and post-operative care. The website also lists capability for cerebral palsy care and care for individuals with stroke and cancer diagnoses. The facility describes itself as providing a home-like environment with nutritious meals and social activities for residents.

Bed count and room-type details are not published for this branch, and no pricing is listed — families should contact Noble Care directly at +60 16-278 6993, noting that this number is shared with the Rawang HQ. Ask to speak specifically with the Ampang team to confirm current availability, local pricing, and staffing levels. As with all chain operators, quality can vary between branches, so a site visit to the Ampang location specifically is worthwhile rather than drawing conclusions from the network's overall reputation. Confirm whether dementia residents have a dedicated unit or are integrated into the general ward.

title: Aliz Nursing Care
longitude: Aliz Nursing Care is a home care service located in Tadisma Business Park, Selangor. It holds a 4.5/5 rating on Google Maps from 8 reviews.
care_types: Aged care, Career guidance service, Home health care service
editorial_summary:
Aliz Nursing Care is based at 12A Jalan Badminton 13/29, Tadisma Business Park, Seksyen 13, Shah Alam — a commercial shopoffice park in central Shah Alam. Unlike most facilities on this guide, Aliz presents primarily as a home-based nursing care provider rather than a residential nursing home: the website and public listings emphasise one-to-one nursing care delivered at the patient's own home, with the commercial address serving as the operational base. Google Maps categorises the service as aged care, career guidance, and home health care, consistent with a hybrid model that may include some centre-based care alongside outreach.

Aliz's stated service range covers aged and elderly care, post-surgery recovery, disability care, injury care, personal care assistance, and 24-hour in-home and hospital nursing. The company indicates it employs local registered nurses trained to assist with activities of daily living, framing one-to-one home-based care as superior to institutional placement for both patient outcomes and family peace of mind. The emphasis on home care means Aliz is likely a good fit for families managing a parent who wishes to remain at home but requires professional nursing input beyond what a domestic helper can provide.

Pricing is not published. Families should call to understand whether Aliz offers any centre-based residential placements or exclusively provides nursing staff for home deployment. Confirm the qualifications and registration of nurses supplied, the minimum engagement duration, and how continuity of care is managed if the assigned nurse is unavailable. For families in Shah Alam, Subang Jaya, or nearby Petaling Jaya, Aliz represents a potentially convenient option for post-hospitalisation home nursing or ongoing personal care management.

title: Golden Years Senior Residence Kota Kemuning Shah Alam
longitude: Golden Years Senior Residence Kota Kemuning Shah Alam is a nursing home located in Kota Kemuning, Selangor. It holds a 4.7/5 rating on Google Maps from 24 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Golden Years Senior Residence in Kota Kemuning is a medically oriented nursing home set within a quiet residential pocket of Shah Alam at 4, Jalan Anggerik Aranda 31/170F — a biophilic environment with gardens designed to reduce the institutional feel that families often dread. The facility was founded by healthcare professionals: the clinical leadership team includes a GP, an Internal Medicine Specialist with over 15 years of experience, a Registered Nurse Matron with 35-plus years in clinical care, and an in-house physiotherapist. A second location in Bandar Kinrara, Puchong, and a home nursing service covering surrounding areas extend the group's reach.

Services at Kota Kemuning are structured around four care modes: long-term residential care for chronic and progressive conditions, short-term post-hospital recovery, adult day care (8:30am–6pm weekdays, RM2,000–2,300/month), and home nursing visits within the local area at RM30/hour. Monthly residential fees are tiered by dependency: RM2,950 for low, RM3,450 for medium, and RM3,950 for high, with short-term stays priced at RM220–300/day. Occupational therapy is available at RM200/session, physiotherapy at RM100/session for non-package residents. A telemedical doctor standby service is listed for acute medical needs, and residents receive daily vital sign monitoring and annual health screenings.

A RM3,500 refundable deposit is required on admission for residential care (RM2,000 for day care). Families should ask about current bed availability — the Kota Kemuning facility appears to be a multi-unit operation — and clarify what "standby telemedical doctor" means in practice versus an on-site physician. Confirm whether the Internal Medicine Specialist provides regular in-person consultations and at what frequency. The Google rating (4.7 stars, 24 reviews) and the published pricing transparency make this one of the more accountable small operators in Shah Alam.

title: Care Concierge | Care Luxe Ampang
longitude: Care Concierge | Care Luxe Ampang is a nursing home located in Kampung Datuk Keramat, Selangor.
care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living facility, Occupational therapist
editorial_summary:
Care Luxe is the premium assisted living branch of Care Concierge, Malaysia's largest specialist senior care group. Occupying Level 3 of Oakwood Hotel & Residence at 222 Jalan Ampang, in the Kampung Datuk Keramat corridor near the Ampang–Kuala Lumpur boundary, it is the most hotel-forward of Care Concierge's several Klang Valley residences. The concept deliberately merges clinical nursing with hotel-grade hospitality — chef-prepared meals, concierge assistance, and housekeeping sit alongside 24-hour care support — targeting seniors who want comfort and privacy without compromising on medical oversight. Primary care covers independent retirement living, post-surgery recovery, stroke rehabilitation, diabetes management, cancer support, dementia, Parkinson's, and palliative care.

The website lists five care packages ranging from RM10,000 per month for Independent Living to RM18,000 for Personal 1-to-1 Care, with a short-stay or respite option at RM500 per night. Each resident receives a personalised care plan, weekly doctor visits, daily health monitoring, physiotherapy, and pharmacy medication reviews. Care Concierge holds B Corporation certification and the group operates a proprietary mobile app used by nursing staff for real-time vital monitoring and care tracking. The broader group employs certified nurses, therapists, and doctors across all its residences, and the Ampang location benefits from the same centralised clinical governance as the rest of the network.

Care Luxe sits at the higher end of the private elder-care market in Malaysia, so the pricing warrants a direct conversation to understand exactly which clinical procedures are included at each tier versus billed separately. Because it operates within a hotel building, ask about how medical emergencies are handled on-site and what the transfer protocol to a nearby hospital looks like. Proximity to Gleneagles KL and DEMC Specialist Hospital Ampang is a practical plus. Visiting arrangements, room sizes, and whether the hotel's residential-tower neighbours affect the care environment are worth clarifying during a site visit.

title: Woodrose Senior Residences 2 Seksyen 11 (Male - Syariah Compliant)
longitude: Woodrose Senior Residences 2 Seksyen 11 (Male - Syariah Compliant) is a nursing home located in Seksyen 11, Selangor. It holds a 4.2/5 rating on Google Maps from 26 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences 2 is the male wing of Woodrose Healthcare's Seksyen 11 campus in Shah Alam, Selangor — a pair of adjacent facilities that together form what is likely the most established Shariah-compliant nursing home operation in the Klang Valley. Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd describes itself as "the most well established nursing home in Selangor" and emerged from a merger between Zara Aisyah Care Centre and MLL Sdn Bhd. Residence 2, on Jalan Gamelan, is specifically designated for male residents and operates under Shariah oversight developed with an in-house Ustaz who shaped the facility's SOPs for food, personal care, daily activities, and spiritual guidance.

The Shariah compliance framework at Woodrose is designed to address the genuine clinical and religious tension of having non-mahram caregivers attend to opposite-gender residents — a significant concern for observant Muslim families. By separating the homes by gender, Woodrose allows for same-gender caregiving, halal-certified food, Islamic daily routines, and religious programming including observances during Ramadan. Services across the Woodrose network include dementia care, Alzheimer's care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, physiotherapy, day care, and short-term respite. The campus features indoor and outdoor activity spaces, and rooms at Residence 2 are described as spacious with good access facilities.

Woodrose is not the cheapest option in Shah Alam and is clearly positioned at the mid-to-upper end of the market. Families should ask about specific capacity at Residence 2 versus Residence 1, the current Ustaz oversight structure, waiting times for admission, and pricing tiers by dependency level. It is worth confirming whether the facility holds a valid JKM licence and whether there is an on-call or visiting physician arrangement — details not published on the website.

title: Sayang Nursing Home Care
editorial_summary:
Sayang Nursing Home Care lists its address as the second floor of Anggerik Mall, Seksyen 14, 40000 Shah Alam, Selangor — a commercial mall location rather than a residential care property. This suggests the business may operate as a home care placement or coordination service rather than a live-in residential nursing facility. Phone: +60 3-5519 7501.

No website, reviews or published care details are currently available. The nature of the operation — residential care or home-based care services — is not confirmed through publicly available information.

Families should contact the Shah Alam number directly to clarify whether the service dispatches caregivers to clients' homes, operates a residential facility at a separate address, or provides both. This will help determine whether it meets your specific care requirements.

title: ZARA AISYAH CARE CENTRE (ZACC)
longitude: ZARA AISYAH CARE CENTRE (ZACC) is a care home located in Seksyen U11, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 68 reviews.
editorial_summary:
ZARA AISYAH CARE CENTRE (ZACC) was founded in 2015 by Wan Salbiah Mior Yahya and is based at Jalan Gunung Tahan U11/10A, Bukit Bandaraya Shah Alam, Selangor — a residential area in the Seksyen U11 corridor of Shah Alam. It is one of Malaysia's more prominently known Islamic-oriented elderly care providers, positioning itself as a pioneer Shariah-compliant care specialist. The company reports growth from two staff caring for two residents at founding to over 300 employees and more than 500 patients served across its network, which now includes a second site branded ZACC MYTOWN.

ZACC offers 24/7 residential nursing care, dementia care, and assisted daily living in what it describes as a luxury environment. Spiritual care is a core differentiator: daily prayers, Quran recitation, and Islamic routines are structured into the daily schedule. Medical support, physiotherapy, and counselling are included in the care model. Room types cover single with attached toilet, twin with attached toilet, and triple configurations. Monthly fees are reported in the RM 3,001–5,000 range, with a comprehensive inclusions package covering 24-hour care, accommodation, housekeeping, laundry, catering, monthly medical review, and security. Visiting hours are structured and booking-based (9 am–2 pm and 6 pm–8 pm, daily including public holidays).

ZACC's strong Islamic identity means it is an excellent fit for Muslim families who want religious observance integrated into care — but families of non-Muslim residents should clarify how the religious programme is structured and whether it is optional or universal. Ask about the nurse-to-resident ratio, the qualifications of the physiotherapy and dementia care staff, and whether the RM 3,001–5,000 range reflects the U11 branch specifically or is a network-wide figure. Given the booking-only visiting policy, also confirm the escalation pathway for families who need to reach staff urgently outside visiting hours.

title: Woodrose Senior Residences 3 Seksyen 13 (Female - Syariah compliant)
longitude: Woodrose Senior Residences 3 Seksyen 13 (Female - Syariah compliant) is a nursing home located in Seksyen 13, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 5 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences is a growing Shah Alam-based operator that emerged from the merger of Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC) and MLL Sdn Bhd, positioning itself as Selangor's leading Syariah-compliant elderly care brand. The Seksyen 13 branch — listed as Woodrose Senior Residences 3, reachable at +60 12-709 8102 — is specifically designated as a female-only, Syariah-compliant residence, making it one of the few nursing facilities in the Klang Valley explicitly designed for Muslim women who require religious and gender-sensitive care. The head office sits in Seksyen 9, Shah Alam, with other branches in Seksyen 11.

Woodrose's Syariah compliance framework is documented on their website and covers four pillars: same-gender medical and caregiving teams assigned to female residents; halal-certified food, beverages, and pharmaceuticals; prayer facilities with ablution assistance and tayammum provision for bedridden residents; and management operating on Islamic ethical principles. Across their network, the operator offers residential nursing care, dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, physiotherapy, respite care, and senior day care. These services are understood to apply across branches, though the specific clinical capacity of the Seksyen 13 location should be confirmed directly.

For families seeking a female Muslim environment, this is likely the most structured option in Shah Alam. The facility does not publish its bed count or pricing online, so call ahead to confirm availability, room configurations, and what level of nursing dependency they can accommodate at the Seksyen 13 site specifically. Ask whether a resident can receive opposite-gender medical attention in emergencies (their stated dharurah policy allows this with a mahram present), and clarify whether the branch handles bedridden or dementia cases or refers high-dependency residents to their other locations.

title: Avicenna Nursing Centre
longitude: Avicenna Nursing Centre is a nursing home located in Seksyen 13, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 1 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Avicenna Nursing Centre occupies a shophouse unit at No. 64-A, Jalan Renang 13/26 in Tadisma Business Park, Shah Alam's Seksyen 13 — a light-industrial and commercial zone about 2 km from Avisena Specialist Hospital (note the different spelling; the two are unrelated). The name references Ibn Sina, the medieval Islamic physician, which alongside the location in predominantly Malay Shah Alam may hint at a Muslim-oriented operation, though this is unconfirmed. The centre is listed simply as a nursing home with a single mobile contact number and no public website or Facebook page found.

Publicly available information about Avicenna Nursing Centre is extremely limited. No service menu, bed count, staffing details, or pricing have been published online. The shophouse unit format in a business park typically suggests a smaller facility — likely under 30 beds — operating on a practical rather than resort model. Proximity to Avisena Specialist Hospital and the broader Shah Alam Hospital catchment may be an operational advantage for residents needing frequent specialist appointments.

Families should treat this as a facility requiring a full in-person visit before any decision. Key questions include: JKM registration and licence number, total bed capacity, nurse coverage at night, whether the unit has been purpose-fitted or is a basic conversion, and what clinical capabilities (wound care, catheter management, NGT feeding) are on offer. The shophouse setting in an industrial park also warrants a look at the physical environment — ventilation, outdoor access, and amenities for residents who are ambulant.

title: Oasis Nursing Home (Setia Alam)
longitude: Oasis Nursing Home (Setia Alam) is a nursing home located in Setia Alam, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 6 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Oasis Nursing Home's Setia Alam branch is located at 66 Jalan Setia Impian U13/8B in the Setia Alam township, Shah Alam — a double-storey corner house within one of Selangor's best-planned residential developments. The facility is one of three Oasis branches operating across the greater Klang Valley, alongside locations in Shah Alam U12 and Klang, giving the group operational depth that single-location homes lack. The lead physician across the Oasis network is Dr. Wendy Oon, and the Setia Alam site shares the same medical and nursing leadership as the group's other branches. A 5-star Google Maps rating from six reviews reflects early strong feedback.

The Oasis group website lists a clear clinical remit across its branches: long-term care, short-term and post-hospitalisation care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and dementia care. Named staff include a physiotherapist (Nur Ain) and nursing leads (Shaziq as Head Nurse, Siti Ramlah as Assistant Head Nurse), indicating a structured clinical hierarchy rather than an informally run home. The 24-hour care model with physician oversight makes this positioning closer to a nursing facility than an assisted-living centre. Phone contact is via mobile (+60 12-270 3166) and email at oasisnursingcare@gmail.com.

Bed count and pricing for the Setia Alam branch specifically are not published. As a corner double-storey house, physical capacity will be modest — families should call ahead to check availability. The Setia Alam township location is an advantage for residents from Shah Alam, Klang, or Subang who want a new, well-serviced suburb setting, but the nearest major hospital (Hospital Shah Alam) is approximately 10–15 minutes by car. Confirm whether the named physician conducts regular site visits at the Setia Alam branch specifically, and ask for the JKM registration number.

title: Oasis Nursing Home (Shah Alam) 安康之家疗养院
longitude: Oasis Nursing Home (Shah Alam) 安康之家疗养院 is a nursing home located in U12, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 15 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Oasis Nursing Home — known in Chinese as 安康之家疗养院 — operates from a three-storey semi-detached house with a lift at 6, Taman Pegaga U12/1AA, 40170 Shah Alam, in the U12 section of Shah Alam's newer northwestern townships. The Chinese name and the U12 location (near the Setia Alam and Elmina growth corridors) suggest the facility primarily serves the Chinese-speaking community in that part of Shah Alam and Klang. A 5-star Google rating drawn from 15 reviews is encouraging, but a 15-review sample is small and should be weighed accordingly. The facility has a Facebook page (oasisnursingcare123) and a basic Wix website, but neither source provided detailed service or pricing information during research.

The Google Maps and Wix website describe the facility as a professional nursing care centre managed by a team of doctor, nurses, and caregivers, indicating at least some level of formal medical oversight. The semi-detached residential format with a built-in lift makes it feasible for wheelchair users and residents with limited mobility, which is an important structural consideration in converted residential properties. The three-storey configuration with a lift is a practical advantage over single-storey shophouse conversions. No detailed information on care scope — whether the facility takes dementia, tube-feeding, or palliative cases — was available from public sources.

Families should contact the facility at +60 12-270 3166 and ask about the level of care accepted, nurse-to-resident ratios across day and night shifts, whether a doctor attends regularly, and what the current vacancy and pricing situation is. The Chinese name 安康之家 (literally 'peace, health, and home') is consistent with a Chinese-community-oriented setting, so ask whether Mandarin and Cantonese are spoken by care staff, which is important for residents who are not fluent in English or Malay. Confirm JKM licence details before proceeding.

title: Mona Elder Care | Nursing Home in Pandan Perdana
longitude: Mona Elder Care | Nursing Home in Pandan Perdana is a nursing home located in Pandan Perdana, Selangor. It holds a 4.5/5 rating on Google Maps from 17 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care's Pandan Perdana branch is located at No. 32, Jalan Perdana 1/4, Pandan Perdana, in the part of the district that straddles the KL-Selangor boundary near Cheras. It is one of at least four Mona Elder Care locations in the Klang Valley — the others being in Ampang, Setapak, and Taiping — and the group's website presents a consistent clinical identity across all sites. The branch targets seniors who need nursing home-level care in a residential neighbourhood setting, with particular emphasis on stroke and coma cases, dementia, and palliative care alongside standard elderly nursing care for immobile or medically complex residents.

The services page lists a notably detailed clinical menu: bedsore dressing, nasogastric tube changes, colostomy bag management, Foley catheter care, tracheostomy care, and post-operative support alongside physiotherapy, respite care, day care, and handicapped care for adults with disabilities. This breadth suggests the facility can take on residents who require active nursing intervention, not just assisted daily living. A 24/7 care model is stated across the group's online presence, and the address is described as accessible to nearby clinics, hospitals, and public transport — useful for families attending regular follow-up appointments.

Bed count, specific staff ratios, and pricing are not disclosed publicly by Mona Elder Care; all of these should be asked directly when you call the Pandan Perdana number (+60 14-688 7358). There is no mention of JKM licence numbers, religious affiliation, or the primary languages spoken by caregivers on the website — the latter is relevant for Chinese-speaking families given the Cheras/Pandan Perdana demographic. The Facebook page for the group shows activity but does not distinguish between branches, so clarify which site photos and updates refer to.

title: Happy Family Nursing Home (Kepong)
longitude: Happy Family Nursing Home (Kepong) is a nursing home located in Taman Kepong, Kuala Lumpur. It holds a 2.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 47 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Kepong) is at 335, Jalan Kuang Gunung, Taman Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. It is one of several houses under the Happy Family brand, which also operates in Taman Kepong (Jalan 2), Taman Desa Jaya, Kajang and Cheras. The official corporate website is currently down, but third-party directory pages confirm the address and phone number 018-777 7383. Google reviews are mixed at 2.8 across 47 ratings as of mid-2026 — a notably low score relative to other Kepong homes and the largest review sample on this batch, so families should weigh recent review themes carefully before booking a visit.

The operator describes itself as a long-running affordable nursing home for the elderly, with bathing, housekeeping, grooming, basic medical support and meals, and says it equips its houses with electronic medical equipment for residents with disabilities or chronic disease. Specific capabilities published online are limited: bed count, registered nurse roster, doctor visit cadence, room types and monthly pricing are not disclosed publicly for this branch, and there is no clear separation in marketing between the Kuang Gunung, Jalan 2, and Desa Jaya houses.

Kepong residents looking for a budget-tier nursing home close to home will recognise the brand, and the multi-house network may help with capacity. Given the 2.8 rating, prospective families should read the actual Google reviews before visiting, ask specifically about staffing ratios overnight, infection-control routines, hygiene and bathing schedules, and how complaints are handled. On a tour: confirm JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number, ask to see the bedrooms in use (not show rooms), check the toilet-to-resident ratio, and ask whether the home takes NG/PEG feeds, wounds and bed-bound residents. Compare against the higher-rated Jalan 2 sister house (4.4) before deciding.

title: Mintygreen Nursing & Retirement Home (Kepong and PJ Bandar Sri Damansara)

Room options run from single rooms up to five-bedded shared rooms, which is typical for a converted-bungalow nursing home in this part of Cheras. An on-call ambulance arrangement is in place for hospital transfers. Capacity, exact staffing numbers, RN cover overnight and JKM Act 506 licence number are not published; the website is dated and parts have been suspended for renewal, so any specifics need to be confirmed by phone.

This is the kind of home that suits families who want a smaller, residential-feel placement near Cheras and the MRT, especially for relatively stable elderly relatives who need 24-hour supervision and basic nursing rather than acute medical care. Before committing, ask: how many beds in total and what is currently available; whether a registered nurse is rostered overnight or only carers; the named doctor and visit frequency; monthly fees by room type and what is excluded (diapers, milk, consumables, physio sessions); whether they take PEG/NG-fed, tracheostomy or fully bedridden residents; halal kitchen arrangements if relevant; and the JKM Act 506 registration certificate. Visit at mealtime to see staffing in practice.

title: Hemei Life Care Center Klang
longitude: Hemei Life Care Center Klang is an assisted living centre located in Kawasan 16, Selangor. It holds a 4.6/5 rating on Google Maps from 17 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Hemei Life Care Center's Klang outlet sits at 10, Jalan Batu Tiga Lama, Kawasan 16, Klang — part of a multi-branch chain that also operates in Cheras, Seri Kembangan, Selayang, Sri Damansara, and Johor Bahru. The group positions itself squarely around short-to-medium-term recovery and step-down care rather than purely long-term placement, with long stay, short stay, day care, and post-operative rehabilitation all listed as core offerings. That makes the Klang branch a candidate not just for families seeking permanent residential care but also for patients who need a supervised recovery environment after surgery or a hospital discharge before returning home.

A third-party aggregator (seniorliving.com.my) lists the Klang branch with a capacity of 200 private rooms and quotes care tiers as follows: independent or assisted living from RM 4,000 per month (standard RM 5,000), wheelchair or dementia care from RM 4,500 (standard RM 5,500), and bedridden or tube-feeding care from RM 5,000 (standard RM 6,000), with a RM 300 add-on for a medical bed. Chargeable extras listed include diapers, underpads, gloves, wet wipes, and wound dressing. The website emphasises hotel-style accommodation, CCTV surveillance, four daily meals, safety handrails, and activity programming to support mental health.

Operating hours shown on the contact page are 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM, which raises a practical question about after-hours access for family visits and whether night-shift nursing coverage is equivalent to daytime. The Klang branch was listed as 'coming soon' on some earlier versions of the Hemei website, so families should confirm it is now fully operational and ask for an updated resident count before committing. Specialist services such as physiotherapy and dementia care, which are available at other Hemei branches, should be verified as active at this specific location.

title: Anns Care Centre
longitude: Anns Care Centre is a nursing home located in Taman Ferngrove, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 3 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Ann's Care Centre is a nursing home and assisted living facility at 123, Jalan Pakis 2, Taman Ferngrove, Batu 8½, Cheras 43200, Selangor — a well-established residential area in the inner Cheras belt with reasonable access to hospital facilities serving the Cheras corridor. Reachable on 03-9076 4855, the centre has been listed in multiple healthcare directories including nursinghome.my, and maintains a Facebook presence at facebook.com/AnnsCareCentre. Its 5-star rating comes from three reviews — encouraging but a small sample.

The facility openly describes its scope as covering geriatric care, medical and surgical nursing cases, bedsore management, post-stroke rehabilitation support, and major nursing care. It accepts both short-term stays (including respite and post-hospitalisation recovery) and long-term placements. This positions Ann's as a clinically-oriented nursing home rather than a basic residential home — suited to residents who need hands-on nursing attention rather than simply a supervised living environment.

Specific details not confirmed publicly include the bed count, staffing ratios (particularly nurse coverage at night), pricing, and whether specialised dementia care or palliative support is available. When calling to enquire, ask about current bed availability, nurse qualifications, how post-stroke cases are managed day-to-day, and the monthly fee for your family member's level of dependency. Confirm JKM registration and ask whether a doctor visits the facility regularly.

title: PALS Caring Home
longitude: PALS Caring Home is a nursing home located in Kampung Cheras Baru, Selangor. It holds a 4.9/5 rating on Google Maps from 11 reviews.
editorial_summary:
PALS Caring Home sits at No. 19, Jalan 32, Kampung Cheras Baru — a residential neighbourhood in southern Cheras that straddles the Selangor-KL border. The facility markets itself as a nursing home providing high-quality assisted living, and its website lists a wide range of conditions it supports: stroke, dementia, Parkinson's, diabetes, cancer, palliative, post-hospitalization, post-surgery, and arthritis care. With a Google rating of 4.9 from 11 reviews, the limited review pool suggests a small, community-facing operation rather than a large corporate facility.

The website indicates that care plans are personalised to each resident and that stay-in caregivers provide overnight coverage when needed. Listed programmes include daily exercises, nutritious meals designed with seniors' digestive needs in mind, and various activities for physical and mental engagement. PALS also promotes home-care services alongside its residential offering, which suggests staffing capacity to support both settings. Dementia care and rehabilitation therapy are specifically cited as areas of focus, though the depth of clinical capability — specialist nurses, physiotherapy equipment, or dementia unit infrastructure — is not detailed on the site.

Bed count and pricing are not publicly disclosed. The Cheras Baru location puts residents reasonably close to Hospital Cheras and the range of specialist facilities along the MRR2 corridor, which matters for follow-up appointments. Families should ask directly about nurse-to-resident ratios on night shifts, what constitutes dementia care in practice (secure unit versus general supervision), and the precise scope of rehabilitation — whether a physiotherapist attends in person or exercises are caregiver-led.

title: PROWELL ELDERLY HOME CARE CENTRE - HQ
care_types: Nursing Home + Dementia + Palliative + Rehab + Day Care
editorial_summary:
Prowell Elderly Home Care Centre is a family-owned operator billing itself as a fifth-generation company in the senior living space. The HQ sits at 17-1, Jalan Dato Haji Harun, Taman Taynton View, Cheras, with a second branch at 2, Persiaran Titiwangsa 2, Titiwangsa, both in Kuala Lumpur. The website is pehcc.com.my.

Clinical capability is a stated strength: the centre lists feeding tube management, urinary catheter care, wound and bedsore dressing, tracheostomy management, physiotherapy and 24/7 doctor on-call with ambulance service. Care programmes cover long-term, short-term, retirement, dementia, bedridden and day care. Operating hours are daily 9am to 7pm.

Seven Google Maps reviews of the Cheras HQ average 4.1 stars. One reviewer expressed gratitude for compassionate end-of-life care provided to their brother. Pricing, bed count and JKM licence numbers are not listed on the operator website — ask for these when you call or visit the facility.

title: Elderly Gardens Care Centre KG CHERAS BARU
longitude: Elderly Gardens Care Centre KG CHERAS BARU is a nursing home located in Kampung Cheras Baru, Kuala Lumpur. It holds a 3.7/5 rating on Google Maps from 6 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Elderly Gardens Care Centre's Kampung Cheras Baru branch is at 21, Jalan 34B, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Cheras. The operator's main outlet is at Jalan Awan Cina, Taman Yarl, off Old Klang Road, with the Cheras Baru house listed as a second site. Elderly Gardens Care Centre (M) Sdn Bhd is registered as a Malaysian company (registration 202301003844 / 1497763-K) and the operator states the brand has more than twelve years of operating history in long-term and rehabilitation care.

The Taman Yarl site publishes a fairly comprehensive service list which the Cheras Baru branch shares: bathing, dressing, toileting and feeding for dependent residents, incontinence and continence care, medication reminders, escort to outpatient appointments, fall-risk supervision, physiotherapy, dementia and Alzheimer's care, bedridden and tube-feeding care, and wound care. Air-conditioned rooms are available. The operator also offers home-based packages from one hour up to 24/7, which is unusual - confirm whether the Cheras Baru house is a residential nursing home, a base for home-care staff, or both. Total beds, current fees, RN ratio overnight, JKM Act 506 licence number and halal status are not published.

This branch suits families in the Kg Cheras Baru / Taman Bukit Mewah / Taman Sri Bahtera catchment who want a Chinese-Malaysian-run nursing home with a clear scope around dementia and tube-feeding care, and is also worth a call from families who want a hybrid arrangement (home care now, residential later). Google reviews are mixed (3.7 average, small sample), which is reason to visit in person rather than rely on the rating. Specifically ask: how many beds at the Cheras Baru house, how many staff on duty overnight, whether a Registered Nurse is rostered or only carers, monthly fees by dependency level (mobile vs bedridden vs tube-fed), what is included vs charged extra, and a sight of the JKM Act 506 certificate.

title: Pusat Jagaan En Yuan PLT ( Kg Cheras Baru)
longitude: Pusat Jagaan En Yuan PLT ( Kg Cheras Baru) is a nursing home located in Kampung Cheras Baru, Kuala Lumpur. It holds a 4.2/5 rating on Google Maps from 20 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan En Yuan PLT is at 5 / 5A, Jalan 28, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, in the densely-packed Cheras Baru terrace-house belt that hosts a cluster of small Chinese-run nursing homes. The PLT suffix on the operator name indicates the business is registered as a Perkongsian Liabiliti Terhad (limited-liability partnership) under SSM. Listings classify it variously as an assisted-living facility and a nursing home, and its Mandarin name (En Yuan / 安养, broadly "peaceful care") is consistent with a Chinese-speaking operator.

Verified information is limited - there is no operator website. Independent listings give the address and a phone contact, and Google reviews show a 4.2 average across about 20 reviews, which is a more useful sample size than several of its neighbours. The home does not publish capacity, room types, fees, staffing model or JKM Act 506 licence details. Foursquare and other directories list it as assisted-living rather than skilled nursing, but local Cheras Baru operators usually mix dependency levels in practice; this needs to be checked directly.

This home is likely to suit families looking for a Chinese-speaking, neighbourhood-scale residential placement in the Cheras Baru / Taman Bukit Mewah area at mid-market price points. Before committing, call and ask: registered name and JKM Act 506 licence number; total beds and current vacancies; whether a Registered Nurse is on duty (and overnight specifically) or only PRA-trained carers; what dependency levels they accept (mobile only, bedridden, NG/PEG tube, tracheostomy, dementia with wandering); monthly fees by room type and what is excluded; and how medical issues are handled - named GP, nearest hospital used, ambulance arrangement. A walk-in visit is the only reliable way to assess this kind of small operator.

title: Happy Family Nursing Home (Cheras) (Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Taman Sri Bahtera)
longitude: Happy Family Nursing Home (Cheras) (Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Taman Sri Bahtera) is a nursing home located in Taman Sri Bahtera, Kuala Lumpur. It holds a 3.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 2 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Cheras), registered as Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Taman Sri Bahtera, sits at 49, Jalan Sekuci, Taman Sri Bahtera, 56100 Kuala Lumpur, on the eastern Cheras side near Taman Connaught and Taman Bukit Mewah. The Cheras outlet is one of several branches under the Happy Family Nursing Home operator, which also runs centres in Taman Kepong (335, Jalan Kuang Gunung), Taman Desa Jaya (Kepong) and Bandar Sungai Long (Kajang). The group operates a single contact number (019-777 7328) across branches and uses a shared corporate website at happyfamilynursinghome.com.my.

As a multi-branch operator, Happy Family is structured as a chain of converted-house nursing homes rather than a single purpose-built facility - typical for the Klang Valley mid-market. Public information about the Cheras outlet specifically is sparse: capacity, room mix, RN cover, dementia and tube-feeding scope, halal status, and JKM Act 506 licence number are not on the website. The Google profile for the Sri Bahtera branch shows only two reviews with a 3.0 average, which is too small to draw conclusions from. The chain pattern itself is a useful signal - operators running four or more outlets tend to have a more standardised admission process and clearer fee schedule than one-off houses.

This home is likely to suit families in Taman Sri Bahtera, Cheras Baru and Taman Connaught who want a Chinese-Malaysian-run residential placement at mid-market pricing, with the option to move a relative between the operator's branches if catchment changes. Before committing, ask the operator directly: which branch holds the JKM Act 506 licence and the licence number; bed capacity and current vacancy at the Sri Bahtera house specifically; whether a Registered Nurse covers nights or only carers; dependency levels accepted (bedridden, NG/PEG, dementia with wandering); monthly fees with itemised inclusions; and arrangements for medical review and hospital transfer. Visit the actual Cheras branch rather than the marketing photos on the website, which are likely a composite of all outlets.

title: Mintygreen Care Suites - Home Care Nursing Cheras KL
editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Suites Cheras is at 100, Jalan Cheras, Phoenix Business Park, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, a few minutes' walk from Taman Connaught MRT and on the Cheras-Kajang Expressway corridor. It is one of four outlets under the Mintygreen brand (PJ, Kepong, Cheras and Sungai Long Kajang HQ), and the operator markets the Cheras outlet as a hotel-based nursing and rehabilitation centre - a converted commercial-suite block running boutique-hotel style rooms rather than the converted-bungalow format that dominates the rest of Cheras. The corporate entity is Mintygreen Care Suites Cheras Sdn Bhd. Person in charge for enquiries is Mr Jack on 012-302 6583, with a 24/7 line on 011-5587 9788.

Published services include 24-hour nursing care, day care and overnight respite, hospice and palliative care, NG-tube feeding, urinary catheter care, bedsore management, post-operative recovery, medication administration, fortnightly doctor visits and physiotherapy. The brand also markets Traditional Chinese Medicine elements (acupuncture, Tui Na) and structured daily activities across its outlets, although it is not stated which of these are delivered specifically at the Cheras suite. Nearest acute hospitals are Pantai Hospital Cheras (3.3 km), Sunway Velocity Medical Centre (6.6 km) and HCTM/HUKM (around 5 km). What is not published is bed count, exact room rates, RN ratio overnight, halal status, the founder/year founded and the JKM Act 506 licence number.

This is the option to look at if the family wants a more modern, hotel-style room (private en-suite, lift access, commercial-grade fire safety) rather than a converted-house room, and is willing to pay a premium for it - the 4.8 average across 21 Google reviews is the strongest score in this batch, although still a modest sample. Suits post-hospital recovery, short-term respite, and longer-term placement for residents who want privacy. Ask directly about: monthly room rates by type, what is included (diapers, milk feeds, physio sessions, doctor visits, oxygen if needed); RN-on-duty pattern overnight; how palliative pain control is prescribed and which doctor signs the schedule; whether the kitchen is halal or non-halal; and the JKM Act 506 licence number for the Cheras outlet specifically (chain operators sometimes hold separate licences per branch).

title: Oasis Nursing Home (Klang) - 安康之家(巴生)疗养院
longitude: Oasis Nursing Home (Klang) - 安康之家(巴生)疗养院 is a nursing home located in Taman Sungai Kapar Indah, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 8 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Oasis Nursing Home (安康之家) is located at No. 50, Jalan Sungai Kapar Indah 5E, Taman Sungai Kapar Indah, in the Kapar district of Klang — a suburban residential area north of Klang town proper. The facility is distinctive in that its founder and doctor-in-charge is Dr. Wendy Oon, a named physician whose involvement is documented on the facility's own website. The care team listed includes a head of nursing (Shaziq), an assistant head nurse (Siti Ramlah), and an in-house physiotherapist (Nur Ain) — a staff configuration that goes beyond what most small nursing homes publish, and indicates genuine clinical structure rather than caregiver-only operations. The facility has a Wix-hosted website and a Facebook page, and carries a perfect 5.0 Google rating from 8 reviews.

Oasis lists its care scope as: long-term care, short-term stays, post-hospitalization, stroke rehabilitation, post-operation, palliative care, and dementia care. A testimonial cited on the website describes improvement in a resident's bedsore from grade 4 to grade 1 — a clinically meaningful outcome that, if verified, suggests nursing wound care competency. The Chinese-language name (安康之家, literally 'home of health and tranquility') suggests the facility is oriented toward the Chinese-speaking community, consistent with the Kapar area's demographics, though the team includes Malay staff names indicating a mixed-language environment. Operating hours listed are Sunday through Friday, 8am–6pm, which refers to visiting or administrative hours; residential care is 24-hour.

Bed count is not disclosed. Kapar is roughly 15–20 minutes from Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah in Klang town, the main government hospital for the area, and 10 minutes from the Kapar town centre. With pricing not published, families should contact via +60 12-270 3166 or oasisnursingcare@gmail.com. Ask about the frequency of Dr. Oon's on-site presence versus on-call arrangement, what physiotherapy sessions look like in practice, and whether palliative care extends to end-of-life support or is primarily post-acute.

title: Acacia by Pacific Senior Living
longitude: Acacia by Pacific Senior Living is an assisted living centre located in Kawasan 17, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 91 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Acacia by Pacific Senior Living is a purpose-built senior living residence in Klang, Selangor, operated by Pacific Senior Living Sdn Bhd, which is part of the Seattle-headquartered Columbia Pacific Management Group. The property was converted from the former Merrida Hotel and officially launched in November 2023. It occupies a resort-like setting in Kawasan 17, Klang, with reasonably convenient access to Kuala Lumpur. Acacia positions itself firmly at the premium end of the market, targeting independent and assisted-living residents aged 60 and above — it is not a conventional nursing home and does not take on acute medical cases.

The residence has 136 private rooms across three tiers — Superior (312 sq ft), Executive, and Premier (up to 592 sq ft) — each with an ensuite bathroom, smart TV, writing desk, and mini fridge. Assisted living units start from RM 9,200 per person per month; independent living from RM 8,500. Wellness Associates are on-site 24/7 for non-clinical support, and healthcare professional visits are scheduled rather than resident. Columbia Asia serves as the strategic healthcare partner under a memorandum of understanding signed in August 2023. On-site amenities include a senior-friendly swimming pool with jacuzzi, gymnasium, mini theatre, karaoke room, games area, F&B restaurant, and hobby workshop. The Silver Club day programme (7 am–7 pm daily) is open to non-residents.

Acacia is the right fit for relatively independent seniors or early assisted-living needs; families expecting full nursing-home-level clinical care should look elsewhere. The pricing is among the highest in Selangor and does not appear to include JKM subsidy eligibility given its private premium model. Confirm precisely what triggers an escalation from Wellness Associate support to nursing-level care, what Columbia Asia's role actually covers day-to-day (scheduled visits, not full-time on-site), and what additional fees apply for medication management, physiotherapy sessions, or transport to hospital.

title: Athena Care Centre (Klang)
longitude: Athena Care Centre (Klang) is a nursing home located in Taman Sri Andalas, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 2 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Athena Care Centre is pinned to Taman Sri Andalas, a township in the southern Klang area with reasonable proximity to Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah — the main public hospital for Klang — roughly 8–10 km away. The home is contactable via the Google Maps listing and carries 5-star reviews from a small reviewer base. The home does not maintain a published website or Facebook page, so most service detail is best gathered on a call or visit.

Care types offered, JKM licence number, capacity, and on-duty nursing capability are best confirmed directly with the home. Taman Sri Andalas is a mixed residential-commercial area that supports a number of small community nursing homes, often operating from converted terrace or shop premises. The proximity to HTAR is a practical asset for families anticipating hospital escalations.

Families should call the listed number to confirm the home is currently accepting residents, establish care type and licence status, and arrange a site visit before considering placement. Useful questions on the call: the JKM licence number, the registered-nurse cover overnight, the visiting-doctor frequency, the monthly fee, and what the protocol is for medical emergencies.

title: RS Global Home Care ( Only For Muslim )
editorial_summary:
RS Global Home Care in Kampung Cheras Baru, Kuala Lumpur, is a residential nursing home serving Muslim residents exclusively, at 1C, Jalan 2, 56100 KL. The home emphasises Islamic programming alongside standard residential care. Phone: +60 11-3586 0025.

Google Maps reviewers consistently highlight Islamic care practices as a key differentiator: residents receive assistance with daily prayers, participate in Quran recitation sessions, and are served halal meals. The facility has over 200 photos documented on Google Maps, reflecting active engagement by staff and families. Specific capacity and pricing details are not published online.

Eighteen reviews average 4.7 stars. Malay-language reviewers describe clean rooms, well-prepared halal meals and staff who are attentive to religious obligations. For Muslim families seeking a values-aligned care environment with structured religious programming, this is one of the few facilities in the Cheras area explicitly committed to this. Confirm bed availability, fees and JKM licence directly by phone.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Penyayang
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Penyayang is a care home located in Taman Serdang Raya, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 1 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Penyayang is listed in Taman Serdang Raya, Seri Kembangan — a well-connected township southeast of Kuala Lumpur with reasonable access to Columbia Asia Hospital Cheras and several private medical clinics. The Google listing categorises it under aged care. The home is contactable on +60 12-203 9932.

The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered directly. Bed count, staffing, the scope of medical care, and JKM registration are best confirmed on a call. Taman Serdang Raya is a mature residential township with multiple smaller community-scale care homes catering to local families.

Families considering this centre should call ahead to establish current admission availability and the level of care offered. Useful questions: bed count, registered-nurse cover, doctor-visit frequency, dependency levels accepted (ambulant, partial-care, bedridden), monthly fees, and the JKM licence number. The Serdang location is a practical asset for families anticipating hospital escalations to UPM Teaching Hospital or Columbia Asia.

title: GoldLeaf Villa Care Centre
longitude: GoldLeaf Villa Care Centre is a nursing home located in Taman Orkid Desa, Kuala Lumpur.
editorial_summary:
GoldLeaf Villa Care Centre is listed at Taman Orkid Desa, Cheras, with phone enquiries on 010-238 3848. The home does not appear on any operator website, sector directory (AGECOPE, hati.my, ielder.asia, Care Concierge top-10 lists), or significant news coverage that we could verify, and the Google profile carries no public reviews or rating at the time of writing. It is registered as a nursing home ("Care Centre" in the name suggests a residential elder-care setup rather than a daycare), and Taman Orkid Desa is a residential terrace-house neighbourhood off Jalan Cheras, consistent with a converted-house operator.

Until more information is published or verified by visit, families should treat this as a home that has to be assessed entirely by phone call and site visit. Ask: registered operator name and JKM (Act 506) licence number; total beds and current vacancy; staff numbers on duty day and night, and whether a Registered Nurse is rostered overnight or only carers; what dependency levels are accepted (mobile, bedridden, NG/PEG tube, dementia with wandering, palliative); monthly fees and what is included vs charged extra; languages spoken; halal or non-halal kitchen; named doctor and hospital used for transfers; and visiting hours.

title: LN HOME CARE CENTER
editorial_summary:
LN Home Care Center is a small nursing home at Bunga Ros, Kawasan 6, Taman Teluk Pulai, 41100 Klang, Selangor — a residential pocket within the Teluk Pulai township, a predominantly Chinese-majority neighbourhood in western Klang. Phone: +60 19-888 8690.

Two Google Maps photos show the facility. Care details, capacity and pricing are not published online.

Three reviews average 5.0 stars. No detailed review text was available, so specific care observations cannot be confirmed at this time. When calling, ask about the JKM licence number, care types accepted, current bed availability and monthly fees.

title: Suria Care Home/Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas/舒利亚乐龄休养中心
longitude: Suria Care Home/Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas/舒利亚乐龄休养中心 is a care home located in Taman City, Kuala Lumpur.
editorial_summary:
Suria Care Home / Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas (Mandarin: 舒利亚乐龄休养中心) is listed in Taman City, on the Jinjang / Kepong side of Kuala Lumpur (not Cheras, despite being in the KL batch), with a landline contact on 03-6241 9132. The trilingual name - Bahasa Malaysia ("Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas"), English and Mandarin - is unusual and suggests an operator catering to mixed Malaysian families rather than a single language community. Listings classify it as aged care. Note that there are at least two unrelated "Suria" elder-care brands in the Klang Valley (one in PJ, one in Titiwangsa), so families should be careful to confirm they are dialling the Taman City address specifically.

No operator website, social-media page, sector-directory listing or Google reviews could be verified for this address, and capacity, fees, services, JKM Act 506 licence, staffing and religion are all unpublished. Treat this as a home to assess entirely by phone and site visit. Ask: registered operator name and JKM Act 506 licence number; total beds and current vacancy; RN cover overnight vs carer-only; what dependency levels are accepted (mobile, bedridden, NG/PEG, dementia, palliative); monthly fees by room type and inclusions; halal or non-halal kitchen; languages spoken by carers; and how medical issues and hospital transfers are handled.

title: Pusat Jagaan Persatuan Kebajikan Warga Emas Chan Kl
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Persatuan Kebajikan Warga Emas Chan Kl is a care home located in Kampung Tasek Permai, Selangor. It holds a 3.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 1 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Persatuan Kebajikan Warga Emas Chan KL — also known informally as 安安老人院 (An An Old Folks Home) — is a welfare-oriented elderly care centre in the Pandan Indah / Ampang Jaya area of Selangor, operated by a Chinese community welfare association bearing the Chan family name. The Malay listing includes 'KL' in its title despite being situated in Selangor, reflecting its proximity to the Ampang border with Kuala Lumpur. It maintains a Facebook presence (pusatjagaanwargaemaschan) and is navigable via Waze. The Google listing categorises it as a 'social services organisation' rather than a formal nursing home — a meaningful distinction for families looking for nursing-grade care.

As a welfare association home, An An is likely structured around community charity rather than commercial care delivery — meaning it may accept subsidised or low-income referrals alongside self-paying residents. The 'languages: 30' data point in the source file appears to be a data entry error rather than a languages field. Bed count, the care dependency levels managed, and JKM registration status are best confirmed directly with the home.

Families should clarify upfront whether the home is JKM-registered as a residential care facility, what nursing qualifications staff hold, and what happens when a resident's medical needs escalate beyond what the home can manage. If it is primarily a social-welfare placement rather than a nursing home, it may not be suited to residents who need wound care, PEG feeding, or dementia-specific programmes. A short visit in person is the most reliable way to assess the physical environment and staffing model.

title: Pusat jagaan orang tua cahaya maju
longitude: Pusat jagaan orang tua cahaya maju is a residential shelter located in Taman Seri Melati, Selangor. It holds a 4.6/5 rating on Google Maps from 8 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Cahaya Maju is a welfare-based elderly care home operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Cahaya Maju (Cahaya Maju Welfare Association) at 22 Jalan Melati 1, Taman Seri Melati, 68100 Batu Caves, Selangor. Incorporated in January 2019, the centre is classified on its registration as providing residential care and is co-listed as a shelter catering to elderly individuals aged 40 and above, including those from low-income or vulnerable backgrounds. It operates daily from approximately 08:45 to 19:00 and accepts both monetary and in-kind donations, reflecting its non-profit community welfare model.

The organisation's charitable mandate centres on providing shelter, food, basic medical access, and a degree of social support to seniors who might otherwise lack these necessities. Google Maps classifies the listing as a homeless shelter alongside an elderly care function, indicating the home serves a mixed population of older adults in need rather than functioning as a clinical nursing home. The facility's capacity and staffing levels are not published, and no nursing or medical programming details are available through public sources.

Families seeking placement for a dependent elderly relative with significant clinical needs should approach this centre with an understanding that it operates as a welfare home rather than a licensed nursing facility. Ask directly about what level of personal and nursing care is provided, whether any medically trained staff are on site, and how medical emergencies are handled. The Batu Caves location provides reasonable access to Selayang Hospital (approximately 8 kilometres). Confirm current JKM registration status before placing a resident here.

title: Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Damai
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Damai is a care home located in Taman Taynton View, Kuala Lumpur. It holds a 2.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 1 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Damai is a small private elderly care home at No. 56, Jalan Arasakesari 2, Taman Taynton, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, in the Taman Taynton View neighbourhood of Cheras. The phone number on file is +60 10-773 1193, with a secondary contact-page listing of 016-256 8XXX. There is no website and no public Facebook page; nearest public transport is Taman Mutiara MRT/LRT, about a 14-minute walk. Online reviews are minimal (a single Google review with a low rating).

Beyond confirming the address and that it operates as an elderly care facility in a residential terrace house, almost no operational detail is published online. Care types, bed count, fee structure, dementia or palliative capability, halal/non-halal status, and JKM registration status all need to be confirmed directly with the operator.

If you are considering this home, call ahead and ask: how many residents and how many staff on each shift; is there a registered nurse on duty day and night, or only caregivers; is the centre registered with JKM under Act 506; what is the monthly fee for shared vs single rooms and what does it include (diapers, milk feeds, laundry, GP visits); can they manage tube feeds, catheters, wound care, oxygen, and dementia behaviours; what is the visiting policy; and can the family inspect the rooms, kitchen, and bathrooms before committing.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Merry
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Merry is a care home located in Kepong Baru, Kuala Lumpur. It holds a 4.7/5 rating on Google Maps from 3 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Merry is a small private elderly care home at 53, Jalan Belinggai, Kepong Baru, 52100 Kuala Lumpur, on the residential side of Kepong Baru. Telephone is +60 3-6277 3595. The home has no website and no public Facebook page, and appears in Malaysian directory listings (worldorgs.com) but not on the larger curated nursing home directories such as hati.my or ielder.asia. Google reviews are positive but very limited in number (rating around 4.7 from only 3 reviewers), so the sample is too small to draw firm conclusions.

No operational detail is published online: care intensity (whether the home accepts bedbound residents, tube feeds, wound care, dementia), staffing model (RN vs caregiver ratio), bed count, fee tiers, halal status, and JKM/Act 506 registration are all unconfirmed. The Kepong Baru area has multiple small homes operating from converted terrace houses, and based on the address format this is most likely the same model.

For families shortlisting Merry, the practical step is a site visit and a structured phone call. Ask for the JKM registration number and date; total beds and current occupancy; whether a registered nurse is on duty overnight or only caregivers; monthly fee and what it includes (diapers, milk feeds, GP, transport to clinic); ability to manage NG/PEG feeds, urinary catheters, oxygen, and dementia wandering; menu and halal certification if relevant; and visiting hours and any restrictions.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Kangiten
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Kangiten is a nursing home located in Taman Selayang Jaya, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 1 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Kangiten (PJWEK) operates from 29, Jalan SP1 in Taman Selayang Jaya, Batu Caves — a residential estate north of Kuala Lumpur that sits within easy reach of Hospital Selayang, one of the country's larger public tertiary centres. The facility markets itself explicitly as both a nursing home and a physiotherapy centre, which is a meaningful distinction: rather than treating physio as a bolt-on, it appears to be a core service pillar. The team description references doctors, nurses, and physiotherapists on staff, though headcounts and shift arrangements are not publicly stated.

Kangiten's clinical capability list is more detailed than most facilities at this level. Documented nursing procedures include wound dressing, bed-sore management, catheter and tracheostomy care, NGT insertion, insulin administration, and medication monitoring — a range that suggests the centre is equipped to accept residents with moderate-to-high dependency. On the rehabilitation side, the facility's physiotherapists reportedly cover home visits across 14 service zones, and treat conditions including stroke, Parkinson's, arthritis, cerebral palsy, joint replacements, and spinal cord injuries. Day care and short-term stay options appear to exist alongside long-term residential places.

Pricing and total bed count are not published, so families should request these directly. The home-visit physiotherapy arm may operate somewhat independently of the residential unit — worth clarifying whether residents automatically access in-house physio or whether this is separately charged. Confirming the JKM licence number and the on-site medical cover arrangement (particularly overnight) are standard due-diligence steps before admission. The Batu Caves location has good road access but limited public transport, which matters for families visiting by bus or LRT.

title: Pusat Jagaan Nurul Jannah
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Nurul Jannah is a home care service located in Ampang Jaya, Selangor. It holds a 4.7/5 rating on Google Maps from 7 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Nurul Jannah is a private elderly care centre established in 2018 and based at Jalan AU 5C/12 in Ampang Jaya, Selangor, on the KL border near Keramat and Wangsa Maju. It was founded with an explicitly Muslim-women-centred mission — the centre's stated tagline translates as "to care for, treat, educate, and show love to Muslim elderly women." This makes it a specialist placement for Malay-Muslim families seeking a faith-aligned residential setting for female relatives. The centre operates 24 hours, seven days a week, and is classified in directories as a home health care service.

The facility offers both monthly and daily placement, giving families flexibility for respite or long-term arrangements. Its website and Google Sites presence indicate that services include light medical treatment for unwell residents, religious classes for interested residents, and comprehensive emotional support. The dual contact model — separate numbers for male (En. Iqbal) and female (Pn. Yus) staff liaisons — reflects cultural sensitivity in communications. The Google Maps rating is 4.7 from seven reviews, suggesting consistent positive feedback from families who have used the service. Specific bed count, room types, and pricing are not publicly stated.

Key questions to raise on enquiry include current capacity and whether there is a waitlist, the level of nursing care available for residents with mobility limitations or medical devices, and how acute medical events are handled overnight. Given the female-only and Muslim-focused character, families should also confirm whether male caregivers are present and under what circumstances. The Jalan AU 5C/12 address in Ampang Jaya places it reasonably close to Hospital Ampang for emergencies.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua SRA
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua SRA is a nursing home located in Taman Malawati, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 5 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua SRA was founded on 1 January 2020 by Sharil Hussein, a former Ministry of Health civil servant, as a protective shelter for elderly people discharged or abandoned from hospitals whose families cannot provide care. The home is located in Taman Malawati, Kuala Selangor — a suburban setting roughly an hour from central Kuala Lumpur. Residents are drawn predominantly from referrals through the hospital system rather than private placements, reflecting the founder's healthcare background and the NGO's welfare-first mandate. The facility is operated under the Selangor Relief Agency (SRA) umbrella.

As of early 2025, the home was caring for 34 elderly and frail residents. Funding has historically come from Selangor Zakat Board support, and the home has periodically run public appeals for donations to maintain its rented bungalow. Local welfare partners (including Mayda) have stepped in at various points to support premises and operating costs. The resident profile appears to include bedridden and uzur (severely debilitated) individuals, suggesting the home handles higher-dependency cases beyond basic assisted living.

The home runs a welfare-first model, so families considering a placement here should call ahead on +60 19-879 1926 to confirm current admission criteria, the latest fee structure for non-asnaf residents, and the current operating premises. Useful questions: the care team composition, whether nursing staff are available overnight, and how the home manages medical escalations given the Kuala Selangor location. SRA also welcomes volunteer and donor enquiries through the same number.

title: Pusat Jagaan Harian Warga Tua Cheras Baru
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Harian Warga Tua Cheras Baru is a care home located in Kampung Cheras Baru, Kuala Lumpur. It holds a 4.6/5 rating on Google Maps from 9 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Harian Warga Tua Cheras Baru is a community day care centre for older adults located at Lot 3716, Jalan 17, Off Jalan Kuari, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur. Unlike a residential nursing home, this is a daytime-only service: opening hours are Monday to Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, closed on weekends. The contact line is +60 3-4297 0316 (with a published mobile 012-643 7XXX). It is registered as a non-profit (Persatuan/association) rather than a commercial operator, and has been running long enough to host structured community programmes with university and teacher-training groups.

Operationally, the centre runs daytime activities for ambulant seniors rather than skilled nursing. Verified programmes documented in community-service reports include light aerobics, sports and recreation, and intergenerational activities run with visiting trainee teachers (one published report involved 63 student volunteers). The model fits seniors who live with family but need structured daytime supervision, social interaction, and basic supervised activity, rather than residents needing 24-hour nursing or post-stroke rehab.

This centre is most useful for KL families in the Cheras / Setapak / Wangsa Maju catchment whose elderly relative is mobile and continent but at risk of isolation, falls, or loneliness when the family is at work.
