A significant limitation in researching this facility is that its official website is hosted on an Odoo platform and returned a 404 error during research, which makes it difficult to verify details such as bed count, room configurations, pricing, and staffing qualifications. Families should request a full written service schedule, pricing breakdown, and JKM registration details when making an enquiry. The Bandar Sunway location is commercially well-connected and near Sunway Medical Centre, which is a practical advantage if hospitalisation becomes necessary — ask whether the home has an existing referral relationship with nearby hospitals.

title: Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21, Subang Jaya
longitude: Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21, Subang Jaya is a nursing home located in UEP Subang Jaya, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 1 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21 is one branch in Mintygreen's growing Klang Valley network, operating from 1, Jalan USJ 21/10 in UEP Subang Jaya — a mature township with excellent road access and proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre (SJMC) and several public clinics. Mintygreen Healthcare Group, established in 2017, is known as an early mover in integrating Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) into its nursing home model, and this positioning runs across all branches. The USJ21 unit is a smaller, neighbourhood-scale branch rather than a flagship; the main Kajang–Balakong site serves as the group's headquarters.

Across the Mintygreen network, the group's documented capabilities include 24-hour nursing care, post-surgery recovery, regular doctor visits, physiotherapy, rehabilitation, psychological counselling, TCM (acupuncture and Tui Na), dietetic services, day care, and home care through its '24 Angels' arm. Whether all of these services are available at the USJ21 branch specifically — or concentrated at larger sites — is not publicly stated and should be confirmed directly. The contact person listed for USJ21 is Ms. Xiao Tong at +60 11-5587 9788, distinct from the group's main line, which suggests branch-level management.

For families in Subang Jaya or USJ who want a local option without travelling to Kajang, this branch is a reasonable first call — but it warrants a site visit to assess the physical space, bed count, and what share of Mintygreen's full capability is actually on-site. Pricing is not published for this branch; the group's rates at other locations tend toward the mid-market range for Klang Valley. Ask specifically about doctor visit frequency, physio session costs, and whether TCM is integrated or a pay-per-session extra.

title: Synergy Care Centre
longitude: Synergy Care Centre is a nursing home located in USJ Sentral, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 127 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Synergy Care Centre is a 24-hour nursing home at 15, Jalan USJ Sentral 3, in the USJ Sentral commercial precinct of Subang Jaya, Selangor. The facility is operated under Nursing Synergy Sdn Bhd, which also maintains a separate contact line at a second location. The centre is led by Dr. Pathma, who is referenced in multiple reviews as being personally involved in resident care and responsive to family enquiries.

The centre provides trained registered nurse coverage around the clock, with a senior physiotherapist on-site delivering daily physiotherapy seven days a week. Clinical capabilities include feeding tube management, tracheostomy care and suctioning, oxygen therapy, wound care, and medication administration. Room types include single rooms, twin rooms, and dormitory beds, with third-party pricing sources listing rates in the RM 2,001–RM 3,000 per month range. Day care for seniors who return home each evening is also offered. Social activities — described as yoga, gardening, drawing, and board games — are organised regularly.

This is a competent option for medically complex residents within the USJ area, particularly those requiring tube care or respiratory support who cannot afford acute hospital costs long-term. The seven-days-a-week physiotherapy is a meaningful differentiator at this price band. Confirm on-site nurse staffing ratios, whether the doctor visit is a dedicated medical director or an on-call arrangement, and current JKM registration status before committing.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Impresif
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Impresif is a care home located in Pjs 7, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 4 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Impresif sits in a residential pocket of PJS 7, Petaling Jaya, at Jalan Bukit Menteri Selatan 7/2. It is classified as a non-profit organisation on Google, which suggests it may operate on a welfare or charitable footing rather than as a fully commercial nursing home — though this has not been independently confirmed. The location gives reasonable access to the Asia Jaya area and several bus routes serving the PJ Selatan corridor.

The home keeps a low online profile — no website or Facebook page — and is best contacted directly via the listed phone number (03-7954 9008). Bed count, staffing, the scope of medical care provided, and JKM registration status are best confirmed on a call. The non-profit classification could mean it operates as a charity home with subsidised placements, or it could reflect an outdated business-type tag on the listing.

Families considering this centre should call first to establish whether it is taking new residents, what level of care is provided, and whether there is a waiting list as is common with welfare-based homes. Useful questions when calling: the JKM licensing status, whether a registered nurse is on staff, and whether the home accepts bedridden residents. A short visit will give a much clearer picture than the listing alone.

title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI)
longitude: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI) is a nursing home located in Taman Tasik Semenyih, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 75 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI) is located at Jalan TTS 5/2, Taman Tasik Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih, in the Semenyih sub-district of Selangor. The facility carries a Google rating of 4.8 from 75 reviews, which is notably high and suggests strong satisfaction among residents and families, though the volume of publicly available operational information is limited. From what is available, PJMI appears to operate as an Islamic-oriented elderly care home serving warga emas — senior citizens — with a stated mission of supporting those who lack family members to care for them.

Registered on the charitable platform Sumbangan.com, PJMI is positioned as a community welfare centre that relies partly on donations to sustain its operations and to assist elderly residents without heirs. This model — part residential home, part charitable welfare centre — is common among community-based care homes in semi-rural Selangor townships. The name and mission suggest a Muslim-managed environment, likely incorporating religious observance such as daily prayers into the daily routine. Specific clinical services, bed capacity, staffing ratios, and pricing have not been publicly disclosed by the facility.

Semenyih is approximately 35–40 km southeast of Kuala Lumpur, making it relatively accessible from Kajang and Beranang but distant from major tertiary hospitals — Hospital Kajang is the nearest government facility. Families considering PJMI should visit in person to assess the physical condition of the premises, staffing levels, clinical capabilities, and nursing care arrangements. Given the charitable funding model, confirm whether the facility holds a valid JKM licence, what medical oversight is in place, and whether the home can manage residents with complex nursing needs or is better suited to ambulatory, lower-dependency elderly individuals.

title: Noble Care Malaysia Sdn Bhd
longitude: Noble Care Malaysia Sdn Bhd is a nursing home located in Usj 9, Selangor. It holds a 3.9/5 rating on Google Maps from 7 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Noble Care (Noble Care (M) Sdn Bhd, reg. 903876-P) is a Malaysian nursing home chain with over 15 residential branches across the country, including Cheras, Rawang, Klang, Petaling Jaya, USJ-9 Subang Jaya, USJ-18, Jalan IPOH, Ampang, Semenyih, Kajang, Mid Valley, Seremban, and Serendah in the central region, plus branches in Johor Bahru, Ipoh, and Penang. The head office is at USJ 9 Subang Business Centre, Subang Jaya. Noble Care has been operating for 16+ years and positions itself as a full-service nursing home group.

Care offerings at residential branches include nursing care, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative care, stroke and cancer recovery, respite admissions, handicapped adult and children care, and physiotherapy. The group also offers home nursing as a secondary service. Each residential branch maintains doctors and nurses available 24/7. The chain provides private and shared living spaces, social and recreational activities, nutritious meals, and outdoor areas across its branches.

This entry covers the Noble Care brand and head office. Individual branch profiles are listed separately in this directory for Rawang, Klang, Subang Jaya (USJ-9 and USJ-18), Jalan IPOH, and other locations. For specific pricing and availability, contact the relevant branch directly. Head office: +60-167786795 or +60-162786993.

title: My Peaceful Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
My Peaceful Home Care Centre occupies a 670-square-metre property at 14, Jalan SS 19/3 in the SS 19 neighbourhood of Subang Jaya, Selangor. The operator expanded with a second centre in 2022 and serves residents from the Subang Jaya, Petaling Jaya and Damansara corridor. The home offers residential elderly care, assisted living and dedicated dementia nursing care.

Two in-house physicians conduct weekly medical examinations; 24/7 trained caretakers are on duty and all rooms are fitted with call buttons. Room configurations include multi-sharing, twin-sharing and single private options, all with outdoor garden access. Operating enquiry hours are 9am to 10pm; visiting hours run 9am to 6pm.

Six Google Maps reviews average 4.8 stars, with reviewers noting well-maintained facilities and experienced, approachable staff. Pricing and bed count are not posted on the website at mypeacefulhome.com.my — request a written quote during your visit and confirm the JKM licence number at the same time.

title: My Joyful Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
My Joyful Home Care Center operates from 12, Jalan SS 19/3 in the SS 19 neighbourhood of Subang Jaya, Selangor — immediately adjacent to a similarly-named home at number 14. The centre launched a professional website in early 2025 at myjoyfulhome.com.my and lists 24/7 nursing staff as a core feature.

Services span nursing home care, assisted living, dementia care, palliative care, diabetes management, respite stays and general senior residential care. Reviewers specifically mention daily doctor visits and describe a clean, homelike environment. Two contact numbers are listed: 019-329 3855 and 011-1281 2292.

Eight Google Maps reviews average 5.0 stars. Reviewers describe professional caregivers, a comfortable atmosphere and attentive day-to-day support. Pricing and bed count are not posted on the operator website — contact the centre directly for a written fee breakdown and ask to confirm the JKM licence number during your site visit.

title: Noble Care Nursing Home | Nursing Home Subang Jaya Selangor
longitude: Noble Care Nursing Home | Nursing Home Subang Jaya Selangor is a care home located in Usj 18, Selangor. It holds a 3.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 21 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Noble Care at USJ 18 is one branch within Noble Care (M) Sdn. Bhd., a national operator running 18 locations across Malaysia. The USJ 18 facility is at 1, Jalan USJ 18/3, 47630 Subang Jaya — a mature residential area with good road access off the Federal Highway and LDP. As part of an established network, Noble Care operates under a standardised care model with corporate oversight, distinguishing it from standalone family-run homes in the same price bracket.

The website indicates the USJ 18 branch provides a broad scope of care including general senior residential care, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and cancer care, stroke care, respite care, and post-operative recovery. Clinical capabilities listed include catheter management, nasogastric tube changes, colostomy care, tracheostomy care, and wound dressing — a meaningful range suggesting the branch can handle medically complex residents who might otherwise require a hospital ward. Physiotherapy and cerebral palsy treatment are also listed. The facility emphasises 24/7 medical supervision and a home-like environment.

The Google rating of 3.8 across 21 reviews is the lowest among Noble Care's Klang Valley branches and is worth noting — families should read recent reviews carefully before visiting. Bed capacity is not disclosed on the website, and pricing is not published, so a consultation call (+60 16-278 6993) is needed to assess costs and availability. Because Noble Care runs multiple sites, clarify during the visit whether the USJ 18 branch retains its own permanent clinical team or whether staff rotate across branches.

title: Attia Global Care Centre ( Subang Jaya)
longitude: Attia Global Care Centre ( Subang Jaya) is a nursing home located in Ss 19, Selangor. It holds a 4.5/5 rating on Google Maps from 24 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Attia Global Care Centre Subang Jaya is part of the Attia Nursing Care network, which has grown since 2011 from a single nursing home into six branches across the Klang Valley and Ipoh. The Subang Jaya branch sits at No. 7, Jalan SS 19/2 in the established SS19 neighbourhood, providing long-term assisted living care in a facility described as homely rather than clinical in character — air-conditioned rooms with attached bathrooms, a large living area, elevators for accessibility, and nurse call systems. Weekly doctor visits, hospital transfer support, and daily monitoring of blood pressure, glucose, and diabetes parameters are part of the standard offering.

The medical services at this branch are notably broad for a community nursing home: medication management, speech therapy, physiotherapy, acupuncture, wound dressing, tracheostomy management, and two-hourly repositioning to prevent pressure sores. Day care is available alongside full residential care. Recreational outings and an in-house activity programme are listed as resident engagement features. The network's longevity and multi-branch presence suggest stable operational management and the economies of scale needed to maintain consistent staffing. The SS19 location is contactable at 03-5611 0548 or WhatsApp 016-647 1674.

Visits to the centre are by appointment only — call ahead to arrange. Families should ask specifically about current capacity at this branch (not network-wide), the on-site nurse staffing ratio, whether the weekly doctor is a GP or specialist, and how complex dementia or palliative cases are managed. Attia's multi-site model means quality can vary between branches, so an in-person assessment of the SS19 premises is worthwhile before commitment. Pricing is not published; request a personalised quote based on dependency level.

title: My Precious Home Care Elder Care
editorial_summary:
My Precious Home Care Elder Care is a residential nursing home at 27, Jalan USJ 1/3B, Grandville, Subang Jaya, founded by a doctor who continues to conduct regular facility visits and health monitoring. The operator runs at least two branches — this USJ location and a second home in Kampung Tunku, Petaling Jaya. The website is myprecioushomecare.com.

Care covers long-term and short-term stays, 24-hour personal care, dementia care, palliative care, an in-house physiotherapist for rehabilitation, and monitoring visits by the founding physician. Rooms have attached bathrooms and wheelchair accessibility; four home-cooked meals are served daily. Pricing is not published on the operator website.

With 122 Google Maps reviews averaging 4.7 stars, this is among the most-reviewed elder care homes in the Subang Jaya area. Reviewers consistently describe kind, attentive staff and dignified treatment of residents. Request a written fee breakdown during your visit, noting that fees vary by care level and room type.

title: Emerald Nursing Care Home (USJ & Subang Jaya Elderly Home) 梳邦再也疗养院
longitude: Emerald Nursing Care Home (USJ & Subang Jaya Elderly Home) 梳邦再也疗养院 is a nursing home located in Putra Heights, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 4 reviews.
care_types: Nursing home, Rehabilitation center, Wellness center
editorial_summary:
Emerald Nursing Care Home (USJ & Subang Jaya Elderly Home, 梳邦再也疗养院) is listed in Putra Heights — a planned township in southern Subang Jaya served by the LRT and within reasonable distance of Sunway Medical Centre and Subang Jaya Medical Centre. The Google listing categorises it as a nursing home, rehabilitation centre, and wellness centre, suggesting a broader rehabilitation-and-recovery focus alongside residential care. The home is contactable on +60 18-987 3862.

The home keeps a low online profile and does not maintain a published website or active social media page, so most service detail is best gathered directly. The Mandarin name (梳邦再也疗养院) suggests the home likely serves the local Chinese community in Putra Heights and surrounding USJ neighbourhoods. Care scope, bed count, staffing, and JKM registration are best confirmed on a call.

Families considering this home should call ahead to establish current admission availability and what rehabilitation programmes are run on-site. Useful questions: physiotherapy and occupational-therapy capability, registered-nurse cover, doctor-visit frequency, languages used by staff, dependency levels accepted, monthly fees, and the JKM licence number. The Putra Heights location is a practical asset for families anticipating hospital escalations.

title: Mega Senior Care Centre
longitude: Mega Senior Care Centre is a nursing home located in Sek 9, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 35 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Mega Senior Care Centre is located at 29 Jalan Pantai 9/7, Seksyen 9, 46000 Petaling Jaya — a shophouse unit in a quiet residential street in PJ's older, well-established Section 9 neighbourhood. Despite the Google Maps area tag of 'Sek 9', the address firmly places it in Petaling Jaya rather than Shah Alam. The centre holds a perfect 5-star Google rating from 35 reviews, a signal worth noting — though a small review base means individual experiences carry significant weight in that average. The website positions the centre across three service categories: retirement living, dementia and palliative care, and home nursing.

The centre's website describes 24-hour care by certified caregivers, four daily home-cooked meals, senior-friendly facilities, and a daily activity programme of gentle exercise and interactive games. Cultural celebrations — Chinese New Year, Mother's Day — and intergenerational visits from local university students suggest an active social calendar. The dementia and palliative care streams appear to be genuine offerings rather than marketing labels, though the clinical depth of each is not independently documented beyond what the website states.

Key practical gaps: bed capacity, pricing, staffing qualifications, and JKM licence details are all absent from the public record. The website names individual staff members (Emie, Siri, Kay, Momo, Pearl) and visitor reviews speak warmly of their attentiveness — but families should ask for written confirmation of nurse-to-resident ratios, whether a GP visits on a fixed schedule, and what the escalation pathway is if a resident deteriorates overnight. The home nursing arm is worth enquiring about separately if your family member is not yet ready for full residential admission.

title: PJ Care Centre
longitude: PJ Care Centre is a nursing home located in Seksyen 14, Selangor. It holds a 4.4/5 rating on Google Maps from 7 reviews.
editorial_summary:
PJ Care Centre's Seksyen 14 branch is a deliberately low-density nursing home at 20 Jalan 14/30, in the mature Seksyen 14 neighbourhood of Petaling Jaya. The centre caps its occupancy at 13 residents across a 4,000-plus sq ft property — a choice that prioritises privacy and one-to-one attention over throughput. Room configurations range from 3-bed sharing to single en-suite options, giving families a meaningful range of privacy levels within a single facility. The Google Maps listing carries a 4.4 rating from seven reviews. PJ Care Centre also operates a second branch, meaning it has institutional continuity beyond a single-owner setup.

Care at Seksyen 14 includes 24-hour monitoring by qualified caregivers covering bathing, grooming, feeding, and mobility assistance. Daily home-cooked meals run from breakfast through dessert with fresh fruit, and dietary consultation is available. Medication management is handled by what the facility describes as a licensed geriatric pharmacist — an unusual and noteworthy capability for a home of this size. The outdoor garden produces organic fruit and vegetables and doubles as an activity space, with residents participating in group exercise, dancing, and mahjong. On-site resident nurses are mentioned, though exact nurse-to-resident ratios are not published.

With a 13-person cap, prospective families should expect a waitlist and should enquire early. Pricing is not advertised; given the upscale positioning and low density, fees are likely above mid-market. Clarify whether in-house nursing extends to residents requiring IV medication, wound care, or nasogastric tube management, as the published scope focuses more on quality-of-life programming than clinical complexity. The Seksyen 14 central location places it within a few minutes of Pantai Hospital Kuala Lumpur and PJ city amenities.

title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ @ My Manor
longitude: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ @ My Manor is a retirement home located in Pjs 7, Selangor. It holds a 4.5/5 rating on Google Maps from 142 reviews.
care_types: Retirement home, Home health care service
editorial_summary:
My Aged Care's My Manor is the flagship property of My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, a licensed elder care operator in Petaling Jaya described on its own website as established in 2008. My Manor sits behind the MBPJ City Council building in PJS 7, occupies roughly 10,000 square feet, and is built around a low-density concept — fewer residents per floor than typical multi-storey nursing homes. The company now runs five Petaling Jaya locations in total, with My Manor being the original.

Care tiers at My Manor span general assisted living, bedridden care, post-stroke rehabilitation, post-operative recovery, and palliative care. The website states 24-hour care with hourly rounds and a doctor on-call arrangement. Six meals daily and laundry are included. The facility also offers home nursing and ambulance services as an extension of its main care offering. An in-house physiotherapy programme supports residents recovering from stroke or surgery.

Pricing was not published on the facility's own site, but third-party sources list room options across the My Aged Care network ranging from RM 3,001 to RM 5,000 per month depending on room type and care level. Families considering My Manor should confirm current bed availability directly — the low-density model means capacity is limited. Ask specifically about the doctor on-call arrangement (response times, whether a GP or specialist), nurse-to-resident ratios at night, and JKM registration status.

title: Mega Nursing Home
longitude: Mega Nursing Home is a nursing home located in Taman Aman, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 2 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Mega Nursing Home is located at No. 1, Jalan 14/47A in Taman Aman, Petaling Jaya — a mature residential neighbourhood close to the Federal Highway, about 3 km from Pantai Hospital and accessible to SJMC. The facility is classified as both a nursing home and assisted living facility, indicating it caters to a spectrum from ambulant residents needing support through to fully dependent cases. Its description emphasises a home-like environment with a serene courtyard, wet-room ensuite bedrooms, and a large multi-functional room — language that prioritises comfort without claiming a resort finish.

Mega Nursing Home provides 24-hour specialist care, with access to trained staff covering nursing, pharmacy, and physician services along with on-site diagnostic capabilities. Residents include individuals living with dementia, diabetes, hypertension, and depression, and the facility is reported to care for residents aged over 100 years — suggesting comfort with long-term, complex chronic care. Religious, laundry, and housekeeping services are included, and a daily activities calendar covers exercise and recreational programming. A second phone number (+60 12-777 5582) appears in some directories alongside the number in our data.

Only two Google reviews (5 stars) are available, which limits independent verification. Pricing is not publicly listed. Families should clarify what "physician services" means in practice — whether a doctor is on-site daily or visits periodically — and confirm the extent of dementia-specific capabilities, including whether they have a designated dementia unit or manage it in a mixed-ward setting. Asking about night nursing ratios and escalation protocols will help gauge how they handle medical emergencies.

title: Unique elderly care centre
longitude: Unique elderly care centre is a nursing home located in Seksyen 17, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 1 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Unique Elderly Care Centre is based at No. 22, Jalan 17/1 in Seksyen 17, Petaling Jaya — a well-connected residential address close to both the LDP and Federal Highway, with Assunta Hospital and Pantai Medical Centre within 10–15 minutes. The centre operates 24 hours daily and explicitly lists a broad clinical capability set on its website, making it one of the more transparent facilities in the Petaling Jaya market. It serves residents requiring moderate to high nursing dependency, including those with dementia.

Published capabilities include wound management, tracheostomy care, catheter management, colostomy care, and palliative care — suggesting the centre can support end-of-life residents. Clinical equipment listed includes hospital beds, ripple and air mattresses, oxygen machines, and suction machines. In-house nurses are on staff, with physiotherapy available and a doctor-on-call arrangement. The facility also offers transportation for scheduled hospital visits, 24-hour CCTV monitoring, and day care in addition to long-term residential placements. Meals are described as local Chinese cuisine with five servings daily; amenities include Astro, WiFi, and newspapers.

Pricing and exact bed count are not published on the centre's website, so families should enquire directly for current rates. The Chinese food emphasis suggests the centre's primary demographic is Chinese-speaking residents; families from other backgrounds should ask about meal flexibility. It is worth confirming the doctor-on-call arrangement — whether this is a retained GP or an on-demand service — and what the caregiver-to-resident ratio looks like during night hours. The JKM licence number should be requested and cross-checked as a standard step.

title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ Old Town
longitude: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ Old Town is a nursing home located in Pjs 4, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 92 reviews.
editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ PJ Old Town occupies premises along Jalan Othman and Jalan Terus in the heart of PJ Old Town, placing it within easy reach of Assunta Hospital and the broader Petaling Jaya healthcare corridor. Founded in 2006 and built by a team the company says brings over 60 years of combined healthcare experience, it is one of five branches operated by My Aged Care Sdn Bhd across Petaling Jaya. The facility is licensed as a nursing home and primarily serves elderly residents requiring full-time nursing care, including those recovering from surgery or stroke and those with dementia.

The website lists 24-hour nursing care with hourly monitoring rounds as the operational baseline, backed by an on-call doctor available around the clock. Six dietitian-approved meals are served daily — a feature the company highlights across all its branches. Published care types include nursing home, dementia care, bedridden care, respite care, post-operative and post-stroke rehabilitation, and palliative care. Room configurations across the My Aged Care network run from dormitory to twin-sharing and single occupancy in what the company describes as hotel-style settings. Network-wide pricing is listed at RM 3,001–5,000 per month, with a general care package starting from RM 2,500 and critical-care options priced separately.

As one of several My Aged Care branches sharing the same phone line (+60 17-851 7500) and management structure, families should confirm which specific services are available at the PJ Old Town site versus other branches before admission. The Google rating of 4.8 from 92 reviews is encouraging, but independent verification of staffing ratios, the dementia unit setup, and licence status is worth doing in person. Pricing transparency is reasonable for the network, but ask whether the quoted monthly fee covers nursing procedures, physiotherapy sessions, and incontinence supplies — these are often itemised separately.

title: De Luxe Retirement Nursing Home Petaling Jaya (House 39)
longitude: De Luxe Retirement Nursing Home Petaling Jaya (House 39) is a nursing home located in Kampung Tunku, Selangor. It holds a 4.9/5 rating on Google Maps from 9 reviews.
care_types: Nursing home, Adult day care center, Assisted living facility
editorial_summary:
De Luxe Retirement Home is a family-run operator with over 25 years in service, founded in 2000 by Susan Lim and co-founded with Dr. Kay Chee Goh — a geriatric practitioner with 40-plus years of experience who serves as the in-house doctor. The House 39 branch referred to here corresponds to the Kampung Tunku location at 37–39, Jalan SS 1/2, one of three licensed branches (the others at Jalan SS 1/1 Kampung Tunku and Jalan SS 3/39 Taman Universiti). The operator publishes its website at deluxerh.com and is fully licensed, with approvals from fire safety, Ministry of Health, Ministry of Social Welfare, and Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ).

De Luxe's website details a meaningful clinical capability: skilled nursing care for bedridden and stroke patients, medication management, wound care, NG-tube and PEG-tube feeding, patient lifting assistance, dementia and Alzheimer's care, Parkinson's rehabilitation, and daily assisted exercises. Physiotherapy and acupuncture are available on demand. Room options run from private air-conditioned rooms with ensuite bathrooms to more affordable shared rooms. Meals are described as nutritious Chinese home-cooked, which gives a sense of the cultural orientation. General Manager Alyssa Lim has aged care experience spanning Malaysia, Singapore, and Australia, suggesting the management has familiarity with international care standards. A 4.9 Google rating from 9 reviews is positive but based on a small sample.

With three branches in SS1 and SS3, the Kampung Tunku area is well-served by the De Luxe network, and the proximity to major PJ hospitals (Assunta, Columbia Asia, Beacon) is a practical advantage. Pricing is not published but the operator accepts WhatsApp inquiries at +60 12-291 3336. The listed categories include adult day care, which makes it a potential option for families who need daytime support without full residential placement. Ask specifically about the Kampung Tunku House 39 bed count, which rooms are in that branch versus the adjacent properties, and wait-list position if immediate placement is needed.

title: Pj Senior Care
longitude: Pj Senior Care is a nursing home located in Seksyen 14, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 105 reviews.
editorial_summary:
PJ Senior Care is a nursing home at 23, Jalan 14/56 in Seksyen 14, Petaling Jaya — a short distance from the Amazing Grace Retirement Home on Jalan 14/55, making Section 14 a notable cluster of elder care options in PJ. The facility's stated focus is on geriatric, orthopaedic, and neurological rehabilitation, with a team the website describes as comprising physiotherapists, nurses, and doctors trained specifically in stroke and mobility care.

The mission statement emphasises using physiotherapy and nursing together to improve both physical and mental wellbeing. In-house physiotherapy, geriatric nursing, medication management, nutritional guidance, and hospital follow-ups are listed as core services. The facility also organises festival celebrations and birthday events for residents. A standby doctor is noted as available on-site. The operating schedule is Monday to Sunday, 10am to 7pm, which raises the question of how overnight medical coverage is structured — this should be confirmed.

Pricing and bed count are not published. The rehabilitation emphasis makes PJ Senior Care worth considering for post-stroke or post-orthopaedic surgery recovery cases within the PJ area, particularly families who want physiotherapy included rather than contracted separately. On a site visit, ask about the doctor's visiting frequency and after-hours protocol, overnight nurse staffing, and JKM registration. Confirm whether the physiotherapy programme is designed around individual patient goals or is a group exercise format.

title: Home Sweet Home Care Centre Jalan Gasing
editorial_summary:
Home Sweet Home Care Centre occupies a residential property at 43, Jalan Gasing in the Bukit Gasing area of Petaling Jaya, Selangor — a well-established hillside neighbourhood with easy access to PJ's commercial belt and the Klang Valley highway network. The home maintains a Facebook page at facebook.com/homesweethomecarecentre. Phone: +60 16-603 6821.

Four Google Maps photos document the facility. Care details and capacity are not published beyond what is visible on the Facebook page.

Three reviews average 5.0 stars. Reviewers describe friendly, accommodating staff and a reassuring atmosphere where families feel confident leaving their loved ones. Confirm care types offered, JKM licence and monthly fees during your first call or visit.

title: Pearl Care Elderly Center Sdn. Bhd.
longitude: Pearl Care Elderly Center Sdn. Bhd. is a nursing home located in Seksyen 12, Selangor. It holds a 4.9/5 rating on Google Maps from 16 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pearl Care Elderly Center operates from Seksyen 12, Petaling Jaya — one of PJ's established residential sections near Jalan Kemajuan — and is part of a growing multi-branch network with additional locations in Sections 5, 11, 12A, and Sunway, all within Petaling Jaya. The facility is registered as a Sdn. Bhd. incorporated in August 2020 with registration number 1379500H, and the Google Maps listing categorises it as both a nursing home and a retirement home, indicating a mixed resident profile spanning independent seniors through to high-dependency cases. A 4.9-star rating from 16 reviewers is consistently positive.

The website states that Pearl Care takes residents with severe illness including stroke, coma, tube feeding, and terminal diagnoses such as cancer — placing it firmly in the skilled nursing home category rather than pure assisted living. Clinical services include wound care, medication management, catheterisation, respiratory support, physiotherapy, and assisted daily living. A doctor on-call model is cited, and 24-hour nursing by certified nurses is stated. Three main meals plus two snacks are provided daily, along with laundry, housekeeping, an emergency call system in each room, and a 24-hour mobility vehicle service for medical transport. Pricing is advertised as starting from RM 1,500 per month, which is relatively accessible for PJ; exact rates by room type and care level should be confirmed by phone.

The Seksyen 12 office number — +60 3-7890 2552 — is the direct contact for this branch, with the group hotline at +60 12-525 5594 for general enquiries. Visiting hours operate between 8:30 AM and 7:30 PM. The website supports Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), English, and Malay, suggesting a multilingual-friendly service, though specific caregiver language competencies should be asked about directly. JKM licence number is not published online — request it during your visit.

title: AMAZING GRACE RETIREMENT HOME
longitude: AMAZING GRACE RETIREMENT HOME is a nursing home located in Section 14, Selangor. It holds a 4.9/5 rating on Google Maps from 140 reviews.
care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living facility, Physical therapist
editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Retirement Home is located at 11, Jalan 14/55 in Section 14, Petaling Jaya, and is one of two properties operated under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand in the Petaling Jaya–Subang Jaya corridor. The Section 14 address is a residential area with strong transport links, which makes hospital follow-ups and family visits manageable. The facility is listed under nursing home, assisted living, and physiotherapy on Google Maps, suggesting a higher care ceiling than the SS19 sister property.

The website and third-party sources describe wound care, bedsore management, pain management, physiotherapy, and 24-hour observation as offered services. Short-term, long-term, day care, and intermittent care options are available. The physical environment is described as including garden space and a massage chair. Published pricing from third-party aggregators places the facility in the RM 2,001–RM 4,000 per month range. Reviewers highlight consistent vital signs monitoring and daily exercise assistance for residents with mobility limitations.

The facility appears appropriate for seniors needing nursing-level support — wound care, bedsore prevention, post-hospitalisation monitoring — in a quieter residential setting. The Google Maps listing includes 'Physical therapist' as a care type, so verify whether physiotherapy is provided by an in-house registered physiotherapist or an external contractor visiting on a schedule. Also confirm JKM registration, whether nursing staff are on-site overnight, and the process for escalating residents who deteriorate to acute care.

title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in Petaling Jaya @ Jalan Assunta
longitude: My Aged Care Nursing Home in Petaling Jaya @ Jalan Assunta is a retirement home located in 4/52, Selangor. It holds a 4.4/5 rating on Google Maps from 85 reviews.
editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ Jalan Assunta sits at 9 Jalan Assunta 4/52, Petaling Jaya, placing it immediately adjacent to Assunta Hospital — one of the most clinically useful locations any nursing home can occupy in the PJ area. Like its sister branch in PJ Old Town, it is operated by My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, a licensed elder care provider founded in 2006 by medical professionals. Google Maps lists this site under 'Retirement home', though the company's own service range covers skilled nursing and medical care. The shared phone number (+60 17-851 7500) routes all five branches through a single enquiry line.

The website indicates the same operating model across the network: 24-hour nursing care with hourly monitoring rounds, an on-call doctor available at all times, six meals per day, laundry services, and structured daily activities. Room configurations run from dormitory to single occupancy. Published care capabilities include post-operative and post-stroke rehabilitation, dementia care, bedridden care, respite, and palliative care, with physiotherapy listed as an add-on service priced at RM 1,500 per month (12 sessions) across the network. The Jalan Assunta location's proximity to Assunta Hospital makes transfers and specialist consultations significantly more practical than at more remote sites.

Families visiting this branch should confirm on-site capacity and staffing levels, as the company markets multiple branches under one brand and it is not always clear which capabilities are at which location. The proximity to Assunta Hospital is a genuine practical advantage, particularly for residents who need regular specialist follow-up. Ask specifically about nursing-procedure fees, dementia unit arrangements, and whether the on-call doctor is the same individual covering multiple branches simultaneously.

title: St. Mary's Nursing Home
longitude: St. Mary's Nursing Home is a nursing home located in Pj Old Town, Selangor. It holds a 4.9/5 rating on Google Maps from 19 reviews.
care_types: Nursing home, Adult day care center, Assisted living facility
editorial_summary:
St. Mary's Nursing Home PJ Old Town is at No. 22, Jalan Othman, 46000 Petaling Jaya — in PJ Old Town's established residential grid, minutes from Jalan Gasing and well within the central PJ catchment. It is one branch of St. Mary's Nursing & Aged Home Sdn. Bhd., a brand founded in 1985 by Mdm Sonia (a UK-trained nurse), making it one of the longest-running private elder care names in the Klang Valley. The PJ Old Town branch carries a 4.9 Google rating across 19 reviews — among the highest scores in the PJ area.

The St. Mary's network offers elderly day care, private nursing, assisted living, short-term respite care, and additional specialised services. The care philosophy is built around four stated principles: empathy, dignity, excellence, and diversity. Operating since 1985 and expanding to multiple KL and PJ branches signals operational stability. The 24-hour care model with hourly rounds, 24-hour doctor-on-call, six daily meals, laundry, and daily activities is described across the network's materials. The Jalan Othman location also benefits from a second nearby branch at 31, Jalan Othman, suggesting the network has a strong foothold in this particular micro-area.

As a multi-branch organisation, families should verify that the specific PJ Old Town branch at No. 22 can accommodate their relative's clinical level — not all branches necessarily carry the same medical staffing depth. Bed capacity and pricing for this branch are not published online; contact +60 17-276 3551 to enquire. Given the long track record and strong rating, this is a sensible first-visit candidate for families based in PJ, particularly those seeking a combination of day care and live-in care for mixed-dependency households.

title: Genesis Life Care Centre PJ
longitude: Genesis Life Care Centre PJ is an assisted living centre located in Taman Petaling Utama, Selangor. It holds a 4.8/5 rating on Google Maps from 22 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care Centre PJ is the flagship location of a five-branch Selangor-and-Johor nursing home group, occupying a purpose-built space at Lot 1, Petaling Utama Avenue, Jalan PJS 1/50 in Taman Petaling Utama, Petaling Jaya. Established in 2020, the PJ centre is the group's most clinically well-resourced site — with 50-plus beds, a dedicated memory care wing, and a rehabilitation suite staffed by senior physiotherapists and occupational therapists. It holds Ministry of Health approval and a 4.9-star Google rating across 58 reviews, the strongest rating in the Genesis network.

The clinical offer here is broader than most community nursing homes. Dementia and Alzheimer's residents are placed in the dedicated memory care wing, overseen by psychologists who run structured cognitive stimulation programmes tailored to individual residents. Stroke rehabilitation, post-operative recovery, and palliative care are all offered as distinct care pathways rather than being bundled under generic "nursing home" care. The 30-plus person care team includes specialist therapists, nursing supervisors, and 24/7 nursing availability. Pricing starts from RM2,500/month — competitive for PJ — with costs scaling by dependency level and room type. Visiting hours are 9am–6pm daily.

Genesis Life Care PJ is one of the more transparent operators in Selangor: the website publishes care pathway descriptions, team credentials, location details, and a cost calculator. Families should use the calculator as a starting point, then call +60 19-295 0457 to confirm current bed availability in the specific ward needed (memory care vs general nursing vs rehab). Ask about the staffing ratio overnight versus daytime, the psychological programme intensity for dementia residents, and what the discharge pathway looks like for post-op recovery patients who stabilise. The 2020 establishment date means the centre is relatively new — confirm how the team handles first-ever complex care scenarios.

title: Golden Mansion Senior Care Centre
longitude: Golden Mansion Senior Care Centre is a nursing home located in Taman Universiti, Selangor. It holds a 4.9/5 rating on Google Maps from 72 reviews.
care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living facility, Retirement home
editorial_summary:
Golden Mansion Senior Care Centre is located at No. 7, Jalan SS3/39, Taman Universiti, 47300 Petaling Jaya — a residential neighbourhood in the western PJ corridor, roughly equidistant from Subang Jaya Medical Centre and Assunta Hospital. The facility positions itself across three care categories: nursing home, assisted living, and retirement home. Its Google rating of 4.9 from 72 reviews is the highest in this batch and points to strong family satisfaction. The centre's care scope, as listed on its website, includes senior citizen care, dementia nursing, palliative care, stroke and cancer care, and home nursing services.

The facility indicates the use of licensed nurses providing 24-hour coverage, medical beds in all rooms, senior-friendly safety features (handrails, slip-resistant flooring, shower chairs, elevated toilet seats), and landscaped gardens for outdoor time. Three main meals and two nutritional snacks are served daily. The dementia programme is described as person-centred, with therapeutic activities aimed at maintaining cognitive function and social engagement. The palliative care offer is described as holistic, covering physical, emotional, and spiritual dimensions with a multidisciplinary team — though specific staffing qualifications are not detailed publicly. Laundry is included, and transport can be arranged.

Pricing is not disclosed on the website; families should call +60 18-233 0817 directly. Given the premium positioning and the breadth of services listed (dementia, palliative, stroke care simultaneously), it is worth asking how many beds the facility has, what proportion are allocated to each care category, and what the nurse-to-resident ratio looks like across day and night shifts. Confirm whether the 'multidisciplinary team' for palliative care includes a visiting palliative physician or is caregiver-led, and whether the dementia unit is a segregated wing or integrated with general nursing.

title: My Precious Home Care Retirement Home
editorial_summary:
My Precious Home Care Retirement Home is the Petaling Jaya branch of the My Precious Home Care operator, located at 38, Jalan SS1/39 in Kampung Tunku, a mature residential neighbourhood near the SS1 and Jalan Utama corridor in PJ. The operator was founded by a doctor who maintains active medical involvement in resident oversight.

Care services mirror those at the sibling USJ branch: 24-hour personal care, dementia and palliative care, in-house physiotherapy, monitoring visits and four daily home-cooked meals. All rooms feature attached bathrooms and wheelchair access. Website: myprecioushomecare.com; contact: +60 11-7489 9115.

Eleven Google Maps reviews average 5.0 stars. One reviewer described a tour of the PJ bungalow as markedly different from a typical nursing home experience, noting a genuinely comfortable, spacious environment and residents who appeared content. Pricing is not listed on the operator website — request a written quote when you visit.

title: AMAZING GRACE SENIOR HOME
longitude: AMAZING GRACE SENIOR HOME is a retirement home located in Ss 19, Selangor. It holds a 5.0/5 rating on Google Maps from 156 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Senior Home is a residential elder care facility located at Jalan SS19/4 in SS19, Subang Jaya. It operates under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand, which also runs a separate property in Section 14, Petaling Jaya. The SS19 home is listed as a retirement home on Google Maps, and published third-party listings place it in the category of assisted living rather than high-dependency nursing care.

Third-party pricing aggregators list room options ranging from RM 2,001 to RM 4,000 per month across Amazing Grace's locations, with the SS19 branch at the higher end of that range (RM 3,001–RM 4,000). Room types available include single rooms with and without attached toilet, twin rooms, and triple occupancy. Reviewers note clean surroundings, responsive staff, and regular monitoring of vital signs. Daily exercise is offered, including for residents with mobility limitations. The home reports not using a computer-based resident management system, which is worth factoring in if you need structured documentation.

This home suits relatively mobile or semi-mobile seniors needing supported daily living in a residential setting, rather than complex medical or dementia care. Families should verify directly whether nursing staff are on-site overnight, what the policy is for residents who deteriorate medically, and whether JKM registration is in place. Given it operates across two sites, also confirm which management team covers the SS19 branch.

title: Pearl Care Elderly Home
longitude: Pearl Care Elderly Home is a nursing home located in Seksyen 12 Petaling Jaya, Selangor. It holds a 4.6/5 rating on Google Maps from 62 reviews.
editorial_summary:
Pearl Care Elderly Home's Section 12 branch sits at Jalan 12/16, Seksyen 12, Petaling Jaya — a residential pocket that is conveniently close to both University Malaya Medical Centre (PPUM) and Assunta Hospital, two of the strongest hospital options in the PJ area. Pearl Care is a multi-branch operator with four homes across PJ (Seksyen 5 Bukit Gasing, Section 11, Sunway Taman Desaria, and Section 12). The network has built a clinical reputation for accepting residents with high-dependency needs including stroke, coma, tube feeding (PEG), terminal cancer, and palliative care — a broader medical intake than many community nursing homes.

The company lists 13+ specialised care services across its network, including 24-hour nursing care by certified nurses, stroke rehabilitation, dementia care, wound care, medication management, urinary catheterisation, suctioning, palliative care, physiotherapy, dietary planning with five meal options, and a 24-hour medical escort vehicle. Doctor on-call is available, and regular doctor consultations are structured into care. Room configurations run from four-bed shared to single, described as hotel-like. Published pricing starts from RM 1,500 per month — the most accessible entry price in this batch — though higher-dependency residents will sit at a higher tier. Office hours are 8:30 am–7:30 pm with a 24-hour hotline.

The RM 1,500 starting price will attract high enquiry volumes, so confirm the actual price bracket applicable to your family member's dependency level early in the conversation. Given the multi-branch structure, ask whether the Section 12 branch specifically has a qualified physiotherapist on-site or operates on a visiting schedule, and confirm overnight nursing staffing levels — particularly relevant if your family member requires suctioning, PEG management, or other nursing procedures on an unscheduled basis. The proximity to PPUM is a genuine asset for residents needing specialist backup.

title: Clover Healthcare Sdn Bhd
longitude: Clover Healthcare Sdn Bhd is a care home located in Taman Nusa Peristis, Johor. The home accommodates up to 20 residents.
editorial_summary:
Clover Care Centre is a licensed nursing home operated by Clover Healthcare Sdn. Bhd. at 2 Jalan Nusa Perintis 4/10, Taman Nusa Peristis, Gelang Patah — in the western reaches of Johor Bahru close to the Second Link corridor. The home was founded by Dr Tee Hua Tiong, a General Practitioner who graduated from the National University of Singapore and who also operates a GP clinic in Pontian, giving the facility a medically-grounded ownership model that is less common among smaller nursing homes. The two-storey bungalow setting is compact but purpose-adapted, with a lift installed, handicap-accessible toilets fitted with grab bars and shower seats, and a nurse-call system throughout.

The facility offers a broad clinical service menu for its size: general nursing and long-term care, elderly day care, post-hospitalisation recovery, short-term and respite care, palliative care, tracheotomy care, and wound care management. Registered staff nurses are on duty, and the doctor-founder's involvement provides a clearer medical oversight pathway than at purely caregiver-staffed homes. With 20 beds on record, it is a small but clinically capable home — the kind of setting that suits families who want a more intensive nursing focus without the scale of a large institutional facility. The contact number is 07-513-0034.

Because the website appears to be offline at the time of writing, most service detail is best confirmed by phone or in person. Families should ask about current bed availability given the 20-bed ceiling, what conditions the clinical team is most experienced managing (wound care and tracheotomy are signalled as strengths), visiting arrangements, and the pricing structure for different dependency levels. The Gelang Patah location is convenient for families based in Nusa Jaya or the Second Link area, though those coming from central Johor Bahru should account for the distance.

title: JB Care Centre
editorial_summary:
JB Care Centre is a nursing home situated in Taman Sri Tebrau, Johor Bahru, operating from Jalan Rentaka. The home accepts elderly residents requiring general nursing care and daily assistance, and has a capacity of around 40 beds. It is one of several independent nursing homes in this part of JB, serving residents in the Tebrau and surrounding corridor.

The home is reachable at 07-3331999 for administrative enquiries. Specific clinical capabilities, staffing details, and visiting hours are not published, and the facility's website is currently inaccessible, which limits what families can verify online before visiting.

Anyone considering JB Care Centre should call directly to ask about current bed availability, the scope of nursing care offered, and overnight staffing arrangements. It is best suited for residents who need general daily support and supervision rather than intensive medical management.

title: Green Acres Home Care
editorial_summary:
Green Acres Home Care has been providing elderly residential care in Johor Bahru since the 1990s, operating from two bungalow-style homes: the main branch at Taman Bukit Kempas on a hilltop site, and a second location at Kim Teng Park off Jalan Intan. The bungalow format gives both homes a genuinely residential feel that sets them apart from the shophouse nursing homes common in JB, and visiting hours run daily from 11am to 6pm.

The clinical offering is solid for a smaller private home. Confirmed services include oxygen therapy, wound dressing, physiotherapy, dietician-controlled meal planning, and ambulance transport. On-call doctors attend when needed, and a qualified Home Manager oversees daily operations. The team is contactable via WhatsApp and a direct mobile line (016-7150393).

Green Acres suits families looking for care that balances clinical support with a homely, non-institutional setting. Doctors are on-call rather than in-house, so residents requiring continuous medical monitoring should be assessed carefully before placement. Pricing is competitive but not published online — call or WhatsApp for a quote based on the level of care needed.

title: Pusat Warga Emas Hillville
longitude: Pusat Warga Emas Hillville is a care home located in Johor. The home accommodates up to 64 residents.
editorial_summary:
Hillville Aged Care Home is a licensed residential care facility set in Taman Ponderosa, Johor Bahru, within a quiet neighbourhood off Persiaran Ponderosa Utama. The home can accommodate up to 64 residents ranging in age from their sixties through to their nineties, and its stated mission is shaped by Christian values — phrases on the Facebook page reference care "inspired by the teachings of the Lord Jesus Christ," which suggests a faith-inflected environment rather than a denomination-restricted admission policy. Care extends to elderly individuals with a range of conditions including Alzheimer's disease, mobility impairments, and those who are wheelchair-bound, pointing to a genuinely mixed-dependency resident profile rather than a purely ambulatory population.

The home's publicly documented care scope covers long-term residential care, dementia and memory support, and palliative care. With 64 beds it sits at a medium scale for the JB market, large enough to maintain dedicated staffing structures but small enough that individual residents can reasonably expect continuity of carers. The Facebook page at HillvilleAgedCareHome is the facility's primary public channel and carries photo updates on resident activities and daily life, giving families a reasonable window into the home's atmosphere. Specific details on nurse-to-resident ratios, visiting doctor schedules, and physiotherapy availability are not published online.

Families considering Hillville should contact the home directly on 07-357 8500 to discuss current availability, room configurations, and monthly pricing — these are not disclosed publicly. Given the Christian-inspired ethos, it is worth confirming how that shapes meal provision and daily programming for residents of different faiths. Useful questions for a site visit include the ratio of nursing staff on overnight shifts, how dementia-stage residents are accommodated (separate wing or integrated), and what the palliative care pathway looks like when a resident's needs escalate.

title: Rumah Seri Kenangan- Johor Bahru
editorial_summary:
Rumah Seri Kenangan Johor Bahru (RSK JB) is a government-run welfare institution operated by Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat (JKM) and one of ten such homes nationwide. Located in Kampung Ungku Mohsin, Johor Bahru, it has long served as a safety net for elderly Malaysians aged 60 and above who have no permanent housing, no income, and no family to rely on. With a listed capacity of 300 beds, it is among the largest public residential facilities for the aged in Johor, providing fully subsidised care funded by the federal government.

Services at RSK JB cover the full spectrum of basic welfare needs: shelter, meals, medical treatment, occupational therapy, physiotherapy, counselling, and organised recreational activities. The facility is monitored and regulated by JKM and operates under a structured three-year residency model. A major upgrade project worth RM57.3 million is currently under construction on the same site — the modernised complex will accommodate 260 residents in a new cluster dormitory (130 male, 130 female) alongside a medical clinic, multi-purpose hall, laundry, prayer room, and ten-storey staff quarters, with completion targeted for June 2027.

Because RSK JB is a public welfare institution rather than a private care home, the admission route is distinct: applications go through any District Community Welfare Office and require JKM approval at the Director General level. Placement is reserved for those who meet the strict eligibility criteria — no property, no income, no next of kin — so it is not an option for most fee-paying families. During the active construction phase it is worth confirming the current operational arrangements and whether resident capacity is temporarily reduced. Families seeking a state-funded placement for a truly destitute elder should begin the application at the nearest JKM district office.

title: Pine Tree Home Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Pine Tree Home Care Centre is located at 277, Lorong Setia, Jalan Parit Mesjid, 82000 Pontian District, Johor — a semi-rural residential area in the southern part of Johor, approximately 45km south-west of Johor Bahru. Phone: +60 19-776 6666.

Two photos are available on Google Maps. Care details, capacity and pricing are not published online. No website is available.

One review rates it 3.0 stars but contains no written comments. Families in the Pontian area have limited nursing home options in the immediate district; confirm care types accepted, JKM licence status and monthly fees by phone before making the trip to visit.

title: Comfort Old Folks Home Centre
longitude: Comfort Old Folks Home Centre is a care home located in Johor. The home accommodates up to 22 residents.
editorial_summary:
Comfort Old Folks Home Centre is a small residential care home in Johor operating at a capacity of 22 beds, placing it among the more intimate facilities in the state. Its phone number carries a 07 area code consistent with the Johor Bahru district, and the home appears to serve the local community as a neighbourhood-scale care option rather than a branded regional provider. With a tightly managed resident count, the environment can offer a domestic, family-like atmosphere where individual residents are known by name to all staff — a quality that families with elderly parents who struggle in larger institutional settings sometimes actively seek out.

The home keeps a low online profile, and most service detail is best gathered on a call or in person. Publicly available data does not specify the care types covered, staffing structure, or whether the home holds a Ministry of Health licence under the Care Centre Act 2018 — all of which are important baselines to confirm. At 22 beds the facility likely operates with a lean team, which can mean close-knit care but also warrants checking how overnight and weekend cover is handled.

Families should ring 07-4322590 as the primary step and ask specifically about current bed availability, the mix of ambulatory and bed-bound residents, monthly pricing inclusive of meals and incontinence supplies, and the facility's licence status. A site visit before any commitment is strongly recommended to assess the physical environment, cleanliness, and how staff interact with current residents during the visit.

title: Pusat Jagaan Rumah Sejahtera Batu Pahat
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Rumah Sejahtera Batu Pahat is a non-profit elderly care home located at KM 7 (Batu 4), Jalan Kluang, 83000 Batu Pahat, within the Kompleks Penyayang welfare complex. Registered with the Registrar of Societies under registration number J/PJB WT 024/2021 and affiliated with the Majlis Pusat Kebajikan Semalaysia framework, the home operates as a charitable institution rather than a commercial care provider — a distinction that typically means fee structures are lower or contribution-based, and that the facility may receive government and community welfare support.

The home has a recorded capacity of 20 beds, which makes it a smaller, community-scale facility best suited to seniors who do not require intensive medical intervention but need daily assistance and supervised living. The Facebook page (oldfolkshomebp) appears to be the primary public communication channel. Office operations run Monday to Friday, 08:00–17:00, which reflects the charitable model; residential care continues around the clock even if administrative contact is weekday-only. The centre has also been associated with contact through a director (Ms. Tan) and secretary (Ms. Teh), suggesting a committee-run governance structure.

For families exploring this option, the key questions to ask on first contact are whether the home accepts private-paying residents or is means-tested, what nursing staff coverage looks like overnight, and what the process is for residents with more complex medical needs. Because it is a non-profit welfare home, expectations around medical infrastructure and therapy programmes should be calibrated accordingly — and confirming current vacancy against the 20-bed limit early in the process will save time.

title: Justlife Care Home
longitude: Justlife Care Home is a care home located in Johor. The home accommodates up to 32 residents.
editorial_summary:
Justlife Care Home is the original operating name for what is now better known as EHA Golfview Eldercare Mansion, located at Lot 115-630, Jalan Ros Merah 4/1, Taman Johor Jaya, Johor Bahru. The facility has been operating since 2009 — making it one of the longer-standing eldercare providers in JB — and has since grown into EHA ElderCare Group, a multi-site operator with branches across Johor and the Klang Valley. The Johor Jaya site sits adjacent to the Daiman 18 Golf Club and presents a distinctly resort-like setting compared with many conventional nursing homes in the area, with generous grounds and a green environment that form part of the brand's appeal.

EHA's published care model covers skilled nursing, assisted senior living, dementia care, physiotherapy and rehabilitation, and round-the-clock caregiver support. The facility operates under what the group calls a "Focused-Care" philosophy, tailoring care plans to maximise each resident's functional independence rather than defaulting to full dependency. Regular doctor visits, certified nursing staff, and structured social and activity programmes are documented across the EHA group's platforms. The 32-bed figure in our dataset may reflect an earlier licensed capacity; families should confirm current bed count and availability directly, as the group has expanded and refurbished individual sites over the years.

Pricing for the Johor Jaya site is not listed publicly, which is standard across the EHA group — a consultation call or enquiry form submission initiates the process. Families should ask specifically about the dementia care approach (whether there is a dedicated unit or integrated care), the staffing ratio on night shifts, and what the monthly fee covers versus what attracts surcharges. Given the rebranding to EHA, correspondence and Google Maps searches under that name will yield the most current contact and directions.

title: Pusat Jagaan Sri Sentosa
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Sri Sentosa is a small private care home situated in Taman Sentosa, Johor Bahru, with a listed capacity of 15 residents. The compact scale places it firmly in the community-home category — the kind of facility where residents often receive more personal, one-to-one attention than in a large institutional setting. The area of Taman Sentosa is a well-established residential neighbourhood within JB, giving families reasonable access from most parts of the city.

The facility operates with a phone-based enquiry process, and most detail on room types, care levels, staffing ratios, and daily routines is best gathered directly by calling 07-7718460. At this size, care capacity tends to be generalist rather than specialist — expect assistance with daily living, meals, and basic nursing support, though the extent of clinical services such as wound care, physiotherapy, or dementia management should be confirmed during a visit. The home keeps a low online profile, so most service detail is best gathered on a call or in person.

For families considering Sri Sentosa, a personal visit is especially worthwhile given the small resident population — touring the home gives a clear sense of the physical environment, caregiver-to-resident ratio, and the overall atmosphere. Key questions to raise include the availability of any nursing-qualified staff on shift, whether the home accepts residents with mobility limitations or incontinence needs, current vacancy status, and monthly fee structure including what is and is not included in the base rate.

title: AFH Elder Care
longitude: AFH Elder Care is a care home located in Selangor. The home accommodates up to 12 residents.
editorial_summary:
AFH Elder Care is a residential nursing home in Kepong, Kuala Lumpur, operating from Jalan Api Api. The facility is managed by a trained Matron and has been described by visitors as one of the cleaner homes in the Klang Valley — a recurring observation in online mentions. The home caters to elderly residents who need daily care and supervision in a residential setting.

Detailed clinical information including bed count, specific services, and staffing ratios is not publicly available, and the facility's website is currently inaccessible. Previous accounts describe a cosy and spacious environment with attentive staff, and pricing is understood to be competitive within the Kepong corridor. Enquiries are handled by phone at 03-6243 3824.

AFH Elder Care is worth considering for families living in Kepong, Sri Damansara, or the Selayang area who want a well-maintained local option. A visit in person before deciding is advisable — call ahead to confirm current availability and to assess whether the home's care scope matches your relative's specific needs.

title: Amazing Grace Caring Home
editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Caring Home operates multiple branches across Kuala Lumpur and Subang Jaya, providing residential nursing care to elderly residents since 2007. The network is guided by Christian values and accepts residents of all backgrounds. Branches include sites at Taman Tan Yew Lai, Taman Yarl (KL), Section 14 Petaling Jaya, and SS19 Subang Jaya. The operator publishes an indicative price band of RM 2,001 to RM 4,000 per month depending on room type — shared twin, triple, or single with attached bathroom — making it one of the few networks in this category that puts a real range online.

Services listed across the network include dementia management, post-stroke rehabilitation support, post-operative recovery, wound care, medication management, and ambulance transport. The Subang Jaya branch in particular has a strong Google and Facebook rating, and it is the branch behind the Trusted Malaysia mention that appears in some directory write-ups. Weekly chapel services are held at some branches for interested residents. JKM or MOH licensing has not been publicly confirmed across branches and should be verified per site.

The most important thing for any family considering Amazing Grace is that branch quality does not generalise. Each location operates independently — different building, different manager, different caregiver roster — and the good ratings concentrated at the Subang Jaya branch say nothing about the Taman Yarl, Section 14 or Taman Tan Yew Lai operations. The published RM 2,001-4,000 band is a network-level indication, not a quote for the specific branch under consideration; the actual rate for the branch you are visiting may sit anywhere inside that range or above it once consumables and care-level loading are added. Visit the exact branch where the bed is being offered, ask for that branch's JKM licence, current staffing ratios on day and night shifts, the visiting or in-house doctor arrangement, and a written quote that lists what the monthly fee covers and what is billed extra.

title: Amitabha Malaysia Old Folks Home KL
editorial_summary:
Amitabha Malaysia Old Folks Home operates from Happy Garden, off Jalan Kuchai Lama in Kuala Lumpur, as part of the Amitabha Foundation established in 1998. The Foundation runs a range of welfare services including a dialysis centre and an orphanage. Despite its Buddhist name, the home explicitly serves elderly residents of all religions, ethnicities, and backgrounds. Current capacity is approximately 30 residents.

The model here is welfare-based residential care: meals, shelter, daily supervision, and community activities including a weekly social programme on Thursday mornings. The organisation is registered as a charity and depends partly on public donations for operating costs. It is not a nursing home in the clinical sense — there are no confirmed nursing or rehabilitation capabilities, and the focus is on basic daily living support and social wellbeing.

This home is most appropriate for elderly individuals who are ambulatory, socially engaged, and do not require medical management or nursing care. Families with a loved one who needs daily nursing, physiotherapy, or clinical monitoring should look elsewhere. For those seeking an affordable, community-oriented option in the Kuchai Lama area, call 03-7890 3700 to understand the admission criteria and whether fees apply or admission is donation-based.

title: Dementia Care Centre
longitude: Dementia Care Centre is a care home located in Johor. The home accommodates up to 20 residents.
editorial_summary:
Dementia Care Centre is a small, specialist-titled residential home with a stated capacity of 20 beds, entered in the dataset under a Johor state classification but carrying a 03 prefix phone number (03-61222000) that corresponds to the Kuala Lumpur and Selangor dialling zone — an unusual combination that may indicate the facility sits near a state border, operates an administrative line from KL, or that there is a data nuance worth confirming directly with the home. The name itself signals a dementia focus, suggesting the home was established with cognitive-impairment residents specifically in mind rather than as a general elderly care facility that added dementia beds over time.

At 20 beds, this is a boutique-scale operation where consistent, familiar staffing is plausible. A dementia-specific home of this size will typically structure its environment around predictable routines, wandering-safe layouts, and sensory-appropriate activities — the practical details of how well these are implemented are best assessed on a site visit. The home keeps a low online profile, so most service detail is best gathered on a call. There is no publicly listed website or social media presence found in current searches, which means families will need to contact 03-61222000 directly for all substantive information including pricing, staffing, visiting policies, and licence details.

Priority questions for an initial call include: confirming the physical address (to reconcile the 03 area code with the Johor state classification), current bed availability, the stage range of dementia the home can accommodate, how medical emergencies are handled, and which hospital the home uses for referrals. A walk-through of the facility at a realistic time of day — not a scheduled open day — will give the clearest picture of day-to-day life for residents.

title: Dhaalia Elderly Care Centre
longitude: Dhaalia Elderly Care Centre is a care home located in Selangor. The home accommodates up to 20 residents.
editorial_summary:
Dhaalia Elderly Care Centre (DECC) has been providing residential care for elderly and disabled adults in the Old Klang Road area of Kuala Lumpur since 2006, operating from Taman Halimahton. With close to two decades of operation in the same location, it has become a known option for families in the Taman Yarl and Old Klang Road area looking for affordable, round-the-clock residential care. Dementia care has been listed among its services in multiple eldercare directories, alongside general nursing and personal care.

Clinical details beyond general nursing and dementia support are not publicly documented. The centre's website (deccmy.com) is currently inaccessible, and the facility is understood to use manual rather than digital administrative systems. The Facebook page (DECCKL) and phone numbers 03-79814729 and 019-389-2155 are the active contact points.

A visit in person before placement is strongly recommended, both to assess the physical environment and to understand how the home is run day-to-day. DECC is best suited for elderly residents with moderate daily care needs and a budget-conscious placement in the Old Klang Road corridor. Ask specifically about nursing qualifications on staff and how medical emergencies are handled overnight.

title: Merry Care Kepong Baru 2
longitude: Merry Care Centre (Jln Belinggai) is a care home located in Selangor. The home accommodates up to 13 residents.
editorial_summary:
Merry Care Kepong Baru 2 is one of six branches in the Merry Care chain, located at 53, Jalan Belinggai, Kepong Bahru, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. Merry Care is a six-branch nursing home chain operating across Kepong Baru, Desa Jaya, and Selayang Baru in the Klang Valley. The group markets itself at elderlycare.my and positions on the affordable end of the residential care market — a deliberate point of difference against private retirement homes that typically start north of RM 4,000 per month. With multiple homes clustered within walking distance of each other in Kepong Baru, families touring the chain often visit two or three branches in a single afternoon.

The operator publishes the same service bundle across all branches: medication management, daily items purchasing, hospital visitation, daily fitness activities, a 24-hour assistant on duty, and five meals a day with unlimited servings on request. Pricing is published on the operator website (elderlycare.my) starting from RM 2,500 per month for a shared room — a starting figure that, in practice, varies with the resident's actual care needs and dependency level. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Kepong Baru is a dense residential pocket in north-Kuala Lumpur with good access to Hospital Sungai Buloh and the Damansara hospital cluster (KPJ Damansara, Sunway, ParkCity Medical Centre). The neighbourhood is tightly built — terrace-house lots, narrow back lanes — so families should ask on a visit how natural light, communal space, and any outdoor compound are handled, how many residents share a room, how many caregivers cover each shift, whether a registered nurse is on-site overnight or on-call, and what specific clinical procedures the home performs in-house versus refers out (PEG feeds, oxygen, wound dressings, urinary catheter changes). Confirm the RM 2,500 starting rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure.

title: Merry Care Kepong Baru 1
editorial_summary:
Merry Care Kepong Baru 1 is one of six branches in the Merry Care chain, located at 42, Jalan Antoi 2, Kepong Baru, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. Merry Care is a six-branch nursing home chain operating across Kepong Baru, Desa Jaya, and Selayang Baru in the Klang Valley. The group markets itself at elderlycare.my and positions on the affordable end of the residential care market — a deliberate point of difference against private retirement homes that typically start north of RM 4,000 per month. With multiple homes clustered within walking distance of each other in Kepong Baru, families touring the chain often visit two or three branches in a single afternoon.

The operator publishes the same service bundle across all branches: medication management, daily items purchasing, hospital visitation, daily fitness activities, a 24-hour assistant on duty, and five meals a day with unlimited servings on request.
