Public transit routes to the facility have been mapped via Moovit, indicating it is reachable by bus for visiting families without private transport.

Families researching this home should call 012-9300233 to ask about the types of residents the centre accepts — including whether it handles bedridden, post-stroke, or dementia cases — as well as the nursing cover at night and the current room configuration and pricing. Confirming the JKM licence and the home's formal registration name will also help set expectations. Given the 20-bed scale, the home is likely to offer a more personalised ratio of carers to residents than a larger facility, which is worth raising directly in conversation.

title: Evagayle Care Home
longitude: Evagayle Care Home is a care home located in Petaling Jaya, Selangor. The home accommodates up to 25 residents.
editorial_summary:
Evagayle Care Home has been providing assisted living and nursing care in Petaling Jaya since 1999, making it one of the longer-established private care homes in the Klang Valley. The facility operates across three locations in PJ — Section 7/2 on Jalan Bukit Menteri Selatan, Section 9 on Jalan Pantai, and Section 18/15 in Taman Kanagapuram — all within a five-minute drive of University Malaya Medical Centre (PPUM) and Assunta Hospital. The batch entry lists 25 beds under the Petaling Jaya area. The home describes itself as a warm environment for retired, nursing, and convalescence patients, with a particular focus on residents who can no longer be cared for at home due to dementia, Alzheimer's disease, or degenerative conditions.

Clinically, Evagayle is equipped for a meaningful range of dependency levels: the website indicates fully adjustable hospital beds with ripple mattresses, oxygen therapy, suction machines, nebulisers, urine catheterisation, and tube (PEG) feeding. The care scope extends beyond the elderly to include post-operative recovery, stroke convalescence, chronic disease management (diabetes, COPD, cancer), and — less commonly for a nursing home — backup childcare for families in acute need. Residents receive five meals daily (three main and two snacks), physiotherapy, geriatric medical consultancy on request, and transportation to hospital and dental appointments. A partnership with First Ambulance Service provides 24/7 emergency ambulance availability.

With over two decades of operation and equipment that supports complex nursing needs, Evagayle is a solid option to shortlist for families dealing with dementia, neurological decline, or post-surgical recovery in the Petaling Jaya area. Pricing is not published online, so a call or WhatsApp to +6018 290 1919 is the starting point. Families should ask which specific branch has current capacity among the three PJ locations, and clarify the nurse-to-resident ratio on night shifts — a critical detail for high-dependency residents.

title: Stella Maris Home Plt Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Stella Maris Home PLT Care Centre is a small residential care facility located in Seksyen 6, Shah Alam, reachable on 03-79606070. The data sheet records a capacity of 11 beds, which places it in the very small, boutique tier of Selangor care homes — likely a converted residential property rather than a purpose-built facility. The PLT (Perkongsian Liabiliti Terhad, or limited liability partnership) business structure is common among small private care operators in Malaysia. No website or social media presence was found in public searches, suggesting this is a quietly operating local home without significant digital marketing.

With 11 beds and no published clinical profile, the most reasonable expectation is that Stella Maris Home provides basic residential and personal care — assistance with daily activities, meals, and companionship — rather than a full nursing or dementia-specialist programme. The Seksyen 6 Shah Alam address is a well-served residential area with reasonable proximity to Hospital Shah Alam and several private clinics. The modest scale may appeal to families who prefer a home-like setting with closer staff-to-resident ratios, provided the care level matches their relative's needs.

Because no online profile exists beyond the Google Maps listing, families should visit in person before committing to placement. Key questions to ask include: what nursing or caregiver qualifications the staff hold, whether a registered nurse is available at any point or if the home relies entirely on trained caregivers, what the monthly fee covers, and whether the home holds a current JKM licence. For residents with dementia, mobility challenges, or active medical needs, confirm that the physical layout and staffing model can safely accommodate these requirements.

title: Than Hsiang Wan Ching Yuen (PJ)
editorial_summary:
Wan Ching Yuen is a Buddhist elder-care home in Section 1, Petaling Jaya, run by the Than Hsiang Buddhist Welfare Association as part of its Mitra Welfare Centre network. The home was opened in November 2002 by Venerable Wei Wu and operates on a charitable rather than commercial basis, framed around the Buddhist principles of loving-kindness, compassion, joy and equanimity. The address at 14 Jalan 1/3, Seksyen 1, sits in PJ's older residential grid close to clinics and Assunta Hospital.

The home runs at a steady occupancy of around 29 residents against a 30-bed cap, with care delivered by trained nursing staff alongside regular volunteer programmes and group activities. Residents share communal living and are free to move about the house, socialise, and join Buddhist chanting sessions. Mandarin and other Chinese dialects dominate day-to-day communication, and meals follow vegetarian Buddhist conventions.

Wan Ching Yuen is best suited to Mandarin- or Hokkien-speaking elders comfortable in a Buddhist communal setting, and to families looking for charitable rather than profit-driven pricing. Vacancies turn over slowly given the small bed count and long stays. Families should ask about waiting-list policy, eligibility criteria for charitable rates, what level of nursing acuity is accepted, dementia and falls protocols, and whether private or twin rooms are available.

title: Corning Elderly Care Centre
editorial_summary:
Corning Elderly Care Centre (CECC) is a privately run nursing home at 7 Lorong 18/18b, Taman Sri Sentosa, Petaling Jaya — a residential side street just off Old Klang Road, an area with several elderly care homes in close proximity. The facility maintains a Facebook page (cecc.owner) and is reachable on 019-217 1108 and 010-788 7987. Reported capacity is 20 beds. The home's published service categories include retirement care, paralysis and post-stroke management, convalescence, post-operative recovery, and day-care for senior citizens — a wider care-type range than many small facilities in the area claim.

Room configurations span single rooms with shared toilet, twin rooms with shared or attached toilet, triple rooms, and dormitory beds, with monthly rates cited in the RM 1,001–RM 4,000 band — a wide range suggesting meaningful price differentiation by room type and dependency level. The Old Klang Road / Taman Sri Sentosa corridor is well-served by the Kesas highway and sits close to Columbia Asia Bukit Rimau and several private specialist clinics, which is a practical logistical advantage for families arranging medical appointments.

One notable operational flag: at least one care directory has noted that CECC does not use a computerised system for records management, which families should factor into their assessment of how medication tracking and care notes are maintained. With only 20 beds, availability is likely limited, and the home has no publicly visible ratings or reviews, making independent verification of care quality difficult. A direct visit — ideally on a weekday — to observe the physical environment, staffing presence, and resident engagement is the most reliable way to evaluate this facility.

title: My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd
longitude: My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd is a care home located in Selangor. The home accommodates up to 20 residents.
editorial_summary:
My Aged Care Sdn Bhd is a licensed Malaysian elder-care operator running a cluster of small nursing homes across Petaling Jaya, with branches at Jalan Taman 7/6, Jalan Assunta 4/52, PJ Old Town's Jalan Othman and Jalan Terus, plus the Jalan University site, an in-house physiotherapy unit (MAC Physio Corner) and a separate Ipoh location. The company has operated since 2006 and was founded by medical professionals with a stated combined six decades of healthcare experience.

The service mix includes residential nursing, respite and day care, post-stroke and post-operative rehabilitation, palliative care, dementia care, wound management and home nursing dispatched from the same teams. A doctor is on call around the clock, six meals per day are provided, and the operator states that meals comply with JKM dietary requirements. Each branch is small (around 20 beds at the listed Selangor site), giving a household-style setup rather than ward-based institutional care.

Families attracted to a doctor-led, multi-site operator with rehab capacity in-house will find this a reasonable fit, particularly for elders moving from a hospital admission into supported recovery. Because branches differ in size and amenities, families should view the specific branch they would use, ask which residents are sent to which site, and clarify nurse-to-resident ratios overnight, dementia behaviour management, palliative protocols, and what is included versus billed extra.

title: Summer Breeze Cottage Caring Home
editorial_summary:
Summer Breeze Cottage Caring Home occupies a renovated double-storey property at 11 Jalan 5/39 in Section 5, Bukit Gasing, Petaling Jaya — a leafy, low-density residential pocket on the edge of the Bukit Gasing forest reserve. The home is built on a 12,000 sq ft plot, giving it unusually generous outdoor space for a PJ nursing home, with a sizable backyard, an activity and dining patio, and two large indoor dining areas. The website positions it as upscale assisted living for mobile and semi-mobile seniors, with a Google Maps rating of 4.4 from seven reviews. Listed capacity is 25 beds.

The facility is staffed 24 hours with trained in-house caregivers and offers a programme that the website describes as physically and mentally stimulating. Listed services include physical therapy, assisted feeding, outdoor activities, and companionship programming. Short-term care and day-care arrangements are explicitly offered in addition to long-term residence, making it a reasonable respite option. Safety-equipped bathrooms with rails are noted, and both private and shared room configurations are available. Operating hours for visits are listed as 9:30 am to 7:00 pm daily, with the centre stating it is wheelchair accessible.

Pricing is not publicly disclosed, and there is no JKM licence number visible in available sources — families should ask directly about the licence status and request it in writing. With only 25 beds and a boutique positioning, availability may be tight; call ahead and book a tour early. The Bukit Gasing location is quiet but not immediately adjacent to a major hospital — the nearest specialists are in Damansara or PJ Seksyen 16, so families should factor travel time for medical appointments into their assessment.

title: Golden Cares Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Golden Cares Nursing Home occupies a shophouse at No. 25, Jalan Changgai 6/22, Seksyen 6, Petaling Jaya — a mature residential neighbourhood with good access to Pantai Indah and hospital facilities along the Klang Valley corridor. The facility was established in 1999, making it one of the older private nursing homes in the PJ area. It holds a 4.3-star rating from 15 Google reviews and is listed with a 25-bed capacity, positioning it as a small, community-scale home. Its Facebook page (goldencaresnursing) and a Wix website are publicly accessible.

The website indicates the home provides round-the-clock nursing care for both short-term and long-term residents, with an on-call doctor for emergency cover. Care is listed as covering stroke patients, bed-ridden residents, NG tube feeding, Alzheimer's and dementia, and Parkinson's disease — a reasonable clinical breadth for a 25-bed facility. Complimentary physical therapy is included, with customisable add-ons available. Shared and private room options are noted, along with a recreational garden and common living areas for socialisation. Contact is available via phone (013-393 9858 / 019-382 6525) and email.

With only 25 beds, availability may be a constraint — families are advised to enquire early. Pricing has not been published, so ask for a full fee breakdown including supplements for higher dependency care, incontinence supplies, and physiotherapy packages. The on-call doctor arrangement should be clarified — ask how quickly a doctor can attend in an emergency versus this being a telemedicine-only service. Given the facility's age, it is also worth asking when the premises were last renovated and what the infection control protocols are.

title: Star Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Star Nursing Home sits in Taman Sri Sentosa, an address that appears in multiple sources placing it along Lorong 18/16a in the Sri Sentosa area of Petaling Jaya or Klang — the exact locality boundary is ambiguous from available map data. The Google listing shows 23 beds and a rating of 4.3 from four reviews, making it one of the few facilities in this batch with a disclosed bed count. Phone listed is 03-7781 4660.

No website or Facebook page was located. The care type field in the source data is blank, so it is not clear from any public source whether Star Nursing Home provides full nursing care (with qualified nurses on shift), basic assisted living, or a simpler residential arrangement. A 23-bed count in a single Taman Sri Sentosa shophouse or terrace setting is a reasonable size for a mid-range independent care home, but without further detail on staffing ratios or medical support, families cannot gauge whether it suits high-dependency residents.

Call 03-7781 4660 to confirm the facility is active and taking enquiries. Key questions: what care level can they accommodate (ambulant versus bedridden), is there a licensed nurse on duty, do they have any experience with dementia or post-stroke care, and what is the monthly fee range for the bed types available.

title: Cherish Retirement And Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
Cherish Retirement and Nursing Home is located in PJS 3, the Jalan Klang Lama-adjacent stretch of Petaling Jaya that falls under the broader Taman Desasiswa corridor. The facility has a Google Maps rating of 4.3 from seven reviews and a listed capacity of 10 beds, indicating a very small, intimate home rather than a conventional multi-ward nursing facility. The phone number 03-7773 2333 is active in directory listings, and a Facebook page exists for the facility. The small bed count and PJS 3 address suggest a converted residential property operating as a boutique retirement home.

Publicly available information about Cherish Retirement's care scope, staffing, and programmes is limited. No dedicated website was found beyond the Facebook presence, and care-type details are not confirmed in independent listings. For a 10-bed facility in a residential neighbourhood, the model is likely one of personalised attention with a small caregiver team; what clinical capabilities exist on-site — particularly for nursing-dependent or post-hospitalisation residents — is unknown from public sources. The 4.3 Google rating is positive on this small sample but not based on a high review volume.

Families exploring Cherish should visit in person and ask directly: whether a JKM licence is held and current; what the nurse or caregiver-to-resident ratio is; how medical emergencies are managed; and whether there are visiting hours or an open-door policy. The PJS 3 location is practical for families in Petaling Jaya, Bangsar, and Old Klang Road, with several private hospitals within a 10–15 minute drive. Given the 10-bed size, availability is likely limited and advance enquiry is essential.

title: MultiCare Nursing Home
longitude: MultiCare Nursing Home is a care home located in Selangor. The home accommodates up to 30 residents.
editorial_summary:
MultiCare Nursing Home is a privately run care facility in Petaling Jaya, operating from 5, Jalan 5/37, Seksyen 1, 46000 Petaling Jaya — a residential-commercial address in the older established part of PJ. The home presents itself as a warm, home-like environment rather than a clinical institution, emphasising flexibility, freedom, and privacy for residents alongside professional nursing care. It is listed with 30 beds. The facility has an active website at multicarehomes.com and a Facebook presence, giving families a reasonable starting point for due diligence.

MultiCare lists a broad service portfolio covering assisted living, elderly care, nursing care, stroke rehabilitation, post-operative care, palliative care, dementia care, and respite care — a range that positions it as a full-dependency home rather than one limited to ambulant or low-needs residents. Care is delivered around the clock by professionally trained registered nurses, caregivers, and a doctor on call for emergencies. Family reviewers have noted that residents appear noticeably happier than they were living alone, which speaks to the social programming and staff engagement. The home's Seksyen 1 location in PJ gives good road access to several private hospitals along Jalan Universiti and the Damansara–Puchong corridor.

Pricing is not listed publicly, so an enquiry call to 03-79321621 is the practical first step. Families should ask about current room availability and pricing tiers, the specific dementia and palliative care protocols, and what medical handover procedures are in place when a resident needs hospitalisation. Confirming the JKM licence number and the nursing staff-to-resident ratio across day and night shifts will help complete the picture before arranging a visit.

title: Pusat Jagaan Jivi
editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Jivi is a small residential care centre in Section 11, Petaling Jaya, reachable on +603-7931 9497, with additional contact persons listed as Ms Lucy and Mdm Avayee — Tamil-sounding names that suggest the facility likely serves, or was founded to serve, the Indian community in the Petaling Jaya area. Section 11 is a mature mixed-residential neighbourhood with easy access to public transport, and the centre operates at a reported capacity of 15 beds. The home keeps a low online profile, with a minimal Facebook presence and a single Google rating on record.

The care type is not publicly published, so the level of nursing care versus basic custodial support is best confirmed directly with the home. The 15-bed size is consistent with a small family-run or community-run home. The Tamil connection, if accurate, may mean Jivi serves seniors who are more comfortable in Tamil or other South Indian languages, which can be a significant advantage for Indian families in PJ who want a culturally familiar placement.

Families considering Jivi should treat this as an early-stage enquiry: confirm JKM registration status, ask what medical oversight is in place (whether a doctor or nurse visits regularly), and clarify what care dependency levels the home can manage. A short physical visit is the most reliable way to assess whether it suits a particular resident's needs, especially for smaller community homes where the right cultural and linguistic fit matters.

title: White Dove Retirement Nursing Home
editorial_summary:
White Dove Retirement Nursing Home is a small private residential facility in Petaling Jaya, set on Lorong 4/49E in Section 4 near the Kwan Inn Teng temple. The home accepts direct admissions from hospitals and from family residences, and operates as a privately run nursing home with 24-hour staff coverage. It is not part of a chain, and the website lists a single location reachable on 03-7499 4943.

The home offers long-term residential care, short-term respite, and day-care arrangements, and lists basic physiotherapy, exercise sessions, social activities, counselling, home-cooked meals, and laundry as in-house services. Short stays for post-illness recovery are explicitly accommodated, which suits families bridging the gap between hospital discharge and home. The published material does not detail nurse-to-resident ratios, dementia-specific programming, or named clinical leads, so families with higher-acuity needs should ask directly.

The facility's modest scale and post-discharge intake pathway make it most useful for relatively stable elders who need supervised assistance with activities of daily living, rather than those requiring complex medical equipment or specialist dementia units. Before admission, ask about doctor visit frequency, what happens during medical emergencies, whether feeding-tube or wound-care residents are accepted, room sharing arrangements, and pricing inclusions for diapers, physio, and outings.

title: Jasper Lodge Nursing Home PJ1
visiting_hours: 10am–8pm daily
editorial_summary:
Jasper Lodge PJ1 is the SS2 Petaling Jaya branch of Jasper Lodge Nursing Home, a Malaysian chain operated by Jasper Healthcare Sdn Bhd (registration 930504W) that runs eleven sites across the Klang Valley, Penang and Kuantan. The chain markets itself as doctor-managed, with a physician overseeing care protocols across all branches alongside nurses and on-call physiotherapists — a structure aimed at families looking for skilled nursing rather than light residential care. PJ1 sits at 37 Jalan SS 2/3, in a residential pocket of Petaling Jaya surrounded by sister branches in the same SS1–SS2 cluster.

The operator's website lists the same service set across its branches: 24-hour doctor's monitoring with regular visits, 24-hour nursing care, daily exercises and interactive activities, basic physiotherapy, healthy meals (three main meals plus two snacks daily), and senior-friendly accommodation. The chain accepts both skilled-nursing residents — including bedbound, post-surgical and chronic-condition cases — and assisted-living residents. Capacity at this branch is listed as roughly 20 beds across single, twin and shared room configurations. Pricing is not published on the operator's website; request a written breakdown covering room type, care plan and any consumable charges when calling.

PJ1 is the original numbered branch in the cluster and has the most visible online review trail of the four PJ sites, with a 4.7 rating across 15 Google reviews at the time of writing. The compact residential-house format suits families wanting a smaller setting close to PJ medical clinics rather than a large institutional building. All Jasper Lodge branches share a single national careline, so specify the PJ1 branch when calling and ask for current bed availability there rather than at the network level. Visiting hours are 10am–8pm daily; useful questions on a first call include the night-shift nurse-to-resident ratio, how often the supervising doctor visits PJ1 specifically, and whether physiotherapy is delivered in-house or shared with nearby sister branches.

title: Jasper Lodge Nursing Home PJ2
visiting_hours: 10am–8pm daily
editorial_summary:
Jasper Lodge PJ2 is one of four Petaling Jaya branches in Jasper Lodge Nursing Home, a Malaysian chain operated by Jasper Healthcare Sdn Bhd (registration 930504W) that runs eleven sites across the Klang Valley, Penang and Kuantan. The chain markets itself as doctor-managed, with a physician overseeing care protocols across all branches alongside nurses and on-call physiotherapists — a structure aimed at families looking for skilled nursing rather than light residential care. PJ2 occupies a house at 15 Jalan SS 1/41, Kampung Tunku — the operator describes it on its branch page as a vibrant building with spacious communal areas used for resident events and group activities.

The operator's website lists the same service set across its branches: 24-hour doctor's monitoring with regular visits, 24-hour nursing care, daily exercises and interactive activities, basic physiotherapy, healthy meals (three main meals plus two snacks daily), and senior-friendly accommodation. The chain accepts both skilled-nursing residents — including bedbound, post-surgical and chronic-condition cases — and assisted-living residents. Capacity at this branch is listed as roughly 20 beds across single, twin and shared room configurations. Pricing is not published on the operator's website; request a written breakdown covering room type, care plan and any consumable charges when calling.

PJ2 has its own page on the operator's website, which makes it one of the better-documented branches in the chain — useful when comparing facilities by photo and description. The Kampung Tunku address sits within walking distance of sister branches in the SS1–SS2 cluster, so families should clarify which specific house their resident will live in rather than the chain in general. All Jasper Lodge branches share a single national careline; specify PJ2 when calling and ask for current bed availability at this branch, the night-shift nurse-to-resident ratio, and whether basic physiotherapy is delivered in-house or shared across nearby sites. Visiting hours are 10am–8pm daily.

title: Jasper Lodge Nursing Home PJ3
visiting_hours: 10am–8pm daily
editorial_summary:
Jasper Lodge PJ3 is one of four Petaling Jaya branches in Jasper Lodge Nursing Home, a Malaysian chain operated by Jasper Healthcare Sdn Bhd (registration 930504W) that runs eleven sites across the Klang Valley, Penang and Kuantan. The chain markets itself as doctor-managed, with a physician overseeing care protocols across all branches alongside nurses and on-call physiotherapists — a structure aimed at families looking for skilled nursing rather than light residential care. PJ3 is reported in third-party directory listings to sit at 47 Jalan SS 2/3, in the same SS2 corridor as PJ1, although the operator's own website does not currently host an individual page for this branch.

The operator's website lists the same service set across its branches: 24-hour doctor's monitoring with regular visits, 24-hour nursing care, daily exercises and interactive activities, basic physiotherapy, healthy meals (three main meals plus two snacks daily), and senior-friendly accommodation. The chain accepts both skilled-nursing residents — including bedbound, post-surgical and chronic-condition cases — and assisted-living residents. Capacity at this branch is listed as roughly 20 beds across single, twin and shared room configurations. Pricing is not published on the operator's website; request a written breakdown covering room type, care plan and any consumable charges when calling.

PJ3 is the least-documented of the PJ branches online — the operator's site does not host an individual branch page, so most practical detail is best gathered in a direct call or on a site visit. All Jasper Lodge branches share a single national careline; specify PJ3 when calling and confirm the exact address (the SS 2/3 number circulating online is third-party reported, not operator-confirmed), current bed availability, the night-shift nurse-to-resident ratio, and whether the doctor's weekly round and the physiotherapist are dedicated to PJ3 or rotated across nearby branches. Visiting hours are 10am–8pm daily.

title: Jasper Lodge Nursing Home PJ5
visiting_hours: 10am–8pm daily
editorial_summary:
Jasper Lodge PJ5 is one of four Petaling Jaya branches in Jasper Lodge Nursing Home, a Malaysian chain operated by Jasper Healthcare Sdn Bhd (registration 930504W) that runs eleven sites across the Klang Valley, Penang and Kuantan. The chain markets itself as doctor-managed, with a physician overseeing care protocols across all branches alongside nurses and on-call physiotherapists — a structure aimed at families looking for skilled nursing rather than light residential care. PJ5 occupies a corner bungalow at 33 Jalan SS 1/25, Kampung Tunku — the operator's branch page highlights a wide open garden, which sets PJ5 apart from the chain's compact terrace-house format and adds a more domestic feel to what is a clinically oriented operation.

The operator's website lists the same service set across its branches: 24-hour doctor's monitoring with regular visits, 24-hour nursing care, daily exercises and interactive activities, basic physiotherapy, healthy meals (three main meals plus two snacks daily), and senior-friendly accommodation. The chain accepts both skilled-nursing residents — including bedbound, post-surgical and chronic-condition cases — and assisted-living residents. Capacity at this branch is listed as roughly 20 beds across single, twin and shared room configurations. Pricing is not published on the operator's website; request a written breakdown covering room type, care plan and any consumable charges when calling.

The garden space and corner-bungalow layout are PJ5's distinguishing features and worth a visit in person to assess how the outdoor area is maintained and how freely residents use it — usage varies considerably across homes that advertise outdoor space. All Jasper Lodge branches share a single national careline; specify PJ5 when calling and ask for current bed availability there, the night-shift nurse-to-resident ratio, which doctor covers the PJ5 weekly round, and whether the physiotherapist is resident at the branch or shared across the SS1–SS2 cluster. Visiting hours are 10am–8pm daily.

title: Pusat Jagaan Rumah Kebajikan Warga Emas St Mark
editorial_summary:
St Mark's Cozy Home is a Christian elder-care facility in Sungai Buloh, Section U19, run under the Anglican Diocese of West Malaysia and operating since January 2008. The home is located on Jalan Besar in the Sungai Buloh new village area and identifies its mission explicitly as ministering to the physical and spiritual needs of older adults within a Christian setting. Sunday services are held on site for residents who wish to attend.

The home provides 24-hour residential supervision, nursing assistance, basic physiotherapy, laundry, and structured daily activities, and the building includes a recreation room, a small on-site pharmacy, a sick bay for unwell residents, outdoor space, and CCTV coverage. A separate elder day-care programme runs alongside the residential service. The home accommodates around 65 beds based on the directory listing, making it one of the larger faith-run homes in the Sungai Buloh corridor.

The Christian mission and Anglican governance make St Mark's a natural fit for Christian families or those comfortable with a faith-based environment, and the larger bed count means more communal living than a small bungalow setup. Visitation is by appointment with health screening. Families should ask about RN coverage overnight, whether high-dependency residents are accepted, the ratio of staff to residents, and how religious observance is handled for residents of other faiths.

title: Nurses at Home
longitude: Nurses at Home is a care home located in Selangor. The home accommodates up to 20 residents.

title: Homage Malaysia
editorial_summary:
Homage is Malaysia's largest app-based home care platform, connecting families with trained caregivers, licensed nurses, and certified therapists for care delivered in the client's own home. The service operates in Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Johor, Penang, Perak (Ipoh), and Kedah. Booking is done through the Homage app (iOS and Android), which shows each Care Pro's qualifications and experience. Care can start within five working days of confirmed booking; 24-hour care is available using two Care Pros in rotating 12-hour shifts. The Care Pro pool is described as 3,000+ Malaysians, with a less-than-5% acceptance rate and a 4.8-star professional rating.

Services cover three categories: Home Personal Care (ADLs — bathing, dressing, toileting, transfers, meal preparation, companionship), Home Nursing by licensed nurses (wound care, NGT feeding, catheter, stoma, medication administration, post-surgery procedures), and Home Therapy by certified therapists (physiotherapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy). Specialised programmes are available for dementia, stroke rehabilitation, cancer care, Parkinson's disease, palliative care, and end-of-life support. Hospital partners include Qualitas, SunMed, and CVSKL.

Published floor rates are: daily living care from RM30/hour (certified caregivers), home nursing from RM36/hour (licensed nurses), and home therapy from RM135/hour (therapists). Multi-session packages reduce the per-hour cost — speak with a Care Advisor for package pricing. Homage is best suited to families who want flexible, on-demand care — from a single hour to full 24-hour cover — without committing to a residential facility.

title: Care Concierge Malaysia
pricing_display: From RM235/day (live-in caregiver); from RM200 for 4h visit; RM10,800/mo (stroke/dementia bundle)
editorial_summary:
Care Concierge is a Malaysian senior care specialist offering home-based caregiving, assisted living residences, and senior day care under one brand. The company focuses on specialised care for Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, dementia, cancer, and post-stroke and post-surgery recovery. At-home services are available in Penang, Perak (Ipoh), KL, Selangor, Melaka, Negeri Sembilan, and Johor. Assisted living residences are located in KL, Petaling Jaya, Penang, and Kuching, Sarawak. A senior day care programme and the company's own care-tracking app round out the offering.

Care Concierge publishes its full price list online, which is unusual in the Malaysian home care market. The Care Plus live-in package starts from RM235/day on a 20-day monthly plan (RM300/day on a daily basis). Flexible Flexi Care visits start from RM200 for a 4-hour plan. Comprehensive specialist bundles for stroke and dementia are priced at RM10,800/month — this includes a nurse/care manager, a neurologist consultation, physiotherapy three times weekly, and a full month of professional caregiving. A Doctor House Call service is available at RM300/visit (Medical Officer) or RM500/visit (Specialist). Physiotherapy sessions are RM200 each, occupational therapy RM180, and speech therapy RM300 for 45 minutes.

Care Concierge is one of the few Malaysian operators that bridges home care and residential care under one roof, which is useful for families not yet certain which route to take. The published price schedule makes it straightforward to compare costs against nursing home placement. Reach them at +603 2724 3828 or via WhatsApp at 60189815128.

title: Noble Care Malaysia
editorial_summary:
Noble Care Malaysia is one of the country's larger managed eldercare networks, operating more than 16 nursing homes across West Malaysia and positioning its home nursing arm as a complement to its residential portfolio. For families in Kuala Lumpur who need professional care delivered at home rather than a bed in a facility, the home nursing service sends trained nurses and caregivers to the client's residence — covering everything from post-surgical wound care and medication management to chronic disease monitoring, physiotherapy, and palliative support. The company's main website is mynoblecare.com; the number in our dataset (03-7728 2886) should be verified on first contact as Noble Care's published lines are 016-778 6795 and 016-278 6993.

The home nursing service is clinically broad. Published capabilities include IV therapy, Foley's catheter care, nasogastric tube management, tracheostomy care, colostomy bag changes, bedsore dressing, and occupational and speech therapy — a list that extends meaningfully into complex medical territory beyond basic companionship. Caregivers and nurses are described as certified, and the organisation emphasises personalised care plans adjusted to the client's specific medical condition. Arrangements are available on a full-time, part-time, or hourly basis, which gives families flexibility in building a care package around existing family capacity.

Pricing is not published on the website and is quoted after a free consultation — a standard approach in Malaysia's home care sector where needs vary significantly. Families should ask at consultation about the qualifications and registration status of nurses assigned to complex cases (particularly for tracheostomy or PEG tube management), the process for replacing a carer who is absent or not a good fit, and how medical escalations are handled when the carer is on-site. Noble Care's scale means it has an established operational framework, but families should still request references or Google Maps reviews for the specific service area relevant to their home.

title: Pillar Care
pricing_display: RM4,600 / 20 working days (home care or nursing support)
editorial_summary:
Pillar Care (Pillar Health Sdn Bhd) is a Petaling Jaya–based home care agency delivering in-home personal care and nursing services for elderly Malaysians. Operating from PJ21 Commercial Centre in SS3, the company places trained caregivers and private duty nurses who visit clients at home. Services cover companion care, personal care assistance (ADLs), senior transportation, medication management, and full home nursing procedures including wound dressing, nasogastric tube care, catheter management, and stoma care. Private duty nurses are available around the clock, 24 hours a day, seven days a week, for both scheduled visits and hourly care.

Specialised programmes are offered for dementia, stroke, Parkinson's, diabetes, cancer, multiple sclerosis, palliative care, and end-of-life care. The company states that 8,582 Malaysian families have used their service, and all Care Specialists go through training reviewed by external auditors. Published pricing is RM4,600 for a 20-working-day package — equivalent to approximately RM230/day — applicable to both home care and home nursing support. The company notes that hourly rates from other providers typically run RM30–RM50/hour by comparison.

Pillar suits Klang Valley families who want a committed package-based arrangement rather than ad-hoc app bookings. The standard billing unit is a 20-day care block. Contact at +6018 2046966 or hello@pillarcare.com; the active website is pillarcare.com (the .com.my variant redirects).

title: WhereWeCare
pricing_display: From RM3,700/28 days (live-in caregiver); RM1,200/week; RM200/day
editorial_summary:
WhereWeCare is a Malaysian home care agency placing certified caregivers and registered nurses for in-home elderly care across major Malaysian cities — Kuala Lumpur, Penang, Johor Bahru, Ipoh, Melaka, and Perak, with nationwide coverage on request. The agency operates on a placement model: it assesses care needs, matches a suitable caregiver, and manages the ongoing arrangement. Key features include no long-term contracts, free carer replacement if needed, and transparent published pricing with no hidden costs. The company cites 500+ satisfied clients and over 10 years of experience in elderly care.

Services focus on live-in caregiving arrangements. All plans cover nutritious meal preparation, mobility support, physiotherapy exercises, bedsore prevention, medical appointment accompaniment, and companionship. Specialised programmes are available for dementia, palliative care, post-surgery recovery, and customised wellness plans including nutrition guidance and mental health support. Registered nurses are also available for nursing-level procedures at home.

Published pricing: the 28-day live-in package starts from RM3,700 per four weeks (approximately RM132/day), the 7-day package from RM1,200/week, and daily bookings from RM200/day. The monthly package price is competitive against shared-room nursing home rates in the Klang Valley, making it a genuine option for seniors who can manage at home with round-the-clock support. Enquire via WhatsApp at +60 11-6196 3941 or visit wherewecare.com.

title: Komune Care Senior Day Club
editorial_summary:
Komune Care Senior Day Club operates within Komune Living & Wellness, an integrated multi-generational development built by UOA Group in Bandar Tun Razak, Cheras — one of the most distinctive senior living concepts in Malaysia. The address is Unit M-03A, 21 Jalan Tasik Permaisuri 2, 56000 Cheras, Kuala Lumpur. Komune Care itself is a joint venture between UOA and Care Concierge, an established home care operator, which means the day club and assisted living suites are backed by a specialist clinical partner rather than managed purely as a property amenity. The day club occupies 6,600 sq ft and is marketed as the largest senior daycare facility in Malaysia.

The service offer across the Komune Care platform is substantial. For day club members, structured programming includes occupational therapist-designed activities, music therapy, daily morning and evening exercises, weekly excursions, and shuttle transfers to a nearby mall. The adjacent Komune Living building houses medical infrastructure through UMH Healthcare — covering general practice, dental, ambulatory health screening, and medical aesthetics — as well as Traditional Chinese Medicine consultation and treatment through Tong Xin Tang, giving day club members access to a layered wellness ecosystem. For residents choosing assisted living suites, 24-hour nursing and caregiver support is available, and the facility has stated capacity for residents managing dementia, stroke recovery, post-hospitalisation care, cancer care, and complex comorbidities. Published pricing: assisted living suites from RM 6,800 per month; day club membership from RM 3,000–3,500 per month.

Komune Care is best suited to seniors who will benefit from a structured, socially active day programme or who need assisted living with a lifestyle component rather than a purely clinical environment. Families should ask on enquiry about clinical escalation protocols — specifically how a medical emergency during a day club session is managed, whether in-house nursing is present during day club hours, and how waitlists for assisted living suites are structured. The RM 500,000 Senior Care Fund established by UOA to subsidise eligible lower-income families is also worth raising if cost is a consideration.

title: Seniora Elderly Day Care Johor Bahru
editorial_summary: Private elderly day care centre with branches in Johor Bahru and Penang. Structured daily programmes including exercise, cognitive activities, meals, and health monitoring. Trial day available for RM80. Monthly packages up to RM3,580. Suitable for mobile seniors with mild to moderate care needs including early-stage dementia. Seniors return home each evening.

title: Merry Care Kepong Baru 3
editorial_summary:
Merry Care Kepong Baru 3 is one of six branches in the Merry Care chain, located at No.1, Jalan 9, Taman Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. Merry Care is a six-branch nursing home chain operating across Kepong Baru, Desa Jaya, and Selayang Baru in the Klang Valley. The group markets itself at elderlycare.my and positions on the affordable end of the residential care market — a deliberate point of difference against private retirement homes that typically start north of RM 4,000 per month. With multiple homes clustered within walking distance of each other in Kepong Baru, families touring the chain often visit two or three branches in a single afternoon.

The operator publishes the same service bundle across all branches: medication management, daily items purchasing, hospital visitation, daily fitness activities, a 24-hour assistant on duty, and five meals a day with unlimited servings on request. Pricing is published on the operator website (elderlycare.my) starting from RM 2,500 per month for a shared room — a starting figure that, in practice, varies with the resident's actual care needs and dependency level. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Kepong Baru is a dense residential pocket in north-Kuala Lumpur with good access to Hospital Sungai Buloh and the Damansara hospital cluster (KPJ Damansara, Sunway, ParkCity Medical Centre). The neighbourhood is tightly built — terrace-house lots, narrow back lanes — so families should ask on a visit how natural light, communal space, and any outdoor compound are handled, how many residents share a room, how many caregivers cover each shift, whether a registered nurse is on-site overnight or on-call, and what specific clinical procedures the home performs in-house versus refers out (PEG feeds, oxygen, wound dressings, urinary catheter changes). Confirm the RM 2,500 starting rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure.

title: Merry Care Kepong Baru 4

title: Merry Care Selayang Baru
editorial_summary:
Merry Care Selayang Baru is one of six branches in the Merry Care chain, located at 12, Jalan 1D, Selayang Baru, 68100 Batu Caves, Selangor. Merry Care is a six-branch nursing home chain operating across Kepong Baru, Desa Jaya, and Selayang Baru in the Klang Valley. The group markets itself at elderlycare.my and positions on the affordable end of the residential care market — a deliberate point of difference against private retirement homes that typically start north of RM 4,000 per month. Pricing is published on the operator website (elderlycare.my) starting from RM 2,500 per month for a shared room — a starting figure that, in practice, varies with the resident's actual care needs and dependency level. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Selayang Baru sits north of Kepong on the KL–Selangor border, with Hospital Selayang (a major MOH tertiary hospital) within a few kilometres — a practical advantage for residents who may need frequent specialist follow-up or acute escalation. The neighbourhood is lower-density than Kepong Baru, with more compound space typical of Selangor terrace lots. Useful viewing questions: how many residents are currently in the home, how many caregivers are on each shift, whether RN cover is on-site overnight, and what acute-escalation pathway the home typically uses. Confirm the RM 2,500 starting rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure.

title: Merry Care Desa Jaya Kepong
editorial_summary:
Merry Care Desa Jaya Kepong is one of six branches in the Merry Care chain, located at No.27, Jalan 5, Taman Desa Jaya, Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. Merry Care is a six-branch nursing home chain operating across Kepong Baru, Desa Jaya, and Selayang Baru in the Klang Valley. The group markets itself at elderlycare.my and positions on the affordable end of the residential care market — a deliberate point of difference against private retirement homes that typically start north of RM 4,000 per month. Bed count and current room availability are not published online.

Taman Desa Jaya is on the Kepong–Desa Parkcity fringe, slightly quieter than the Kepong Baru cluster and closer to the Damansara hospital corridor (KPJ Damansara, Sunway, ParkCity Medical Centre). Useful viewing questions: how many residents share a room, how many caregivers cover each shift, whether RN cover is on-site overnight or on-call, what specific clinical procedures are handled in-house versus referred out, and whether the property has a usable outdoor compound. Confirm the RM 2,500 starting rate matches the actual care plan since add-ons can shift the monthly figure.

title: Sunway Sanctuary
editorial_summary:
Sunway Sanctuary is the senior-living arm of Sunway Group's integrated township in Bandar Sunway, Selangor - a purpose-built community offering hotel-grade apartments and suites for older adults who want to remain independent while having medical backup nearby. The community is annexed to Sunway Medical Centre, which is the practical reason most families consider it: residents have priority access to one of the country's largest private hospitals without leaving the compound. Suites are configured for ageing-in-place - wide doorways, level thresholds, grab rails as standard - and the campus shares amenities with the wider Sunway township, putting the pyramid mall, monorail, and parks within a covered walkway. 

Daily life leans active rather than clinical. Residents have a calendar of group exercise, art and music programmes, and outings; meals are served in a communal dining room with dietary options. The community is licensed and openly published as part of Sunway's healthcare arm, which is unusual transparency for the segment and helpful for families used to opaque smaller operators. 

Pricing sits at the premium end of the Klang Valley AL market - expect five-figure monthly fees with the model evolving (some suites have been offered as memberships rather than month-to-month rentals), so the financial model is the question to ask first on a tour. Other call-time questions: what happens if a resident's care needs escalate beyond AL (does the community have a nursing wing, or is the transfer path to Sunway Medical and then out), what is and isn't included in the fee (utilities, meal tiers, additional carer hours), and minimum tenancy.

title: ReU Living
editorial_summary:
ReU Living is a hybrid senior-living and post-hospitalisation residence on the edge of KLCC, positioned by its operators as a 'hotel-in-a-hospital' concept. It serves two adjacent populations under one roof: older adults who want serviced apartment living with concierge medical support, and patients in the post-acute window who need structured recovery — typically post-stroke, post-surgical, or after a chemotherapy cycle — without staying on a hospital ward. The dual-track model is deliberate, and worth understanding before booking, because it shapes everything from the staffing pattern to the daily rhythm. 

Suites are hotel-style with private bathrooms and city views; residents have access to in-house Traditional Chinese Medicine, physiotherapy, and a doctor on call. Meals follow tiered dietary plans (Western, Asian, therapeutic) and the concierge layer covers the small things — laundry, transport to appointments, guest hosting — that families end up doing themselves at less integrated communities. The KLCC location is genuinely useful: most major specialists and private hospitals are within ten minutes, which matters more for the post-hospitalisation track than the lifestyle one. 

Pricing is premium, billed monthly, with shorter post-acute stays priced differently from long-stay residency — confirm both rates and what's bundled. Useful call-time questions: how is care escalation handled (is there an in-house nursing capability for higher acuity or is transfer to a partnered hospital the expected path), what's the actual mix of long-stay vs. short-stay residents on a typical floor, and how are meal/care additions billed.
